Title: Job | Data Analyst Risk, Finance & ESG | Bruxelles
Description:
Description

Vous aimez transformer des défis complexes en solutions techniques innovantes ? Vous souhaitez contribuer à des projets qui ont un impact sur la stratégie et les procédés Risque, Finance et ESG (Environnement, Social, Gouvernance) de Belfius ? Alors, nous avons besoin de vous !

Rejoignez nos équipes du Data Office, au cœur du département Risk. Ensemble, nous développons des solutions qui permettent de mesurer, analyser et améliorer les processus de données au service des activités Risque, Finance et ESG.

Votre job en deux mots

Vous serez responsable de la conception, du développement et de la maintenance de pipelines de données.

À ce titre, vous développerez et implémenterez des méthodologies ainsi que des solutions techniques pour soutenir les projets de la banque. Vous réaliserez des opérations ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) afin d’intégrer et de transformer les données, tout en travaillant dans un environnement de développement moderne, principalement basé sur Python et les services cloud Azure.

Enfin, vous comprendrez les besoins du business et les traduirez en solutions et analyses innovantes et automatisées, contribuant ainsi à renforcer l’impact durable de Belfius.

Pas besoin d’être un oiseau rare

Mais vous pouvez en devenir un, parmi nous.

Nous recherchons un(e) Data analyste technique et motivé(e), prêt(e) à se développer dans un environnement dynamique et innovant. Vous serez au cœur de l’exploitation de données, contribuant directement à l'impact positif de la banque.

Vos responsabilités principales:


Concevoir, développer et maintenir des pipelines de données dans Azure.
Programmer et implémenter des solutions techniques en Python (et PySpark) et en SQL.
Travailler avec des sources de données variées et les transformer en informations exploitables.
Contribuer à l'amélioration continue de notre écosystème data : qualité, gouvernance, documentation, bonnes pratiques.



Vous aurez l'opportunité de travailler sur des projets innovants et impactants, tout en développant vos compétences techniques et en durabilité.

Vous ferez partie d'une équipe dynamique et collaborative, où votre contribution sera valorisée et reconnue.

Chez nous, vous pouvez vous distinguer et révéler le meilleur de vous-même si vous répondez aux critères suivants:


Diplôme en STIM (Sciences, Technologies, Ingénierie, Mathématique) ou en sciences économiques/commerciales, ou expérience équivalente.
Expérience en développement et en manipulation de base de données.
Capacité à travailler en équipe et à communiquer efficacement avec les parties prenantes.
Esprit analytique et capacité à résoudre des problèmes techniques complexes.
Capacité à écouter les besoins et à y répondre avec des solutions data pragmatiques et efficaces.



Atouts supplémentaires


Bilingue (néerlandais/français) avec une bonne connaissance de l'anglais.
Curiosité naturelle et goût pour l'apprentissage continu.
Connaissance des services cloud Azure (Azure Synapse, DevOps), ainsi qu’une experience en développement (de préférence Python) est un plus.



Belfius prend soin de ses talents

Outre la possibilité de faire votre chemin dans un job qui vous plaît, nous vous offrons un contrat à temps plein à durée indéterminée avec :


Un salaire attractif complété par une offre innovante vous octroyant un budget à utiliser selon vos préférences personnelles (p. ex. voiture, vélo, PC, smartphone, tablette, loyer ou crédit hypothécaire sous certaines conditions) et des avantages extralégaux, tels que des chèques-repas, des avantages sur les produits et services internes, une assurance hospitalisation et une assurance groupe.
Des possibilités de développement par le biais d'un large éventail de formations et des opportunités de carrière dans l’ensemble du groupe Belfius.
Un bon équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée grâce à des horaires flexibles, à la combinaison du travail au bureau et à domicile et un nombre attractif de jours de congé.
Un lieu de travail moderne au cœur de Bruxelles, avec une salle de sport branchée, un délicieux restaurant sain et des espaces de réunion confortables, le tout à quelques pas de la rue commerciale, de la gare Bruxelles-Nord et de la station métro Rogier.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WHAT IS CREAM ALL ABOUT?

We could be a Cream Factory, a cooking blog or even a detective agency…

But in reality, we are a Business & Technology consulting company, active in large organizations and industry leaders in Banking, Insurance, Telecom, Energy, & Transport.

The Cream recipe

• 1/3 Personal development • 1/3 Collective intelligence • 1/3 Intrapreneurship • Bonus ingredient: some passionate colleagues




YOUR FUTURE PROJECTS :

Like Benjamin, Data Analyst at Cream, you work close to business teams and help them turn data into concrete insights that support decision-making. Your tasks will be :

• Understand business needs and translate them into analytical questions

• Collect, clean and analyse large datasets

• Identify trends, patterns and performance drivers

• Build dashboards, reports and analytical outputs

• Support business users in data interpretation

• Contribute to the selection and use of analytics tools

• Work closely with business, IT and data teams




WHAT DO WE EXPECT FROM OUR CONSULTANT ?

• At least 2 years of experience in data analysis AND Master’s degree (or equivalent in experience)

• Experience with SQL and BI tools (Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, …)

• Knowledge of Python or SAS

• Fluent in French and English, a good level of Dutch is an asset

• Ability to explain data insights in a clear and business-oriented way

• Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset




WHY SHOULD YOU TASTE THE LIME FLAVOR ?

A strong learning environment: tribes, training budget
Career follow-up with dedicated Business Managers and an associate plan
Attractive benefits package (mobility plan, meal vouchers, insurances,...)
Hybrid working model: client working, home working & Cream working
Strong CSR commitments, with recognized labels in environmental responsibility and diversity & inclusion.
A consulting company of around 200 Creamers, located in the center of Brussels




Our recruitment process is clear and structured, with three steps and feedback throughout.

Cream Consulting is committed to equal opportunities. If you need any specific accommodation to fully participate in the recruitment process or the interview, please let us know.

Think this role is a match? Apply and let’s talk, or explore our other opportunities at Cream.

Title: Data Analyst | Dynafin Consulting
Description:
DynaFin is a consulting company with 15 years of experience in delivering high quality services to one single sector: Financial Services. We have built a team of > 150 financial services experts in Belgium, Luxembourg and France, passionate to support the sustainable transformation of the financial industry.

DynaFin advises this key sector of the global economy to boost its operating performance and innovate for the future.

We provide unique and pragmatic solutions to address market challenges, bundled in 5 DynaFin core expertises:


Business Model Innovation
Enterprise Operating Performance
Regulatory Compliance
Risk Management
Technology



We are looking for a Data Analyst with strong expertise in data warehousing and modelling to support data-driven initiatives within a banking environment. The analyst will work closely with business stakeholders, architects, and delivery teams to translate complex business needs into reliable and scalable data solutions. The listing

Your main responsibilities will include:


Analyse and challenge business requirements and translate them into structured functional and technical specifications.
Collaborate with Solution Architects to ensure alignment with existing data architecture.
Model and document data structures using 3NF and dimensional modelling (Kimball methodology).
Support the design, validation, and optimisation of ETL processes,
Conduct impact analysis and formulate clear solution proposals.
Maintain consistent and structured documentation of requirements, models, and solutions.
Support testing and validation activities (e.g. defect analysis, UAT follow-up).
Contribute to knowledge sharing, peer reviews, and team improvement efforts.
Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (e.g. sprint planning, retrospectives).
Identify data-related risks and ensure compliance with internal governance and security standards.



Hier gaan we vanuit


Master’s degree (e.g. Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).
Minimum 3 years of experience as a data analyst, business analyst, or functional analyst in a complex environment.
Deep knowledge of data modelling techniques: 3NF, Kimball/star schema (facts/dimensions).
Strong SQL skills and solid experience with ETL tools.
Experience with Oracle and Oracle Data Integrator (ODI) is a strong asset.
Knowledge of Data Vault modelling is a plus.
Able to work independently and provide structured input to project teams.
Precise, analytical, and solution-focused approach.



Arbeidsvoorwaarden

About Dynafin Consulting:


Founded in 1970
Currently working in 1 office.

DynaFin advises this key sector of the global economy to boost its operating performance and innovate for the future.

We provide unique and pragmatic solutions to address market challenges, bundled in 5 DynaFin core expertises:

  • Business Model Innovation
  • Enterprise Operating Performance
  • Regulatory Compliance
  • Risk Management
  • Technology


We are looking for a Data Analyst with strong expertise in data warehousing and modelling to support data-driven initiatives within a banking environment. The analyst will work closely with business stakeholders, architects, and delivery teams to translate complex business needs into reliable and scalable data solutions. The listing

Your main responsibilities will include:

  • Analyse and challenge business requirements and translate them into structured functional and technical specifications.
  • Collaborate with Solution Architects to ensure alignment with existing data architecture.
  • Model and document data structures using 3NF and dimensional modelling (Kimball methodology).
  • Support the design, validation, and optimisation of ETL processes,
  • Conduct impact analysis and formulate clear solution proposals.
  • Maintain consistent and structured documentation of requirements, models, and solutions.
  • Support testing and validation activities (e.g. defect analysis, UAT follow-up).
  • Contribute to knowledge sharing, peer reviews, and team improvement efforts.
  • Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (e.g. sprint planning, retrospectives).
  • Identify data-related risks and ensure compliance with internal governance and security standards.


Hier gaan we vanuit

  • Master’s degree (e.g. Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).
  • Minimum 3 years of experience as a data analyst, business analyst, or functional analyst in a complex environment.
  • Deep knowledge of data modelling techniques: 3NF, Kimball/star schema (facts/dimensions).
  • Strong SQL skills and solid experience with ETL tools.
  • Experience with Oracle and Oracle Data Integrator (ODI) is a strong asset.
  • Knowledge of Data Vault modelling is a plus.
  • Able to work independently and provide structured input to project teams.
  • Precise, analytical, and solution-focused approach.


Arbeidsvoorwaarden

About Dynafin Consulting:

  • Founded in 1970
  • Currently working in 1 office.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
WHO IS EDENRED?




Edenred is the global leader in Benefits & Engagement and Mobility. With more than 1 million client companies across 44 countries, Edenred's platform gives more than 60 million users access to the services and products of more than 2 million partner merchants. Edenred offers digital solutions dedicated to employees (meal vouchers, commuting, gift cards, wellness, rewards, and preferential offers), fleet managers (multi-energy solutions including EV charging, maintenance services, tolls, and parking), and corporate payments (virtual cards).




'Enrich connections. For good.'

Guided by the Group's purpose, these solutions enhance employees' well-being and purchasing power and simplify the lives of professional drivers. They promote access to healthier food, more environmentally friendly products, and more sustainable mobility. Finally, they improve the attractiveness and efficiency of businesses while vitalizing the employment market and local economies. Edenred's 12,000 employees are committed to making the world of work a connected ecosystem that is safer, more efficient and more responsible every day. In 2025, thanks to its unique technology platform, the Group generated a business volume of €49 billion, mainly through mobile applications, online platforms, and cards. Edenred is listed on the Euronext Paris stock exchange and included in the following indices: CAC Next 20, CAC Large 60, Euronext 100, Euronext Tech Leaders, FTSE4Good, DJSI Europe Index and DJSI World Index.







YOU WILL VIBE WITH US




As part of Edenred BeLux, you will be working as Data Analyst in our Tech Data Team.

The main scope of Data Team is to create data products on our Global Edenred Azure Data Platform, build data sets, provide strategic dashboard & reports in Power BI and enable self-service reporting across the organization/

As a Data Analyst, you are a professional specialized in the design, implementation, and management of the systems, architecture, and tools necessary for the collection, storage, transformation, and distribution of data. The primary role of a Data Analyst is to develop and maintain the infrastructure that allows business teams to organize and efficiently exploit their data.




The key stakeholders for this position are:




Internal: End-users & key users, Data & Product managers and executives, process owners/team leaders and managers, Agile Teams (Scrum Masters and Product Owners).
External: HQ’s corporate IT, Remote Development centers, customers and partners.
Reporting Line : you report directly to the IT Back-end & Data Product Owner and your N+2 will be the CTO Edenred BeLux.




As a Data Analyst, you will be in charge of Collecting & organizing the data on an efficient, compliant & secured way (pricing analytics, client/merchant segmentation, Business Review reporting,…) :




Business analysis & modelling: understanding, documenting, supporting, and analyzing procedures that support the business functions performed in the different departments of the company.
Data management: Takes responsibility for the accessibility, retrievability and security of specific subsets of data
Data visualization: develop reports & dashboards in PowerBI or other Data Visualization tools
Requirements definition & management: defining user stories and acceptance criteria with the key users, Product Owners, Product Managers and Business Sponsors.
Development & implementation: participate actively with the dev. team, lead the validation efforts with the end users and proactively help the team members with their tasks to finish each Sprint.
Application support & Customer service support: As a team, take full responsibility of changes put into production
The quality of the deliverables: testing Business Process & applications.







WE WILL VIBE WITH YOU




You have at least 3 years of experience in business analysis and/or business process management. Ideally, you have experience in financial services industry or gained in a consultancy environment or similar.
You are fluent in French and/or Dutch and English.
You have proficiency in PowerBI (and/or Tableau) including DAX and SQL.
You feel comfortable with statistical data analysis:
Analysis of information from a data dashboard
Knowledge of data exploration statistical methods
Conducting tests to confirm hypotheses
Data quality assessment through statistical analysis
You can convert business requirements into tasks and implement them in the analysis process.
You have at least a basic understanding of data security and legislation (GDPR) and data collection and management (formulation of collection needs based on field-specific issues and data modeling, extraction of relevant data from large volumes).
You demonstrate analytical and synthesis capacities, communication and presentation skills, capacity to lead through challenging situations and customer orientation.




At Edenred, we recognize, recruit, and develop all talents while respecting each individual's uniqueness.

We encourage and consider all applications without distinction of origin, gender, identity or expression of gender, sexual orientation, age, disability status, or any other protected criteria as defined by Unia.







START DATE

ASAP







OTHER INFORMATION

Permanent contract (38 hours/week).
Location: Brussels (Auderghem).
A stimulating position within a dynamic organization that focuses on technological innovation to enhance workers' purchasing power.
The opportunity to work remotely 2 days a week with your manager's approval, including a homeworking allowance of 115€.
You are covered with hospitalization and ambulatory care insurance (with the option to extend it to your family).
You benefit from all Edenred-solutions, such as vouchers but also Edenred Engagement (including Edenred Discounts).
Your package includes a car or a mobility budget, depending on what you prefer.

Title: Data Analyst (Power BI, ML)
HTML:
We currently have a vacancy for a Data Analyst (Power BI, ML) fluent in English, to offer his/her services as an expert remotely (within EU). In the context of the first assignment, the successful candidate will be integrated in the Development team of the company that will closely cooperate with a major client's IT team on site.

Tasks:

    • Evolutive maintenance of Power BI dashboards;
    • Gather requirements from Business Units, implement improvements, new data loads and refreshes for all datasets included;
    • Maintain and upgrade the Data Gateway connections;
    • Evolutive maintenance of the data pipeline classification in Python;
    • Train and deploy machine learning models in a production environment, monitor performance and make improvements as needed;
    • Apply machine learning to improve the quality of data or products and deploy data tools in data analysis;
    • Define, implement and monitor security plans, vulnerability scanning, penetration testing, risk management, security audits, security operations and incident response;
    • Provide guidance and support to users, follow up on incidents and user requests, write user manuals and IS on-line help for Information Systems, prepare and provide training on Information systems.

Requirements

      • University degree in IT or relevant discipline, combined with minimum 4 years of relevant working experience in IT;
      • Experience with programming languages (e.g. Python, R);
      • Experience with data visualization tools (e.g. tableau, Power BI) or software frameworks to process Big Data (e.g. Spark, Databricks, Splunk, Hadoop);
      • Experience with AI platforms, tool kits and techniques (e.g. TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, CNTK, Scikit, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG));
      • Experience with gathering requirements from Business Units;
      • Experience in implementing connections to new data sources, data cleaning, testing;
      • Experience in creation of new AI prompts to improve classification accuracy;
      • Experience in documentation of changes;
      • Excellent command of the English language.

Benefits

If you are seeking a career in an exciting and dynamic company, where you will offer your services as part of a team of a major European Institution, operating in an international, multilingual and multicultural environment where you can expect real chances to make a difference, please send us your detailed CV in English, quoting reference: (20165/07/2026).

We offer a competitive remuneration (either on contract basis or remuneration with full benefits package), based on qualifications and experience. All applications will be treated as confidential. 

You may also consider all our other open vacancies by visiting the career section of our web site (www.eurodyn.com) and follow us on Twitter (@EURODYN_Careers) and LinkedIn.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (www.eurodyn.com) is a leading Software, Information and Communication Technologies company, operating internationally (Athens, Brussels, Luxembourg, Copenhagen, Stockholm, London, Nicosia, Hong-Kong, Valetta, etc). The company employs over 1000 engineers, IT experts and consultants (around 3% PhD, 36% MSc and 53% BSc or equivalent). We design and develop software applications using state-of-the-art technology. The group generates annual revenues in the range of EURO 40 million, with an EBITDA in the range of 20%. The value of our contract portfolio exceeds EURO 300 million. EUROPEAN DYNAMICS is a renowned supplier of IT services to government institutions, multinational corporations, public administrations and multinational companies, research and academic institutes.

As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, we are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, color, national origin, ethnicity, gender, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, or religion

By submitting an application to this position and by sharing your personal data with ED, you acknowledge and accept its Policy and authorize ED to process your personal data for the purposes of the company's recruitment opportunities, in line to the Policy.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Your mission:

As a Data Analyst in the E2E Quality department, you will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting complex data sets to help drive data-driven business decisions. You will work closely with various departments to understand their data needs, provide insights, and develop data strategies that improve customer quality and business performance. Your ability to turn data into actionable information will be key in supporting our organization’s quality and efficiency.




Job location: Brussels or Gosselies or Ans.




Your main responsibilities:

Gather and process both structured and unstructured data from various sources including databases, APIs, and raw data files thats contribute to the company data lake.
Challenge any assumption and perform data exploration, cleaning and preprocessing to ensure data integrity, quality, and relevant usability.
Analyse large data sets to identify trends, patterns, and correlations relevant to business objectives by leveraging on statistical methods and data mining.
Translate complex technical data into simple usable indicators and perform in depth analysis delivering understandable findings that will trigger actionable business recommendations.
Create visualizations and dashboards to represent data findings in a clear and concise manner to stakeholders, including management and other departments.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business challenges and develop data-driven solutions to improve the customer experience, strengthen the technical leadership and optimize resource allocation.
Automate repetitive tasks and processes using scripts and macros or building simple frameworks.




Your profile:

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science
Minimum 3 years of experience in a similar function
Experience with statistical software and database management (e.g. BigQuery, SQL, Python)
Proficiency in BI tools (e.g. PowerBI, Google Looker, Tableau)
Experience with ETL within Cloud environments (e.g. GCP, DataForm, Matillon, Alteryx, AWS Redshit)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail and high level of accuracy
Excellent communication and presentation skills
Ability to work independently and as part of a team
Fluent French & English. Dutch would be a plus




Join Our Dynamic Team!

We invite you to work in a collaborative and stimulating environment, engaging with exciting AI projects. Enjoy the flexibility to work from Brussels, Ans, Gosselies, or remotely from home, in accordance with our internal policies.




You will make the difference in this highly innovative vibe. As a result, you will develop your full potential by working with highly skilled colleagues and through continuous training. Moreover, you can count on a highly competitive and versatile compensation (market competitive salary, company car or legal mobility budget, homeworking and net allowance, performance bonus, meal vouchers) and benefits package.




Inspired? Please submit your CV and we will get back to you to discuss the next steps.

Title: Data Analyst Confirmé (H/F)
Description:
Description Du Poste

Vous serez intégré(e) dans une équipe de consultants innovants et motivés, composée de Business Analysts, Data Scientists et Data Engineers spécialisés sur les problématiques Data Analytics. À ce titre, vous interviendrez de façon opérationnelle sur des missions de conseil autour des sujets de transformation Data Analytics, de retraitement et modélisation de données et de représentation visuelle de la donnée pour adresser des problématiques métiers.

Vous serez notamment en charge de :


Comprendre les problématiques métier,
Collecter et traiter les données en vue de l'analyse, incluant notamment la création de variables agrégées et complémentaires aux données brutes,
Automatiser et d’industrialiser les retraitements effectués sur les données,
Représenter les résultats dans un outil de restitution visuelle de façon ergonomique et intuitive,
Restituer les analyses au client notamment pas la création d’indicateurs pertinents pour les questions stratégiques des entreprises


PROFIL RECHERCHE

Diplômé d'une école d'ingénieurs ou équivalent (Bac +5), vous justifiez d’a minima 2 ans d’expérience réussis en tant que data analyst :


Vous possédez une bonne connaissance des outils et méthodologies de Data Analytics, incluant notamment des bases techniques (modèles de données, langage SQL, …) mais également méthodologiques,
Vous possédez une connaissance approfondie d'Excel et maitrisez les langages statistiques tels que R, Python ou SAS,
Une connaissance d'un logiciel ou langage dédié à la Data Visualization (Tableau, Qliksense ou Qlickview, Microsoft Power BI…) ainsi que des compétences en automatisation (VBA par exemple) seraient un plus.


PROCESSUS DE RECRUTEMENT


Échangez avec un ou une chargée de recrutement pour un premier contact téléphonique
Rencontrez un ou une Ingénieure d’Affaires (Business Manager) sur vos souhaits d’évolution
Approfondissez avec nos responsables et directeurs d’agence pour connaître la vision de l'agence


Selon votre profil, des tests ou entretiens techniques pourront vous être demandés. Tout au long du processus, vous pourrez échanger avec nos consultants pour en savoir + sur leur quotidien chez nous !

PACKAGE SALARIAL

Chez Extia Belgique IT, la transparence fait partie intégrante de l'expérience positive, voici un aperçu des éléments de package salarial dont peuvent profiter nos collaborateurs, sous réserve d'éligibilité

👛 Voiture de société ou budget mobilité ✨ Chèques repas & Ecocheque 🎁 Assurance groupe 🎉 Frais de représentation 📈 Plan cafétéria / Flex Income Plan 🚀 Primes d'apport d'affaire, de cooptation et de formation 🌍 20 jours de congés légaux + 12 jours de récupération

Ces informations sont communiquées à titre indicatif et seront abordées plus en détails lors du processus de recrutement.

LES PETITS PLUS

🎉 250 événements par an pour rencontrer tes collègues et des locaux feel good 📢 Des formations, conférences et une plateforme d'e-learning avec LinkedIn pour monter en compétences #greatplacetolearn 👩‍ Des Communautés Métiers et du Mentorat pour partager son expertise et en apprendre sur de nombreux sujets #greatplacetoshare 🕹️ Une communauté de e-sport : Extia Gaming ! #greatplacetoplay 🌏 Envie d’évoluer ? Grâce à notre politique de mobilité interne, explorez de nouvelles opportunités en Belgique ou dans l’un de nos 7 autres pays !

Title: BI Reporting Analyst
Description:
Ben je gepassioneerd door data, rapportering en analyse? Vind je het boeiend om complexe data om te zetten in heldere inzichten die collega's helpen betere beslissingen te nemen?




Als BI Reporting Analyst speel je een belangrijke rol in het ondersteunen van onze organisatie met betrouwbare rapportering en data-inzichten. Je bouwt een grondige kennis op van onze systemen, data en businessprocessen en vertaalt deze naar rapporten, dashboards en analyses die dagelijks gebruikt worden door sales, boekhouding, management en directie.




Je bent niet alleen technisch sterk, maar begrijpt ook de business achter de cijfers. Dankzij jouw interesse in commerciële processen slaag je erin om data om te zetten in informatie die écht bruikbaar is voor de organisatie.




Jouw uitdaging

Je bouwt een grondige kennis op van onze systemen, databronnen en backofficeprocessen.
Je ontwikkelt, onderhoudt en optimaliseert rapporten, dashboards en KPI-overzichten voor verschillende afdelingen.
Je vertaalt businessvragen van sales, finance, management en directie naar duidelijke rapporteringsoplossingen.
Je bewaakt de kwaliteit, consistentie en betrouwbaarheid van de data waarop rapporteringen gebaseerd zijn.
Je zorgt voor een efficiënte distributie en documentatie van rapporteringen.
Je voert analyses uit die operationele beslissingen ondersteunen.
Je detecteert afwijkingen en opportuniteiten in de data en vertaalt deze naar concrete inzichten.
Je ondersteunt collega's bij de interpretatie van rapporten en cijfers.
Je denkt actief mee over nieuwe KPI's, rapporteringsbehoeften en optimalisaties van bestaande processen.
Je fungeert als brug tussen businessgebruikers en technische teams.




Jouw profiel

Je beschikt over 3 tot 5 jaar relevante ervaring in een gelijkaardige rol.
Je bent gepassioneerd door cijfers, data en analyse.
Je werkt nauwkeurig, gestructureerd en hebt oog voor detail.
Je bent nieuwsgierig en wil begrijpen hoe bedrijfsprocessen werken, van commerciële activiteiten tot financiële verwerking.
Je hebt een sterke interesse in commerciële processen en de impact ervan op bedrijfsresultaten.
Je kan cijfers niet alleen rapporteren, maar ook interpreteren en toelichten aan business stakeholders.
Je hebt ervaring met BI- en rapporteringstools zoals Qlik Sense, Power BI, Tableau of gelijkaardige oplossingen.
Je hebt een goede kennis van SQL en databanken.
Een gevorderde kennis van Excel is een pluspunt.
Kennis van Python of andere analysetools is een troef.
Je communiceert vlot met zowel operationele gebruikers als management.
Je hebt een uitstekende kennis van het Nederlands. Een goede kennis van Frans is een plus.




We bieden je

Bij Mediafin kom je terecht in een succesvol mediabedrijf. Innovatie, ambitie en ondernemingszin maken deel uit van ons DNA. Talent vormt de kern van wie we zijn. Daarom hechten we veel belang aan je persoonlijke ontwikkeling, een fijne werksfeer en een aangename werkomgeving op de bruisende site van Tour & Taxis.




Over Trustmedia

Trustmedia is de Advertising Solutions afdeling van Mediafin, uitgever van de zakenkranten en nieuwssites De Tijd en L’Echo, de personal finance magazines Mijn Geld/Mon Argent het weekendmagazine Sabato en de nieuwsbrieven De Belegger/L’Investisseur.

Met steeds vernieuwende communicatieoplossingen speelt Trustmedia in op de behoeften van haar klanten en streeft daarbij steeds naar het versterken van hun groei en hun marktpositie.

Research, advertising, crossmediale content services, projecten op maat en datatoepassingen, behoren tot de expertises van onze medewerkers. Hun oplossingen vertalen zich in steeds meer klanten. Op die manier verzekert Trustmedia aan bedrijven en organisaties een directe en efficiënte toegang tot het unieke en steeds groeiende lezerspubliek van De Tijd en L’Echo.

Title: Data Analyst / Data Visualisation
Description:
Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultant

Who We Are

NORRIQ is a leading international consulting firm headquartered in Denmark and Belgium, with 350+ consultants driving business transformation through technology. Our Financial Services practice includes 100+ specialists supporting banks, insurers, and financial institutions in navigating regulatory challenges, embracing emerging technologies, and meeting evolving client demands. We prioritize growth, innovation, and excellence, offering abundant opportunities for professional development and collaboration.

The Opportunity

NORRIQ is seeking to grow its Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence team with additional Data Analyst / Data Visualisation Consultants to improve the business performance of financial institutions. You will help our clients to optimize the value they can get from their internal and external data, through improved business performance monitoring and enhanced reporting quality and readability. Join us to work on impactful projects that shape the future of finance.

Responsibilities


Collecting and interpreting internal and external data about customer behaviour via the digital platforms (Web and mobile) and their digital transactions with the bank.
Translate data insight into actionable recommendations in order to coordinate the business needs around data and improve the bank’s digital and marketing strategy and optimize future campaigns.
Collect, analyze, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and correlations
Develop and maintain complex data models, databases, data warehouses and data marts .
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate insights to stakeholders.
Perform data quality checks and ensure data integrity, develop and implement data governance policies and procedures
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and emerging technologies in data analysis and visualization


Your Profile


Master’s degree (or equivalent work experience) in mathematics or statistics, business intelligence, computer engineering, economy.
1-3 years of experience in data projects, preferably within the financial services industry.
Technical Expertise & Tooling: SQL (mandatory), SAS (nice to have), Python & R (to support our AI team), Reporting & Visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Adobe Analytics, ...),.
ETL Processes: Affinity with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes, including an understanding of data preparation, cleansing, and integration from multiple sources.
Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving and analytical abilities to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Business Analysis Skills: Experience in requirements gathering, process design and developing action plans.
AI knowledge: AI knowledge & interest to connect easily with our data scientist and machine learning engineers.
Communication & Collaboration: Excellent communication skills; ability to work well in cross-functional teams and engage effectively with stakeholders.
Languages: Fluent in English and native in French or Dutch.


What We Offer


Dynamic Work Environment: High-impact projects with leading financial institutions in a fast-growing consulting firm.
Career Development: Continuous learning and growth opportunities to advance your career.
Collaborative Culture: A supportive, innovative team environment that encourages new ideas and professional development.
Global Exposure: International experience working on cross-border projects that shape the financial services industry.


Why Join Us?

At NORRIQ, we are shaping the future of finance through transformation and innovation. If you're passionate about improving financial processes, driving change, and delivering value to clients, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Title: Job | Data Analyst Risk, Finance & ESG | Bruxelles
Description:
Description

Vous aimez transformer des défis complexes en solutions techniques innovantes ? Vous souhaitez contribuer à des projets qui ont un impact sur la stratégie et les procédés Risque, Finance et ESG (Environnement, Social, Gouvernance) de Belfius ? Alors, nous avons besoin de vous !

Rejoignez nos équipes du Data Office, au cœur du département Risk. Ensemble, nous développons des solutions qui permettent de mesurer, analyser et améliorer les processus de données au service des activités Risque, Finance et ESG.

Votre job en deux mots

Vous serez responsable de la conception, du développement et de la maintenance de pipelines de données.

À ce titre, vous développerez et implémenterez des méthodologies ainsi que des solutions techniques pour soutenir les projets de la banque. Vous réaliserez des opérations ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) afin d’intégrer et de transformer les données, tout en travaillant dans un environnement de développement moderne, principalement basé sur Python et les services cloud Azure.

Enfin, vous comprendrez les besoins du business et les traduirez en solutions et analyses innovantes et automatisées, contribuant ainsi à renforcer l’impact durable de Belfius.

Pas besoin d’être un oiseau rare

Mais vous pouvez en devenir un, parmi nous.

Nous recherchons un(e) Data analyste technique et motivé(e), prêt(e) à se développer dans un environnement dynamique et innovant. Vous serez au cœur de l’exploitation de données, contribuant directement à l'impact positif de la banque.

Vos responsabilités principales:


Concevoir, développer et maintenir des pipelines de données dans Azure.
Programmer et implémenter des solutions techniques en Python (et PySpark) et en SQL.
Travailler avec des sources de données variées et les transformer en informations exploitables.
Contribuer à l'amélioration continue de notre écosystème data : qualité, gouvernance, documentation, bonnes pratiques.



Vous aurez l'opportunité de travailler sur des projets innovants et impactants, tout en développant vos compétences techniques et en durabilité.

Vous ferez partie d'une équipe dynamique et collaborative, où votre contribution sera valorisée et reconnue.

Chez nous, vous pouvez vous distinguer et révéler le meilleur de vous-même si vous répondez aux critères suivants:


Diplôme en STIM (Sciences, Technologies, Ingénierie, Mathématique) ou en sciences économiques/commerciales, ou expérience équivalente.
Expérience en développement et en manipulation de base de données.
Capacité à travailler en équipe et à communiquer efficacement avec les parties prenantes.
Esprit analytique et capacité à résoudre des problèmes techniques complexes.
Capacité à écouter les besoins et à y répondre avec des solutions data pragmatiques et efficaces.



Atouts supplémentaires


Bilingue (néerlandais/français) avec une bonne connaissance de l'anglais.
Curiosité naturelle et goût pour l'apprentissage continu.
Connaissance des services cloud Azure (Azure Synapse, DevOps), ainsi qu’une experience en développement (de préférence Python) est un plus.



Belfius prend soin de ses talents

Outre la possibilité de faire votre chemin dans un job qui vous plaît, nous vous offrons un contrat à temps plein à durée indéterminée avec :


Un salaire attractif complété par une offre innovante vous octroyant un budget à utiliser selon vos préférences personnelles (p. ex. voiture, vélo, PC, smartphone, tablette, loyer ou crédit hypothécaire sous certaines conditions) et des avantages extralégaux, tels que des chèques-repas, des avantages sur les produits et services internes, une assurance hospitalisation et une assurance groupe.
Des possibilités de développement par le biais d'un large éventail de formations et des opportunités de carrière dans l’ensemble du groupe Belfius.
Un bon équilibre entre vie professionnelle et vie privée grâce à des horaires flexibles, à la combinaison du travail au bureau et à domicile et un nombre attractif de jours de congé.
Un lieu de travail moderne au cœur de Bruxelles, avec une salle de sport branchée, un délicieux restaurant sain et des espaces de réunion confortables, le tout à quelques pas de la rue commerciale, de la gare Bruxelles-Nord et de la station métro Rogier.

Title: Data Analyst - BI & Automation
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
Automate reporting processes where possible.
Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
Strong Excel skills.
Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
Experience with financial or business performance data.
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
Microsoft Power Automate experience.
Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.
Independent, structured, and reliable.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics)

📍 Lugano | Hybrid Working Model | Permanent Position

About the Company

Our client is a well-established financial services institution operating in Switzerland and committed to leveraging data-driven insights to enhance customer experience, digital engagement and business performance.

To support the continued development of its analytics capabilities, we are looking for a Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics) to join a dynamic and collaborative team based in Lugano.

This role offers a unique opportunity to combine data analytics, business intelligence, customer insights and predictive modelling within a modern and innovative environment.

Your Mission

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

Title: Data Analyst – Cloud Migration & BigQuery
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)
Perform data profiling to identify anomalies, duplicates, and inconsistencies
Define logical data mappings from Oracle to BigQuery for each data domain
Collaborate with data engineers on the definition of transformation rules


Validation & Quality Assurance


Design and implement data reconciliation tests for pre- and post-migration phases
Develop SQL / BigQuery SQL queries to compare source and target datasets
Produce data quality reports covering completeness, accuracy, and consistency metrics
Log and track data anomalies using issue-tracking tools
Strong data modeling skills, including conceptual, logical, and physical design, with hands-on experience in normalization and dimensional modeling (star/snowflake schema).﻿


Migration Support ﻿


Actively participate in sprint planning and stand-ups with technical and business teams
Support the migration of legacy reports into GCP-native tools (Looker, Looker Studio)
Contribute to the definition of data governance standards and cloud best practices
Document data transformation processes for knowledge transfer purposes


Personal Profile & Soft Skills﻿


Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders
Operational autonomy and a proactive approach to problem-solving
Good time management in multi-task and project-driven environments
Team player: comfortable working in cross-functional IT/Business teams
Flexibility and adaptability in contexts of technological change
Language: Italian Native or Bilingual
Language: English (written and spoken, minimum B2 level)


Education


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Specialization courses in data engineering, cloud computing, or analytics are a plus

Title: Energy Data Engineer
Description:
Drive the energy transition by providing sustainable technological solutions through our digital platform, facilitating from planning to contract tendering to implementation of renewable energy systems in real estate and industrial companies. We enable our customers to increase the economic efficiency and CO2 balance of their properties while minimizing technical, commercial, and energy risks.

To strengthen our Product Management team, we are looking for an Energy Data Engineer who will contribute to the analysis of energy data, support the development of AI-based price and energy forecasts, and generate actionable insights through the analysis of operational and financial performance. Join this energy revolution and help us transform energy supply in a sustainable and economically viable way!

Tasks


Analyze energy data, including production, consumption, and storage information to identify optimization opportunities
Conduct AI-based market analysis and contribute to the development of price and energy forecasts
Analyze asset data, project progress and operational, environmental as well as financial performance
Perform trend, variance and gap analysis, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Validate data quality and perform plausibility checks to continuously improve analytical outputs
Develop and maintain a central data and reporting infrastructure in close collaboration with IT teams
Create KPI reports, dashboards, and data visualizations for internal and external stakeholders
Collaborate closely with management, operations, sales, and country organizations to support data-driven decision-making


Requirements


Completed degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Business Informatics, Physics, Industrial Engineering or a related field.
2–4 years of experience as a Data Analyst, ideally within an energy sector
Proven experience in designing and building purpose-driven analytical environments
Experienced in building data pipelines, including data extraction, validation, transformation and loading
Practical experience working with modern cloud data infrastructures such as BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or Databricks
Ideally, experience applying and training AI models and machine learning algorithms
High level of proficiency in SQL and solid experience with Python, R, MATLAB or comparable programming languages


Benefits


An attractive salary package and exciting development opportunities
Further training in renewable energies and project management
A dynamic, innovative environment with short decision-making processes and plenty of creative freedom
The opportunity to play an active role in the energy transition and make a real impact

Title: Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F)
Description:
We are opening a position for a Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F) to strengthen a team committed to a high-level medical research program, based in Nyon. This role is part of a multidisciplinary environment combining data, science, and health, with a focus on structuring and advanced data exploitation in a demanding and evolving context.



Position Responsibilities

Analyze and exploit structured datasets, including longitudinal and potentially medical or sports data

Participate in structuring, validating, and documenting data (rules, definitions, quality)

Contribute to analytical projects related to research and innovation activities

Identify methodological limitations and propose improvements in data usage

Collaborate with non-technical stakeholders to popularize analytical issues and results

Intervene in the development and improvement of platforms or tools related to data management

Monitor multiple projects in parallel with an organized and rigorous approach

Gradually support activities as an autonomous reference on data issues

Job requirements

Degree in data science, statistics, computer science, or equivalent field

Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in data analysis, data management, or research support

Good understanding of complex data (health, sports, or longitudinal data is an asset)

Developed analytical mindset with the ability to assess data quality and relevance

Proficiency in at least one analytical language or tool (R, Python, SQL, SPSS, or equivalent)

Comfortable with Microsoft office tools, especially Excel

Ability to work in a team while gradually gaining autonomy

Proficiency in written and spoken English (French and/or German is an advantage)

Joining Sword means...
💼 Continuous training: A training budget that increases every month, with access to a wide range of courses

🏝 25 days of paid leave: All our employees are entitled to 25 days of paid vacation per year

🚑 International accident insurance coverage

🎉 Regular events: Monthly gatherings to connect in both small and larger groups

🌍 International environments: Opportunities to work on projects within large international organizations

🛠 Innovation: Contribute to impactful and innovative projects across various industries

🎁 Little perks: Our employees benefit from an attractive perks and discount program

Title: Associate, Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Associate, Data Analyst, Business Intelligence to join our BI implementation team based in Stabio, Switzerland.




Let’s talk about the role!

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Associate to join our EMEA Business Intelligence team.




This role is ideal for someone with a foundational understanding of data analytics and business intelligence who is eager to grow their technical and functional skills in a collaborative environment. The successful candidate will support the maintenance and enhancement of Data & Analytics solutions while working closely with senior team members and business stakeholders.




How You Will Make A Difference

Support the development and maintenance of ETL processes using tools such as SAP BODS, SQL Server, and Visual Studio
Hands on SQL, SAP HANA, Databricks, Power BI, Genie AI, IBM Cognos
Help monitor and maintain existing data solutions and pipelines
Participate in the analysis of current BI and analytics platforms to identify improvement opportunities
Contribute to technical and functional documentation for Data initiatives and migration projects
Work with business users and technical teams to understand requirements and support the delivery of BI solutions
Monitor scheduled jobs and escalate issues when necessary
Support implementation and adoption of Data AI solutions
Support cloud migration initiatives




Skills For Success

Good knowledge of SQL and relational databases
Familiarity with data warehousing and ETL concepts
Exposure to SAP HANA, SAP BODS, SQL Server, Databricks, Power BI or similar technologies is considered a plus
Familiarity with Cognitive AI concept and products
Ability to document processes and technical solutions clearly and accurately
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to understand business processes and translate business requirements into scalable technology and data solutions
Good attention to detail and willingness to learn new technologies
Effective communication, teamwork and proactiveness skills
English fluency, both written and spoken, is mandatory




Preferred Qualifications

Academic background in scientific or quantitative disciplines is mandatory
Up to 2 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field, preferably within multinational and international business environments.
Experience through internships, academic projects, or professional roles involving data analysis or reporting
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Agile methodology




About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com




What’s In It For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do
An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together
Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization
On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives
A discount card with 50% on all VF brands
Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks




Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.




If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

  • Familiarity with data warehousing and ETL concepts
  • Exposure to SAP HANA, SAP BODS, SQL Server, Databricks, Power BI or similar technologies is considered a plus
  • Familiarity with Cognitive AI concept and products
  • Ability to document processes and technical solutions clearly and accurately
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to understand business processes and translate business requirements into scalable technology and data solutions
  • Good attention to detail and willingness to learn new technologies
  • Effective communication, teamwork and proactiveness skills
  • English fluency, both written and spoken, is mandatory


Preferred Qualifications

  • Academic background in scientific or quantitative disciplines is mandatory
  • Up to 2 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field, preferably within multinational and international business environments.
  • Experience through internships, academic projects, or professional roles involving data analysis or reporting
  • Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
  • Agile methodology


About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com


What’s In It For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

  • A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do
  • An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together
  • Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization
  • On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives
  • A discount card with 50% on all VF brands
  • Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks


Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.


If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

Title: Data Platform & Analytics Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Rolle

In dieser Rolle tauchst du tief in komplexe Datenwelten ein und verwandelst vielfältige IT-Datenquellen in klare, belastbare Entscheidungsgrundlagen. Du verantwortest die Konzeption, Umsetzung und Optimierung von Analysen, Reports und Dashboards und stellst sicher, dass Datenqualität, Datenkonsistenz und Transparenz jederzeit gewährleistet sind. Mit deinem fachlichen Know-how in Statistik, Datenmodellierung und gängigen Analyse-Tools identifizierst du Muster, Abweichungen und Potenziale in grossen Datenmengen. Du arbeitest eng mit verschiedenen Stakeholdern zusammen, übersetzt fachliche Anforderungen in technische Lösungen und präsentierst Ergebnisse adressatengerecht und verständlich. Durch deine strukturierte, analytische Arbeitsweise trägst du entscheidend dazu bei, datengetriebene Entscheidungen im IT-Bereich zu stärken und kontinuierliche Verbesserungen anzustossen.

Verantwortung


Du entwickelst und betreibst skalierbare Datenmodelle für analytische Plattformen
Du optimierst Datenpipelines und stellst konsistente Datenverfügbarkeit sicher
Du verantwortest Komponenten einer Reporting- und Visualisierungsplattform
Du koordinierst Anforderungen zwischen Fachbereichen und externen Partnern
Du treibst die Weiterentwicklung der Data- und Analytics-Architektur aktiv voran


Qualifikationen

Du bringst Erfahrung in SQL-basiertem Data Engineering oder Data Warehousing mit
Du kennst dich mit Reporting-Tools wie Power BI, Tableau oder Qlik aus
Du hast ein Grundverständnis für Linux-basierte Systeme oder Cloud-Datenplattformen
Du arbeitest eigenverantwortlich, kommunikativ und lösungsorientiert

Flexible Arbeitszeitgestaltung

Title: Ticketing Data Coordinator
Description:
Job information

Division/Unit: UEFA Events SA / Ticketing

Contract type: Max-term

Location: Nyon (WFH up to one day per week)

Start date: 01.10.2026

End date: 31.07.2029

Main goal

The Ticketing Data Coordinator works with the Ticketing Data Analyst to deliver actionable ticketing data analytics and insights for UEFA club and national team competition finals, including UEFA EURO 2028 and UEFA Women’s EURO 2029.

The role primarily focuses on mobile ticketing data, while also supporting overall ticketing data quality and analysis to optimise sales operations, mobile ticket distribution and the fan experience.

Key Responsibilities


Developing, maintaining and optimising ticketing data sources within Snowflake and reporting dashboards in Tableau, or similar systems
Ensuring ticketing data quality and integrity across multiple platforms (SSO, ticketing sales system, UEFA Mobile Tickets app, etc.)
Analysing ticket sales and mobile distribution trends to identify patterns, risks and opportunities
Identifying and tracking KPIs to measure ticketing performance and operational success delivery
Working with multiple stakeholders, in particular internal ICT colleagues as well as external providers
Supporting operational tasks in relation to the distribution of mobile tickets, including monitoring, performance and incident tracking
Gaining sufficient knowledge on ticketing processes from end to end, including venue configuration, inventory management, ticket sales, mobile ticketing, access control and custom
Supporting and performing other ticketing functions as required

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in computer science, data analytics, engineering or a related field
From 1 to 3 years in data analysis using Tableau or Power BI and working with Snowflake or similar data warehouse platforms


Languages:


Fluent in English


Other requirements:


Data-driven and analytical mindset
Interest in mobile applications on Android and iOS
Experience in fraud prevention is an advantage
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-pressure event environment
Strong organisational skills and excellent attention to detail
Proactive problem solver with a collaborative team-player approach
Comfortable working with a high degree of flexibility, autonomy and responsibility.

UEFA welcomes applications from all, actively working to ensure that we are an inclusive employer that finds the best candidate for our positions, regardless of aspects such as sex, gender identification, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity or nationality.

Title: Data Warehouse Specialist Controlling 80 – 100% (w/m/d)
Description:
Das wirst du bewirken


Du wirkst aktiv beim Betrieb und der Weiterentwicklung unseres zentralen Data Warehouses mit und stellst hochwertige Daten für Reporting, Analysen und das Tagesgeschäft bereit.
Gemeinsam mit verschiedenen Fachbereichen analysierst du Anforderungen, priorisierst Reportingbedarfe und entwickelst praxisnahe Lösungen.
Wiederkehrende Reports und Datenaufbereitungen automatisierst, optimierst und betreust du selbstständig.
In bereichsübergreifenden Projekten und Arbeitsgruppen bringst du dein Fachwissen ein und entwickelst Prozesse sowie Anwendungen kontinuierlich weiter.
Mit deinem analytischen und technischen Verständnis gestaltest du moderne Datenlösungen, Reportingtools und die Weiterentwicklung unserer Business-Intelligence-Landschaft.



Das bringst du mit


Du verfügst über eine abgeschlossene Ausbildung in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder eine vergleichbare Ausbildung mit entsprechender Berufserfahrung, idealerweise im Banken- oder Finanzumfeld.
Sehr gute SQL-Kenntnisse sowie Erfahrung mit relationalen Datenbanken und Reportinglösungen im Microsoft-Umfeld zeichnen dich aus.
Du kommunizierst fliessend in Deutsch und arbeitest analytisch, strukturiert sowie selbstständig.
Hohe Dienstleistungsorientierung, Eigeninitiative und Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Anspruchsgruppen runden dein Profil ab.



Auskünfte

Gerne beantwortet Mathis Hungerbühler, Leiter Controlling, Telefon 071 231 35 67, ab dem 20. Juli 2026 deine Fragen.

Bewerbung

www.sgkb.ch/karriere, Ivo Streule, HR Business Partner

Title: Data Scientist / Analyst/-in II (OSINT)
Description:
Zimmerwald | 80-100% (Jobsharing möglich)

Diesen Beitrag kannst du leisten


Strukturierte und unstrukturierte Daten aus öffentlich zugänglichen Quellen beschaffen, zusammenführen und analysieren, um nachrichtendienstliche Produkte zu erarbeiten
Geodaten für die nachrichtendienstlichen Produkte aufbereiten; mithilfe von Analysetools und Data-Mining-Methoden Datenanalysen und Modellierungen durchführen; Visualisierungen und Karten erstellen
Informationen bearbeiten und abgleichen, um die aufgestellten Hypothesen zu bestätigen/ zu widerlegen
Die technischen und methodischen Entwicklungen in den Bereichen Web Intelligence / Social Media Intelligence sowie FIMI verfolgen und auswerten
Als kompetente/-r Ansprechpartner/-in für Anfragen, Referate sowie Fachgespräche im eigenen Analysebereich zur Verfügung stehen
Die relevanten Entwicklungen im Bereich der Sicherheitspolitik verfolgen und die eigenen Kenntnisse erweitern


Das macht dich einzigartig


Höhere Fachausbildung: Masterabschluss (Universität/ETH, HF) in Informationstechnologie, Informatik, Politikwissenschaften, Kriminalistik, internationale Beziehungen oder Sicherheitspolitik
Selbständige Arbeitsweise, rasche Auffassungsgabe, analytisches Denken, ausgezeichnete Teamfähigkeit sowie redaktionelle Fähigkeiten
Kenntnisse in OSINT; Beherrschen der Techniken in Internet- und Social-Media-Recherche; Fähigkeit, in digitalen Infrastrukturen Nachforschungen anzustellen
Erfahrung in den Bereichen Programmiersprachen (insbesondere Python) sowie Beschaffungs-, Analyse- und Visualisierungstools; Kenntnisse der Best Practices in Entwicklung (git, CI/CD)
Ziel- und ergebnisorientiertes Arbeiten; Interesse an neuen Technologien und Themen der internationalen Sicherheitspolitik
Gute aktive Kenntnisse einer zweiten Amtssprache und sehr gute Englischkenntnisse; weitere Fremdsprachen sind von Vorteil


Auf den Punkt gebracht

Du arbeitest autonom in einem Team von OSINT-Spezialistinnen und -Spezialisten. In diesem dynamischen, auf die Organisationen des Sicherheitsverbundes Schweiz ausgerichteten Arbeitsumfeld trägst du mit hohem Verantwortungsbewusstsein zur Beschaffung nützlicher Informationen für die Sicherheitspolitik der Schweiz bei.

Das bieten wir


Arbeiten für die Schweiz Wir setzen uns für das Erfolgsmodell Schweiz ein und arbeiten zum Wohl der Bevölkerung.
Gelebte Vielfalt Dank Chancengleichheit entfalten wir unsere Kompetenzen und bringen unterschiedliche Perspektiven ein.
Gesund am Arbeitsplatz Wir unterstützen und beraten unsere Mitarbeitenden im Bereich der physischen und psychischen Gesundheit.


Alle Benefits

Dein Einsatz für Sicherheit und Freiheit

Das Kommando Cyber (Kdo Cy) steht für Kompetenz in den Bereichen Cyber und Digitalisierung. Wir vereinen die Begeisterung für unsere Aufgabe mit Know-how, Innovation, modernsten Technologien und agilen Arbeitsweisen. Wir erbringen sichere, robuste und resiliente Informatikleistungen für die Schweizer Armee und unsere Partner im Sicherheitsverbund Schweiz. Damit ermöglicht das Kdo Cy der Schweizer Armee den notwendigen Wissens- und Entscheidvorsprung – für eine sichere Schweiz. Die Schweizer Armee beschäftigt über 9'000 Mitarbeitende in vier militärischen und rund 200 zivilen Berufen – und dies an 110 Standorten schweizweit. Auch für den Nachwuchs ist gesorgt: Die Schweizer Armee bildet in über 30 Berufen Lernende aus.

Zusätzliche Informationen

Eine Anstellung bei der Schweizer Armee macht Sinn und bringt viele Vorteile mit sich. Überzeuge dich auf armee.ch/vorteile. Interessiert an Jobsharing? Du kannst dich einzeln oder mit Partner/-in bewerben. Auch wenn ihr euch im Duo bewerbt: Reicht eure Bewerbungen einzeln ein und gebt den Namen der Partnerin/des Partners im Bewerbungskonto an. Angebote von Personalvermittlern werden nicht berücksichtigt. Für weitere Auskünfte kontaktiere melde dich bittte über folgende Kontaktdaten: Tel. 058 464 19 01 oder DAI.contact.cy@vtg.admin.ch

Fragen zur Stelle

Title: Datenanalyst/-in Polizeiliche Lage, 80-100% (befr. 3 Jahre)
Description:
Analysieren. Vernetzen. Vorausschauen.

Im Lagezentrum der Kantonspolizei Bern verarbeiten/entwickeln Sie Daten- und Analyseprozesse weiter, identifizieren Trends und erarbeiten aussagekräftige Lagebilder für die operative und strategische Führung. Mit Ihrem Know-how tragen Sie dazu bei, die Sicherheitslage von morgen schon heute zu verstehen.

Ihre Aufgaben


Erarbeitung, Implementierung und Weiterentwicklung interner Standards und Prozesse zur Steuerung lagerelevanten Daten sowie deren Analyse sowie Visualisierung
Aufbau und Pflege lagerelevanter Datenbanken und deren Datenablagestruktur
Entwicklung und Implementierung von Qualitätssicherungsverfahren zwecks Gewährleistung der Genauigkeit und Zuverlässigkeit von Lageinformationen
Unterstützung von IT-Projekten sowie Teilnahme an Fachgremien
Technische Unterstützung der Nutzer/-innen der Datenbanken und Anwendungen
Unterstützung im Bereich polizeiliche Lagebeurteilung (u.a. via OSINT)
Erstellung von umfassenden Analyseberichten zuhanden operative und strategische Führungsebene
Trend Scouting


Ihr Profil


Abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium in Informatik oder Datenwissenschaften und/oder vergleichbare Ausbildung/Praxiserfahrung
Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in einem relevanten Themengebiet
Kenntnisse und Interesse in den Bereichen automatisierte Datenverarbeitung, KI/ML und Wissensmanagement
Erfahrung in der Entwicklung von Datenarchitekturen
Erfahrung mit Programmiersprachen
Ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit und Flexibilität
Sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeit gepaart mit einer hohen sozialen Kompetenz
Hohe Belastbarkeit, schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie ausgeprägte Eigeninitiative
Bereitschaft für Pikettdienst sowie unregelmässigen Einsätzen (Wochenende und Nacht)


Wir bieten Ihnen


Attraktive Sozialleistungen und Vorsorge
Sicherer Arbeitgeber und transparentes Lohnsystem
Dynamisches Umfeld in einem Grossbetrieb, spannende Kontakte mit Menschen im Polizeialltag
Wertebasierte, wertschätzende Umgangsformen
Stufengerechte Personalentwicklung: kompetente Einarbeitung, Unterstützung von individueller Weiterbildung, Entwicklung des Frauenanteils
Flexible Arbeitsmodelle: Jahresarbeitszeit, Teilzeit-, Topmodelle, Homeoffice
Gesundheitsförderung: verschiedene Sport- und Kulturangebote, Gesundheitscheck
Mobiltelefon inkl. Übernahme der Abokosten
Arbeitsplatz im Zentrum der Stadt Bern
