  • Unterstützung im Bereich polizeiliche Lagebeurteilung (u.a. via OSINT)
  • Erstellung von umfassenden Analyseberichten zuhanden operative und strategische Führungsebene
  • Trend Scouting

Ihr Profil

  • Abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium in Informatik oder Datenwissenschaften und/oder vergleichbare Ausbildung/Praxiserfahrung
  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in einem relevanten Themengebiet
  • Kenntnisse und Interesse in den Bereichen automatisierte Datenverarbeitung, KI/ML und Wissensmanagement
  • Erfahrung in der Entwicklung von Datenarchitekturen
  • Erfahrung mit Programmiersprachen
  • Ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit und Flexibilität
  • Sehr gute Kommunikationsfähigkeit gepaart mit einer hohen sozialen Kompetenz
  • Hohe Belastbarkeit, schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie ausgeprägte Eigeninitiative
  • Bereitschaft für Pikettdienst sowie unregelmässigen Einsätzen (Wochenende und Nacht)

Wir bieten Ihnen

  • Attraktive Sozialleistungen und Vorsorge
  • Sicherer Arbeitgeber und transparentes Lohnsystem
  • Dynamisches Umfeld in einem Grossbetrieb, spannende Kontakte mit Menschen im Polizeialltag
  • Wertebasierte, wertschätzende Umgangsformen
  • Stufengerechte Personalentwicklung: kompetente Einarbeitung, Unterstützung von individueller Weiterbildung, Entwicklung des Frauenanteils
  • Flexible Arbeitsmodelle: Jahresarbeitszeit, Teilzeit-, Topmodelle, Homeoffice
  • Gesundheitsförderung: verschiedene Sport- und Kulturangebote, Gesundheitscheck
  • Mobiltelefon inkl. Übernahme der Abokosten
  • Arbeitsplatz im Zentrum der Stadt Bern

Title: Master Data Expert
Description:
In Cartier we have built a strong flow network based on a detailed and qualitative Data Environnement. We are working to ensure data quality and integrity of all our Cartier articles delivered from the manufactures to our central and worldwide distribution centers. Our portfolio is diverse and with many specificities which need to be maintained, monitored and enriched regularly.

Your role

We are looking for a person to contribute to the team’s mission: provide expertise on master data product topics, operational support for markets and operational functions:


Provide Master Data Expertise and Process Excellence to operational cases
Lead and contribute to Projects to meet data article business needs
Pilot Continuous Improvement: Map existing processes, identify solutions and implement them
Support operational workflows on key master data
Data Analytics: manage data by extracting, comparing, testing, and analyzing in order to identify issues and propose solutions.
Monitor KPIs for data consistency and alignment in the various sources, define and follow up the relevant action plans
Data Compliance: Manage Compliance topics related to Master Data
The successful candidate will also contribute to team performance by being part of transformation projects and new tools testing and implementation.
Develop a strong partnership with our main partners within Cartier and Richemont


Split between Operational tasks and Initiatives will be around 70% for Projects and 30% for Operational.

How will you experience success with us?

You have an academic background in Supply Chain and Data, paired with solid professional experience in Data Management, Supply Chain, and Operations. You are technically proficient in SAP systems, BI tools such as PowerBI or Google Looker, SQL, and Google Cloud Platform data architecture. Combining strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, you are proactive, curious, and well-versed in Agile methodologies. Bilingual in French and English, you possess the excellent communication skills needed to easily bridge the gap between technical and non-technical teams in a matrix organization. Finally, you are a rigorous, highly organized professional with an structured approach to your work.

How do we keep you smiling?

You will be a part of a dynamic and international work environment, where we always go the extra mile to uphold outstanding service for our clients.

You are able to work with a team of motivated professionals, at the heart of the Supply Chain, in a luxury Maison.

supervised.

Title: Data & Analytics Specialist (Life Sciences)
Description:
Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company that offers customers from various industries a unique range of analytical services to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat to the water you drink to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins works with the world's largest companies to ensure the products delivered are safe, theirs Ingredients are authentic and labeling is accurate. The company is a leading global provider of analytics for food, environmental, pharmaceutical and cosmetic products as well as contract research (CRO) services.



Eurofins has more than 950+ laboratories within its global network, employing over 65’000 staff.



Eurofins Professional Scientific Service® (PSS) is a global insourcing solution for our customers. Together with the customer, we define a project for the required services that our Eurofins employees should provide at the customer's location. During the ongoing project, the responsibility for project management, the previously defined laboratory services and the deployment of our employees in this project lies with Eurofins PSS.





Job Description



As a Data & Analytics Specialist, you will join the Eurofins Professional Scientific Services (PSS) team to support our client with the development and implementation of data-driven solutions across their Process Science & Tech Ops Department. You will contribute to data analytics, data management, automation, and visualization initiatives, transforming complex data into actionable insights and supporting data quality, governance, and operational efficiency.



This role offers the opportunity to combine analytics, technology, and Life Sciences expertise within a collaborative and international environment.



Contribute to the development and execution of a centralized data strategy, including the rollout of data solutions, data governance and data quality guidelines
Design, develop and implement data analytical solutions for data processing, analysis, and visualization
Develop scalable data pipelines and workflows using Databricks and cloud technologies (AWS)
Maintain and continuously improve databases, supporting systems, templates and metadata to ensure reliable data operations
Monitor data quality metrics and continuously improve data accuracy, consistency, and robustness across systems
Develop automation solutions, web applications and API integrations
Contribute to local and global projects and collaborate with internal and external stakeholders


Qualifications


Degree in Data Science, Bioinformatics, Computer Science, Life Sciences, or a related field
Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, SQL, R, or similar scripting languages
Experience in data analysis, data management, and database systems
Knowledge of Databricks is an advantage
Familiarity with cloud platforms, preferably AWS
Experience with web application development and API integrations
Hands-on experience with Power BI and data visualization best practices
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to data quality and accuracy
Ability to work effectively in cross-functional and international project teams


Additional Information



Company Culture



At Eurofins PSS, our company culture is deeply rooted in our four core values: Teamwork, Integrity, Superlative Customer Satisfaction, and Productivity. We believe in fostering a collaborative environment where every team member is encouraged to contribute their unique expertise and ideas. Our commitment to integrity drives us to uphold the highest ethical standards in all our endeavors. We strive for superlative customer satisfaction by understanding and exceeding our clients' expectations. Embracing productivity, we continuously seek innovative ways to streamline processes and deliver exceptional results. Together, these values form the foundation of a supportive and empowering workplace, enabling us to achieve excellence in everything we do.



Opportunities for Growth



As part of our commitment to employee development, we offer abundant growth opportunities at Eurofins PSS. You'll have access to learning and development programs, coaching & mentorship, and the chance to take on challenging projects, empowering you to achieve your professional aspirations.



Equal Opportunity Employer Statement



At Eurofins PSS, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, embracing diversity, and cultivating an inclusive workplace where every individual's unique background and perspective are valued, respected, and celebrated.







Please note that as per Eurofins policy, we do not accept unsolicited resumes from third-party recruiters unless such recruiters were engaged to provide candidates for a specified opening. Any employment agency, person or entity that submits an unsolicited resume does so with the understanding that Eurofins will have the right to hire that applicant at its discretion without any fee owed to the submitting employment agency, person or entity.

Title: Data scientist / analyste II (OSINT)
Description:
Zimmerwald | 80-100% (jobsharing possible)

Ce à quoi tu peux contribuer


Collecter, fusionner et analyser des données structurées et non structurées issues de sources ouvertes, afin d'élaborer des produits de renseignement
Préparer des géodonnées pour les produits de renseignement, réaliser des analyses de données et des modélisations (à l'aide d'outils d'analyse et des méthodes de data mining), créer des visualisations et autres cartographies
Traiter et croiser les informations pour confirmer/ infirmer les hypothèses faites
Suivre et évaluer les évolutions techniques et méthodiques dans le domaine Web intelligence/ Social Media intelligence ainsi que dans la thématique FIMI
Assumer le rôle d'interlocuteur/-trice qualifié/-e pour des demandes, des exposés et des entretiens techniques dans votre domaine d'analyse
Suivre les développements pertinents en matière de politique de sécurité et élargir ses connaissances


Ce qui te rend unique


Formation spécialisée supérieure : master (université/ EPF, ES) en technologie de l'information, informatique, sciences politiques ou criminelles, relations internationales, politique de sécurité
Autonomie, vivacité d'esprit, pensée analytique, excellent esprit d'équipe et aisance rédactionnelle
Connaissances en OSINT, maîtrise des techniques de recherche sur internet et les réseaux sociaux, capacité à investiguer des infrastructures numériques
Expérience dans les domaines suivants : langages de programmation (Python en particulier) ; outils utilisés en matière de collecte, d'analyse et de visualisation ; connaissances des bonnes pratiques de développement (git, CI/CD)
Capacité à se focaliser sur les objectifs et les résultats, intérêt pour les nouvelles technologies et les sujets liés à la politique de sécurité internationale
Bonnes connaissances actives dans une deuxième langue officielle, ainsi que de très bonnes connaissances en anglais. La maîtrise d'autres langues étrangères constitue un avantage


En quelques mots

Tu travailleras de manière autonome au sein d'une équipe de spécialistes OSINT. Dans cet environnement de travail dynamique, axé sur les organisations du Réseau national de sécurité, tu faites preuve d'un niveau élevé de responsabilités et veux contribuer à l'acquisition d'informations utiles à la politique de sécurité de la Suisse.

Ce que nous offrons


Travailler pour la Suisse Nous nous engageons pour le succès du modèle suisse et pour le bien-être de la population.
La diversité au quotidien Nous veillons à l'égalité des chances, qui nous permet de déployer nos compétences et d'exprimer des points de vue différents.
Santé au travail Nous assistons et conseillons nos collaboratrices et collaborateurs pour toutes les questions liées à la santé physique ou psychique.


Tous les avantages

Ton engagement pour la sécurité et la liberté

Le commandement Cyber (cdmt Cyber) est gage de compétences en matière cyber et de numérisation. Nous allions l'enthousiasme pour notre mission à notre savoir-faire et les associons à l'innovation, aux technologies les plus modernes et aux méthodes de travail agiles. Nous fournissons des prestations informatiques sûres, robustes et résilientes pour l'Armée suisse et nos partenaires du Réseau national de sécurité. Le cdmt Cyber permet ainsi à notre armée d'avoir l'avance nécessaire en matière de connaissances et de décisions pour assurer la sécurité de la Suisse. L'Armée suisse emploie plus de 9000 collaborateurs/trices dans quatre professions militaires et environ 200 professions civiles, et ce, sur 110 sites dans toute la Suisse. Elle assure également la relève en formant des apprenti/e/s dans plus de 30 métiers.

Informations complémentaires

Travailler auprès de l'Armée suisse est une option judicieuse qui présente de nombreux avantages. Plus d'informations sur armee.ch/avantages. Le jobsharing t'intéresse ? Tu peux postuler individuellement ou avec un partenaire. Si vous postulez ensemble, veuillez nous faire parvenir vos dossiers de candidature séparément en indiquant le nom du partenaire dans le compte d'utilisateur. Les offres émanant de bureaux de placement de personnel ne seront pas retenues. Pour plus d'informations, merci de nous contacter aux coordonnées suivantes : Tél. 058 464 19 01 ou DAI.contact.cy@vtg.admin.ch

Title: Analyste de données
Description:
Poste: Bienne




Contrat: Contrat à durée indéterminée avec Alten Suisse.




Nous recherchons un(e) Analyste de données pour rejoindre un environnement industriel à forte exigence qualité et contribuer à la fiabilisation, à l’analyse et à l’optimisation des données de référence. Véritable interface entre les opérations et les systèmes d’information, vous jouerez un rôle clé dans la cohérence des données, l’amélioration continue des processus et le succès des projets de transformation.




Responsabilités :




Assurer la création, la mise à jour et le nettoyage des données de référence en garantissant leur cohérence et leur intégrité
Veiller à l’alignement entre les données présentes dans les systèmes d’information et la réalité du terrain
Analyser la qualité des données, identifier les écarts et mettre en œuvre les actions correctives nécessaires
Participer activement aux initiatives d’amélioration continue visant à renforcer la fiabilité et la stabilité des données
Contribuer à la définition et à la maintenance des standards liés aux temps opératoires et aux données industrielles associées
Réaliser les analyses et chiffrages nécessaires à la gestion des temps de production et assurer la saisie des informations de référence
Accompagner les projets de migration et de transformation des systèmes en garantissant la conformité et la qualité des données
Apporter un support aux équipes métiers en traitant les anomalies, investigations et demandes opérationnelles
Collaborer avec des interlocuteurs variés dans un contexte transverse et multidisciplinaire




Qualifications :




Formation technique supérieure (ES, CFC complété par une spécialisation, ou équivalent)
Expérience significative dans l’analyse, la gestion ou la gouvernance des données au sein d’un environnement industriel
Excellente compréhension des données techniques et industrielles telles que les nomenclatures, gammes, coûts de fabrication ou données d’assemblage
Bonne maîtrise des systèmes ERP, idéalement SAP, ainsi que des solutions PLM
Une connaissance d’outils de reporting ou de pilotage de données constitue un atout
Solides capacités d’analyse et aptitude à structurer des informations complexes
Rigueur, sens de l’organisation et approche méthodique
Français courant




Postulez dès maintenant et faites partie de notre incroyable aventure. Nous croyons en une croissance commune !

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Ivy Partners est une société de conseil suisse qui accompagne les entreprises dans leurs défis stratégiques, technologiques et organisationnels.




Nous avons à cœur d’offrir à nos collaborateurs une carrière qui favorise leur épanouissement personnel et professionnel. Nous les accompagnons dans leur montée en compétences et leur ouvrons de réelles perspectives d’évolution.




En tant que Business Analyst Data chez Ivy Partners, vous serez amené(e) à :

Recueillir les besoins métiers et animer des ateliers avec différentes parties prenantes pour prioriser ces exigences.
Rédiger des spécifications fonctionnelles détaillées et participer à la modélisation des données, y compris la conception de modèles sémantiques.
Analyser les processus métiers pour identifier les opportunités d'amélioration et standardiser les usages des données.
Contribuer à la gouvernance des données en définissant des règles métier et des référentiels, tout en accompagnant les utilisateurs dans l’adoption des nouvelles solutions analytiques.
Assurer le maintien d’une documentation fonctionnelle efficace pour garantir la pérennité et la compréhension des solutions déployées.




Vous êtes notre candidat(e) idéal(e) si :

Vous avez entre 8 et 10 ans d’expérience dans le domaine de la Business Intelligence ou de l'analyse fonctionnelle.

Title: Business/Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Ekkiden ist eine in 2019 gegründete internationale Beratungsgruppe: sie vereint ein Ökosystem leidenschaftlicher und engagierter Beraterinnen und Berater, die Transformationsprojekte in den Bereichen Organisation, Operations und Technologie durchführen, insbesondere in IT/Digital, Industrie/F&E sowie Nachhaltigkeit, für Großunternehmen und KMU in Frankreich, der Schweiz, Spanien und Deutschland.

Über die Rolle

Als Business Analyst und/oder Data Analyst übernimmst du eine zentrale Rolle an der Schnittstelle zwischen Fachbereichen, Data/BI und IT. Du analysierst Prozesse und Daten, definierst Kennzahlen und Berichte und trägst so massgeblich zu datenbasierten Entscheidungen und zur Optimierung der Geschäftsabläufe bei.

Aufgaben


Aufnahme, Analyse und Strukturierung von fachlichen und datenbezogenen Anforderungen aus den Business Units
Analyse und Dokumentation von Geschäftsprozessen sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenflüsse und Systeme.
Durchführung von Datenanalysen und Auswertungen (z. B. in Excel oder BI-Tools) zur Unterstützung von Management-Entscheidungen.
Definition von KPIs, Reports und Dashboards in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und Data/BI-Teams.
Erstellung von Fachkonzepten, Spezifikationen und User Stories für datengetriebene Lösungen (Reporting, Analytics, Automatisierung).
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineers und BI-Spezialisten bei der Umsetzung der Anforderungen in Datenmodelle und Berichte.
Kontinuierliche Identifikation von Optimierungspotenzialen in Prozessen, Datenqualität und Transparenz.
Strukturierte Dokumentation von fachlichen Anforderungen, Prozessen und relevanten Datenstrukturen.


Must-have


Mehrjährige Erfahrung (5+) als Business Analyst, Data Analyst, Requirements Engineer oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle im datengetriebenen Umfeld
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten und ein ausgeprägtes Zahlenverständnis.
Erfahrung in der Arbeit mit Daten und Reporting (z. B. Excel, SQL-Grundlagen oder gängige BI-Tools z.B. Power BI)
Erfahrung in der Aufnahme und Dokumentation von Anforderungen (z. B. mittels Use Cases, User Stories, Fachkonzepten).
Sicheres Auftreten und starke Kommunikationsfähigkeit gegenüber fachlichen und technischen Stakeholdern.
Strukturierte, selbständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Qualitätsorientierung.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) in Wort und Schrift, gute Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil.


Nice to have


Kenntnisse in Datenmodellierung, DWH- oder ETL-Konzepten.
Erfahrung in agilen Methoden (Scrum, Kanban) und Arbeit mit Tools wie Jira und Confluence.
Kenntnisse in Prozessmodellierung (z. B. BPMN, UML).


Wie sieht der Rekrutierungsprozess bei Ekkiden aus?

Bei Ekkiden sind wir bestrebt, jedem Kandidaten eine positive Erfahrung zu bieten.


Erstgespräch: Unser Rekrutierungsteam wird sich mit Ihnen in Verbindung setzen, um Ihre Motivation und Erwartungen zu besprechen.
Interviews: Je nach der Rolle, für die Sie sich bewerben, führen Sie ein oder zwei Interviews mit unseren Geschäftsleitern.
Kundengespräch: Schließlich haben Sie die Möglichkeit, den Kunden kennenzulernen.


Obwohl dies unser Standardprozess ist, können wir Anpassungen vornehmen, die auf spezifischen Umständen basieren.

Title: Procurement Data Manager / Analyst (EMEA) 100% (f/m/d) (Contract through our external payroll partner with immediate start until 31.12.2026, possible extension)
Description:
At Julius Baer, we celebrate and value the individual qualities you bring, enabling you to be impactful, to be entrepreneurial, to be empowered, and to create value beyond wealth. Let’s shape the future of wealth management together.

Reporting to the Head of Procurement Data & Performance — who serves as the global data steward for procurement data and owns all relevant purchasing data — this role supports the design, management, and implementation of data standards

underpinning the new procurement platform across EMEA.

Contribute to the ramp-up of a global Source-to-Pay transformation, maintaining

alignment with regulatory and risk frameworks and working closely with stakeholders across HR, Finance, TPRM, Outsourcing, Legal, and IT.

This is a supporting role focused on designing and translating the data steward’s

standards into consistent, well-governed practices across the end-to-end lifecycle of purchasing data, including collection, enforcement, and reporting. This is not an

operational data-entry position.

YOUR CHALLENGE


Support the Head of Procurement Data & Performance in defining, managing, and implementing end-to-end standards for purchasing data collection, enforcement,
and reporting
Contribute to the definition and application of master data structures, standards, and governance within SAP S/4HANA, particularly for Business Partner records covering both suppliers and customers
Aid in data collection standardisation along supplier, engagement and commercial data aggregation
Safeguard data quality across key data objects along the procurement lifecycle, including purchase requisitions, purchase orders, contracts, contract schedules, standing payments, and associated conditions
Help embed data ownership, stewardship rules, and quality controls across systems feeding procurement processes
Contribute to the planning, implementation, and ongoing operation of addressable spend reporting


YOUR PROFILE


Degree or proven hands-on experience in information management, data science, business informatics, supply chain, finance, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience
Proven experience in procurement data management, with a strong track record in defining, applying, and enforcing data standards and quality controls
Hands-on experience with SAP S/4HANA and / or SAP Ariba, with a solid understanding of underlying data structures, master data, and commercial object configuration beyond the front-end interface
Strong understanding of how data is modelled, stored, and integrated across interconnected systems
Ability to operate effectively within defined standards set by the data owner and translate them into consistent, reliable execution
Experience managing data collection and metadata within onboarding or questionnaire-driven tools
Demonstrable experience implementing data standards, governance frameworks, and quality controls
Exposure to spend reporting and analytics, including planning and operational support
Familiarity with procurement master data in financial services or other regulated environments
Familiarity with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, PowerBI etc.
Driven and proactive, demonstrating initiative and ownership
Hands-on and adaptable, comfortable operating in a fast-paced transformation environment
Curious and quick to learn new tools, systems, and ways of working
Collaborative, with a strong focus on contributing to team impact and outcomes
Relevant SAP or data management certifications (e.g. SAP S/4HANA, SAP MDG, DAMA/CDMP, or similar) are advantageous
Fluency in English is required, German would be preferred


This is a back-end data role and is not suited to candidates whose SAP experience is limited to front-end usage.

We are looking forward to receiving your full job application through our online application tool. Further interesting job opportunities can be found on our Career site.

Is this not quite what you are looking for? Set up a job alert by creating a candidate account here.

Title: Consultant Data / AI X/ F/H
Description:
Dans le cadre de nos projets Data et IA, nous recrutons notre futur Consultant Data/IA pour intervenir sur nos projets de valorisation de la donnée.

Vos missions :

Collecter, structurer et analyser les données
Mettre en place des pipelines data
Développer des modèles analytiques ou prédictifs
Concevoir des dashboards et outils d’aide à la décision
Participer à des projets IA / Machine Learning




​
Compétences en data (SQL, Python, BI, ML) de minimum 5 ans
Expérience sur projets data / analytics / IA
Sens business et capacité à vulgariser
Curiosité et veille technologique




​ HOUSE OF ABY : un terrain de jeu unique pour nos talents !

Le Groupe HOUSE OF ABY rassemble 10 marques complémentaires au sein d’un même écosystème, couvrant des secteurs variés tels que l’aéronautique, le digital, le nucléaire, le pharmaceutique, le ferroviaire et l’infrastructure... Cette diversité fait de HOUSE OF ABY un véritable terrain de jeu professionnel, offrant à nos consultants et Business Partners l’opportunité d’évoluer dans des environnements, métiers et projets aux enjeux multiples.

Grâce à cette organisation multi-marques, chacun peut construire un parcours sur mesure, développer ses expertises, explorer de nouveaux domaines et intervenir sur des projets à forte valeur ajoutée.

Rejoindre HOUSE OF ABY, c’est intégrer un Groupe en mouvement, qui fait confiance à ses équipes et leur offre les moyens de performer, grandir et réussir.

✅ ABYLSEN est une société de conseil en ingénierie qui accompagne ses clients dans la réalisation de leurs projets technologiques grâce à des consultants experts. Leur mission : apporter expertise, innovation et capacité d’exécution.
Depuis plus de 20 ans, Abylsen s’impose comme un acteur majeur du conseil en ingénierie en Europe. Abylsen accompagne plus de 660 clients dans leurs projets industriels, digitaux et opérationnels.

Nos domaines d’expertise :
R&D
Production & opérations
Digital & IT
Industrialisation
Validation & tests
Maintenance & sûreté
Nos secteurs d’activité : ⚡ Énergie ✈️ Aéronautique, Spatial & Défense 🚗 Automobile 🧬 Life Sciences 🏗 BTP & Infrastructures ⚙️ Industrie lourde 📡 IT & Télécommunications 💼 Banque, Finance & Assurance

Égalité des chances
Aucune barrière ne doit freiner le talent ! Engagé pour l’égalité des chances, notre Groupe s’inscrit dans une démarche inclusive : chaque poste est ouvert à toutes et tous, et nous soutenons activement l’intégration des personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Data Analyst for Beam Instrumentation (SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP)
Description:
Job Description

As an experienced Data Analyst with a solid background in physics, you will join the team responsible for the LHC Longitudinal Density Monitor (commonly known as the BSRL: Beam Synchrotron Radiation Longitudinal monitor) within the Beam Instrumentation Group.

In this role, you will lead the work on the online and offline data processing pipelines, which are fully implemented in Python. The analysis results from this instrument are mission-critical, directly impacting the calibration of the LHC experiments, facilitating the troubleshooting of operational issues, and enabling detailed beam physics analysis.

Additionally, you will play an important role in R&D and optimisation, conducting advanced studies to shape the upcoming upgrades of the BSRL instrument.

Your Responsibilities


Lead the redesign and optimisation of the current online and offline Python data analysis software for the BSRL instrument to ensure scalability, performance and reproducibility.
Develop specialised offline analysis routines using BSRL data, cross-correlating with other beam instrumentation where necessary, to meet the requirements of stakeholders.
Extend the online and offline diagnostics tools, architecting automated notification systems to detect early signs of instrument degradation, anomalies, or malfunctions.
Design and implement the specific software tools, calibration routines, and data pipelines required for the rapid commissioning of the renovated system after the Long Shutdown 3 (LS3).
Contribute to the validation and performance verification of the associated upgraded hardware planned for deployment during LS3.
Evaluate and benchmark different upgrade scenarios for LS3 by combining advanced data analysis of historical run data with physics-based simulation techniques.


Your profile


Proven background in the advanced analysis of complex experimental data, preferably from physics or large-scale scientific installations.
Extensive experience in software development with a strong emphasis on clean, maintainable code.
Basic experience in Monte Carlo-based physics simulation techniques.
Basic experience with laboratory work.


Skills


In-depth knowledge of advanced statistical methods, data modelling, and data analysis techniques applied to scientific datasets.
Proven proficiency in Python, including familiarity with the standard scientific stack (such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, etc) for high-performance processing.
Knowledge of version control systems (Git/Gilab) and continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD)
Basic knowledge of photon counting techniques would be advantage.
Spoken and written English, with a commitment to learn French.


Eligibility criteria:


You are a national of a CERN Member or Associate Member State.
You have a professional background in Applied Physics, Data Analytics (or a related field) and have either:
a Master's degree with 2 to 6 years of post-graduation professional experience;
or a PhD with no more than 3 years of post-graduation professional experience.
You have never had a CERN fellow or graduate contract before.


Additional Information

Job closing date: 24.08.2026 at 23:59 CEST.

Contract duration: 24 months, with a possible extension up to 36 months maximum.

Working hours: 40 hours per week

Job flexibility: Hybrid

Target start date: 01-December-2026

This position involves:


Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.


Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits


A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
On-the-job and formal training including language classes.


Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

  • Proven background in the advanced analysis of complex experimental data, preferably from physics or large-scale scientific installations.
  • Extensive experience in software development with a strong emphasis on clean, maintainable code.
  • Basic experience in Monte Carlo-based physics simulation techniques.
  • Basic experience with laboratory work.

Skills

  • In-depth knowledge of advanced statistical methods, data modelling, and data analysis techniques applied to scientific datasets.
  • Proven proficiency in Python, including familiarity with the standard scientific stack (such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, etc) for high-performance processing.
  • Knowledge of version control systems (Git/Gilab) and continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD)
  • Basic knowledge of photon counting techniques would be advantage.
  • Spoken and written English, with a commitment to learn French.

Eligibility criteria:

  • You are a national of a CERN Member or Associate Member State.
  • You have a professional background in Applied Physics, Data Analytics (or a related field) and have either:
    • a Master's degree with 2 to 6 years of post-graduation professional experience;
    • or a PhD with no more than 3 years of post-graduation professional experience.
  • You have never had a CERN fellow or graduate contract before.

Additional Information

Job closing date: 24.08.2026 at 23:59 CEST.

Contract duration: 24 months, with a possible extension up to 36 months maximum.

Working hours: 40 hours per week

Job flexibility: Hybrid

Target start date: 01-December-2026

This position involves:

  • Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.

Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits

  • A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
  • 30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
  • Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
  • Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
  • A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
  • Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
  • On-the-job and formal training including language classes.

Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Rejoindre Assura, c’est évoluer dans un environnement collaboratif et dynamique où chacun joue un rôle essentiel et contribue à une mission qui fait sens. Chaque jour, nous mettons notre énergie et notre expertise au service de nos clients, pour les accompagner dans leurs décisions de santé.




Envie de nous rejoindre? Alors, nice to meet you!




Au sein de notre département Informatique, tu rejoins une équipe engagée et experte, qui œuvre chaque jour pour garantir des environnements fiables et performants. Dans ce rôle clé, tu contribues activement à la stabilité, à l’évolution et à la modernisation de notre infrastructure IT.




Ce que tu fais au quotidien




Tu conçois et maintiens des modèles de données structurés adaptés aux besoins analytiques, notamment en mettant en œuvre des modèles en étoile et des datasets optimisés pour la restitution. Tu définis les règles de calcul des indicateurs, et veilles à l’harmonisation des structures de données utilisées pour les analyses à travers l’ensemble du système décisionnel.




Tu mets en place les transformations de données, en garantissant la qualité, la lisibilité et la cohérence des données exposées. Tu analyses et optimises les performances des modèles et des solutions analytiques, notamment dans les outils de restitution tels que Power BI et Microsoft Fabric.




Tu participes activement aux projets analytiques en apportant ton expertise en modélisation, structuration des données et performance des solutions. Tu définis et documentes les bonnes pratiques et mets en place des contrôles de qualité sur les données et indicateurs critiques. Enfin, tu animes des sessions d’analyse et de restitution avec des parties prenantes de différents niveaux, jusqu’aux dirigeants, afin de soutenir la prise de décision stratégique.




Ce que tu apportes à l’équipe




Tu disposes d’une formation supérieure en informatique, data ou dans un domaine équivalent, et bénéficies d’une expérience significative dans la conception de solutions data et analytiques. Tu as évolué dans des environnements BI et Data Warehouse, ce qui te permet de comprendre les enjeux liés à la valorisation et à la gouvernance des données.




Tu maîtrises les principes de modélisation de données, ainsi que les outils de data visualisation tels que Power BI ou équivalent. Tu possèdes une bonne connaissance des langages de transformation et d’analyse comme SQL et DAX, et évolues avec aisance dans des architectures data modernes incluant Data Warehouse, pipelines de transformation et environnements analytiques tels que Microsoft Fabric.




Tu es capable d’optimiser les performances des modèles et des requêtes afin de garantir efficacité et scalabilité des solutions. Rigoureux et orienté résultats, tu sais allier expertise technique et compréhension des enjeux métier, ce qui te permet de concevoir des solutions fiables, performantes et adaptées aux besoins des utilisateurs.




Nos avantages




Semaine de travail de 40 heures (à100%)
Minimum 5 semaines de vacances
Possibilité de télétravail
Participation mensuelle aux frais de déplacement si abonnement de transports publics
Prestations sociales attractives et importante réduction sur l'assurance-maladie
Welcome Day et formation interne métier




Date d’entrée : de suite ou à convenir

Taux d’activité: 100%

Lieu de travail : Lausanne

Type de contrat : de durée indéterminée

Title: Senior Data Analyst & Data Scientist
Description:
Rund 3000 Mitarbeitende von RUAG und RUAG Real Estate leisten jeden Tag einen wesentlichen Beitrag zur Sicherheit der Schweiz. Sie sorgen dafür, dass die Schweizer Armee sowie andere Einsatz- und Sicherheitsorganisationen ihre Aufgaben jederzeit umfassend wahrnehmen können.
 

Senior Data Analyst & Data Scientist
 

Das kannst du bewegen

Entwicklung, Pflege und Weiterentwicklung von Analysen und Reports in Qlik Sense
Mitgestaltung und Aufbau neuer Analytics-Services (z.B. Planungsservice, Predictive Analytics) sowie deren Bereitstellung für das Business zur gemeinsamen Entwicklung konkreter Use Cases
Betrieb, Optimierung und Ausbau des eingesetzten on premise Analytics Toolstack
Einbringen und Etablieren von Best Practices rund um Business Intelligence, datengetriebene Planung und Künstliche Intelligenz im Team
Beratung und Unterstützung der Businessabteilungen bei der Anforderungsspezifikation sowie bei Anliegen rund Analytics
Förderung der Data Literacy im Unternehmen

 

Das bringst du mit

Studium in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Data Science oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
Vertiefte, mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Entwicklung von Analysen und Reports mit Qlik Sense, idealerweise in einem on premise Umfeld
Nachgewiesene konzeptionelle und praktische Erfahrung im Bereich Machine Learning und Data Science sowie fundierte Kenntnisse in Python
Solides Verständnis von ETL-Prozessen, Datenmodellierung und relationalen Datenbanktechnologien – MSSQL- und Talend-Kenntnisse sind ein Plus, jedoch kein Schwerpunkt dieser Rolle
Kenntnisse im Umgang mit SAP S/4HANA
Erfahrung in der Arbeit innerhalb von Matrixorganisationen sowie Fähigkeit, komplexe analytische Sachverhalte verständlich gegenüber nicht-technischen Stakeholdern zu kommunizieren
Strukturierte, qualitäts- und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise mit Fokus auf Delivery sowie ausgeprägte Eigeninitiative
Sichere Kommunikation auf Deutsch in Wort und Schrift, gute Englischkenntnisse sind ein Plus

Erfüllst du nicht alle Voraussetzungen hundertprozentig? Bewirb dich trotzdem. Wir sind stets auf der Suche nach talentierten und motivierten Personen, die unser Team bereichern möchten. Wir legen grossen Wert auf Vielfalt und Chancengleichheit und unser Ziel ist es, ein vielfältiges Team aufzubauen, in dem alle ihre persönlichen Stärken voll entfalten können. Wir ermutigen insbesondere alle Personen, unabhängig von ihrem Geschlecht, die möglicherweise Bedenken haben, eine Stelle in einem armeenahen Umfeld anzunehmen, sich zu bewerben.
 

Deine Vorteile

Lohn und Nebenleistungen

Wir bieten dir ein leistungsorientiertes- und marktgerechtes Salär, 13 Monatslöhne sowie grosszügige Prämien- und Zulagen. Zusammen mit weiteren Nebenleistungen ergibt sich daraus ein attraktives Gesamtpaket.

Flexibles Arbeiten

Wir legen grossen Wert darauf, dass unsere Mitarbeitenden ein gesundes Gleichgewicht zwischen Beruf und Privatleben finden. Darum bieten wir unter anderem flexible Arbeitszeiten und Homeoffice-Optionen an.

Vergünstigungen

Bei RUAG profitierst du von Vergünstigungen in den Bereichen Mobilität und Verpflegung. Zudem profitierst du auf unserem Benefit-Portal von attraktiven Angeboten und hast Flottenrabatte bei verschiedenen renommierten Automarken.

Aufstiegs- und Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten

Das Potenzial unserer Mitarbeitenden zu fördern und zu entfalten ist uns wichtig. Durch Schulungen, Kurse und Weiterbildungen unterstützen wir sie dabei, ihre Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich auszubauen um ihre beruflichen Ziele zu erreichen.

Freizeitangebote

Unsere Mitarbeitenden liegen uns am Herzen. Deshalb schenken wir ihnen zum Geburtstag einen freien Tag, bieten ein vielfältiges Sportangebot und die Möglichkeit, ergänzend zum jährlichen Ferienguthaben zusätzliche freie Tage gegen Lohnabzug zu beziehen.
 

Deine Ansprechperson

Stefanie Pascarella
Talent Acquisition Specialist
+41 58 461 75 62

Title: Finance Analytics Engineer
Description:
CONTEXT

We are looking for a Finance Analytics Engineer to translate business needs into data requirements, validating outputs and enabling our FP&A organization to work effectively in the new data environment.

This is not a pure engineering role. The ideal candidate combines analytical engineering capability, comfortable with SQL, BigQuery and Looker, with strong Finance domain fluency and the communication skills to bridge the technical and business worlds. You will be embedded in a small, high-impact team and will work closely with the Group Data Office, Finance Process & Data, and FP&A leadership.

HOW WILL YOU MAKE AN IMPACT?


Own the FP&A requirements for the GCP/Looker migration, defining what data FP&A needs, in what form, and to what quality standard
Act as the primary liaison between Group Business Analytics and the Finance Data domain lead, ensuring business priorities are represented and delivered
Build, validate and maintain Looker dashboards and data models that serve the reporting and insight needs of the team
Drive adoption of the new data platform within the Central Group Finance organization, providing training, documentation and ongoing support to non- technical stakeholders - Identify and close data gaps: working with upstream teams to ensure the right data assets exist, are accurate and are accessible
Enable the Business Analytics team to operate effectively in the new stack, upskilling colleagues and embedding good data practices
Contribute to the design and continuous improvement of the team's reporting and insight infrastructure


HOW WILL YOU EXPERIENCE SUCCESS WITH US?

Education, Experience And Core Competencies


Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Engineering or a related field
5+ years of experience in an analytics, data or BI role, ideally within a Finance context
Hands-on proficiency in SQL and BigQuery; experience with Looker (LookML) is strongly preferred
Proven experience translating business requirements into data models, dashboards or analytical solutions
Solid understanding of Finance processes, reporting cycles and KPIs; you can speak the language of FP&A
Experience working alongside a data migration or platform transformation program
Fluency in English is a must; French is considered a plus


Personal And Methodological Skills


A bridge-builder mindset: able to translate between technical and business stakeholders with ease
Proactive and ownership-oriented, you identify gaps and act on them without being asked
Strong attention to data quality and detail, balanced with the ability to deliver pragmatically under pressure
Comfortable with ambiguity in a transformation context; able to help define the path forward
Structured thinker who can manage multiple workstreams and prioritize effectively


Social Skills


Confident collaborator across technical and non-technical teams, including senior leadership
Clear communicator who can explain complex data concepts in plain business language
Empathetic enabler, you get satisfaction from helping others become more capable and independent

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Le cabinet Sigma Suisse, basé à Plan-les-Ouates, est un cabinet de recrutement spécialisé dans le recrutement de profils informatique et stratégiques accompagnant des acteurs influent en Suisse romande.




Au sein du département IT, nous intervenons sur des besoins relatifs aux secteurs du Trading de matières premières ou financiers, Pharmaceutique, Aéronautique, Spatial, Énergie, Horlogerie et des fondations Suisse.




Nous recrutons un(e) BI / Data Engineer pour un groupe agro-industriel international, poste fixe basé à Fribourg, sur site.




Le contexte, un écosystème BI (Data Warehouse, SSRS, Tableau) au coeur du pilotage d'un siège et de filiales multi-pays. Votre rôle, reprendre et développer l'opérationnel BI, faire évoluer le Data Warehouse et accompagner les projets Data transverses.




Ce que vous ferez :




Concevoir, développer et maintenir les rapports et tableaux de bord du catalogue BI Groupe sous SSRS et Tableau
Garantir la fiabilité, la performance et la disponibilité des rapports en production
Prioriser et traiter les demandes évolutives et correctives remontées par les métiers et les filiales
Assurer le support BI aux utilisateurs du siège et des filiales, gérer les accès et le suivi des tickets
Accompagner la montée en autonomie des Business Owners et des utilisateurs clés
Faire évoluer le Data Warehouse, schémas, tables, vues et data marts, au service des besoins métiers
Développer et maintenir les flux d'alimentation ETL / SSIS depuis les applications sources (ERP, RH, agro)
Veiller à la qualité, la cohérence et l'exploitabilité des données
Contribuer aux chantiers Data transverses, référentiel Agro, reporting industriel, SEI, IBM PA, ArcGIS
Travailler en lien avec les équipes dev / applications, infrastructure et cybersécurité




Le profil :




3 ans minimum en BI / Data Engineering
Solide maîtrise SQL et SQL Server
Expérience Data Warehouse et notions ETL (SSIS)
Connaissance ERP
Français langue maternelle, anglais C1




Un plus selon votre appétence, DBA SQL Server, IBM Planning Analytics (TM1), Architecture Data / MDM ou Power BI.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer (M/F)- Geneva
Description:
Main responsibilities:

As a Business Intelligence Engineer, you will support business units in analysing their reporting and data utilisation needs in order to aid decision-making. You will design, develop and maintain dashboards and Business Intelligence solutions, whilst contributing to the continuous improvement of the company’s data strategy.




You will be part of a dynamic and expert team, where you will be encouraged to put forward innovative ideas, develop new concepts and play an active role in strategic projects.




Key Responsibilities:

Title: EPM OneStream Developer/Business Data Analyst
Description:
Our Company

JOB DESCRIPTION

EFG International is a global private banking group, offering private banking and asset management services. We serve clients in over 40 locations worldwide. EFG International offers a stimulating and dynamic work environment and strives to be an employer of choice.

EFG is committed to providing an equitable and inclusive working environment that is founded on the principle of mutual respect. Joining our team means experiencing a supportive environment, where your contributions are valued and recognised. We strongly believe that the diversity of our teams gives us a competitive advantage by fostering better decision-making and greater innovation.

Our Purpose and Mission

Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future.

We are a private bank, offering personalised solutions on a global scale to private and institutional clients. Our sustainable success is based on our talents and on how we partner with our clients and communities to create lasting value.

Job Description

The EPM OneStream Developer/Business Data Analyst will be part of the Group Performance Measurement, Budgeting & Reporting Team in Lugano and will contribute to develop and run an integrated planning, reporting and performance review process to facilitate value-driven business decision-making. In his role as EPM OneStream Developer, the resource will oversee running, maintenance and development of our existing OneStream planning platform. In his role as EPM OneStream Developer/Business Data Analyst, the resource will support the performance measurement activities by running granular analysis of financial performance root causes and by providing insights on value creation levers.

The resource will play an active role supporting business development and providing value-added decision support using data modelling and forecasting intelligence.

Main Responsibilities


EPM OneStream Developer
Business analyst and power user of MI data
Design and maintain data models for analysis and forecasting
Establish and compile dashboards and presentations to senior management
Take active role in the business performance dialogues
Active collaboration with Strategy & Corporate Development unit during budgeting and forecasting process
Design and develop MI reports and simulations with data from the various MIS systems of the bank to provide insight on value creation levers to the business
Develop strong relationship with business management and business controllers to support the Planning & Controlling business partnership


Skills And Experience


Professional experience in EPM Business Intelligence in Financial Industry
OneStream software: EPM applications maintenance and developments expert
Ability to understand large datasets and experience of data modelling, providing accurate data analysis
Power user of OneStream, MS-PowerBI, MS-Excel and PowerPoint; Knowledge of Excel Macros, VBA, Python, SAS Enterprise Guide to automate data extraction and reporting
Quick learner, accurate and efficient
Proactive attitude and willingness to take on new knowledge and skills
Ability to work under pressure and adapt to unforeseen work demands
Good communication skills to communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels
Fluent in Italian and English


Our Values


Accountability: Taking ownership for tasks and challenges, as well as seeking continuous improvement
Hands-on: Being proactive to rapidly deliver high-quality results
Passionate: Being committed and striving for excellence
Solution-driven: Focusing on client outcomes and treating clients fairly with a risk-aware mindset
Partnership-oriented: Promoting collaboration and teamwork. Working together with an entrepreneurial spirit.

Title: Data Analyst for Beam Instrumentation (SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP)
Description:
Job Details

Job Description

As an experienced Data Analyst with a solid background in physics, you will join the team responsible for the LHC Longitudinal Density Monitor (commonly known as the BSRL: Beam Synchrotron Radiation Longitudinal monitor) within the Beam Instrumentation Group.

In this role, you will lead the work on the online and offline data processing pipelines, which are fully implemented in Python. The analysis results from this instrument are mission-critical, directly impacting the calibration of the LHC experiments, facilitating the troubleshooting of operational issues, and enabling detailed beam physics analysis.

Additionally, you will play an important role in R&D and optimisation, conducting advanced studies to shape the upcoming upgrades of the BSRL instrument.

Your Responsibilities


Lead the redesign and optimisation of the current online and offline Python data analysis software for the BSRL instrument to ensure scalability, performance and reproducibility.
Develop specialised offline analysis routines using BSRL data, cross-correlating with other beam instrumentation where necessary, to meet the requirements of stakeholders.
Extend the online and offline diagnostics tools, architecting automated notification systems to detect early signs of instrument degradation, anomalies, or malfunctions.
Design and implement the specific software tools, calibration routines, and data pipelines required for the rapid commissioning of the renovated system after the Long Shutdown 3 (LS3).
Contribute to the validation and performance verification of the associated upgraded hardware planned for deployment during LS3.
Evaluate and benchmark different upgrade scenarios for LS3 by combining advanced data analysis of historical run data with physics-based simulation techniques.


Your profile


Proven background in the advanced analysis of complex experimental data, preferably from physics or large-scale scientific installations.
Extensive experience in software development with a strong emphasis on clean, maintainable code.
Basic experience in Monte Carlo-based physics simulation techniques.
Basic experience with laboratory work.


Skills


In-depth knowledge of advanced statistical methods, data modelling, and data analysis techniques applied to scientific datasets.
Proven proficiency in Python, including familiarity with the standard scientific stack (such as NumPy, Pandas, SciPy, etc) for high-performance processing.
Knowledge of version control systems (Git/Gilab) and continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD)
Basic knowledge of photon counting techniques would be advantage.
Spoken and written English, with a commitment to learn French.


Eligibility Criteria


You are a national of a CERN Member or Associate Member State.
You have a professional background in Applied Physics, Data Analytics (or a related field) and have either:
a Master's degree with 2 to 6 years of post-graduation professional experience;
or a PhD with no more than 3 years of post-graduation professional experience.
You have never had a CERN fellow or graduate contract before.

Additional Information

Job closing date: 24.08.2026 at 23:59 CEST.

Contract duration: 24 months, with a possible extension up to 36 months maximum.

Working hours: 40 hours per week

Job flexibility: Hybrid

Target start date: 01-December-2026

This Position Involves


Work during nights, Sundays and official holidays, when required by the needs of the Organization.


Job reference: SY-BI-SW-2026-127-GRAP

Field of work: Data Science & Data Analytics

Benchmark job: 200140 - Applied Physicist

Global Benefits


A monthly stipend between 6372-7004 Swiss Francs per month (tax free) depending on your degree.
30 days of paid leave per year plus 2 weeks annual closure.
Coverage by CERN’s comprehensive health insurance scheme (for yourself, your spouse and children), and membership of the CERN Pension Fund.
Family, child and infant monthly allowances depending on your individual circumstances.
A relocation package (installation grant and travel expenses) depending on your individual circumstances.
Possibility to extend your contract up to 36 months.
On-the-job and formal training including language classes.


Overview of CERN - Discover a world where the impossible is made possible!

At CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, we are pushing the frontiers of science and technology. Our groundbreaking work brings together not only physicists but also a diverse range of professionals from engineering, technical, scientific, and administrative fields. Together, we foster an environment where innovation and collaboration thrive.

Every day, we face exciting new challenges and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research that shapes our understanding of the universe. We meet these challenges through the diverse perspectives within our teams, ensuring every contribution is valued and driving our shared sense of inclusion and purpose. Diversity is a core value of CERN since its foundation, and it remains central to our mission and continued success.

If you are ready to be part of a dynamic, inclusive community pushing the boundaries of knowledge, CERN is the place where your curiosity and skills can thrive. Be part of our mission to uncover what lies at the heart of the universe! TAKE PART!

More information about us, here: careers.cern

Title: CFA: Data Management Officer - Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM) (P)
Description:
Job Description

Introduction

Established in 1951, IOM is a Related Organization of the United Nations and the leading UN agency in the field of migration. Working closely with governmental, intergovernmental and non-governmental partners, IOM promotes humane and orderly migration for the benefit of all. It saves lives and protects people on the move, drives solutions to displacement, and facilitates pathways for regular migration, while providing services and advice to governments and migrants.

IOM is committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive and supportive workplace where all employees can thrive professionally and feel valued. By creating such an environment, IOM aims to better harness the full potential of migration and strengthen its support to people on the move.

IOM invites candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply and provides reasonable accommodation throughout the recruitment process when required. Learn more about IOM’s workplace culture at IOM workplace culture | International Organization for Migration

Internal and external candidates will be considered for this vacancy.

For the purposes of this vacancy, internal candidates are defined as staff members holding a regular, fixed-term or short-term graded or ungraded contract, including Junior Professional Officers (JPOs), UN Volunteers with at least twelve (12) months of continuous service in IOM, staff on Special Leave Without Pay (SLWOP), and staff members on secondment/loan released by the Organization, unless otherwise specified in their contract. Staff members holding a regular, fixed-term or short-term graded contract will not retain their contract type if appointed to an ungraded position.

Organizational Context and Scope

Under the direct supervision of the Information Management Officer, and the overall supervision of the Senior DTM Coordination Officer, the Data Management Officer – Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM) will be responsible in supporting the global data management activities and workflows, including the consolidation, cleaning, refinement, and quality assurance of datasets, databases, and the DTM’s Central Data Warehouse (CDW) as well as the roll out and implementation of Data Dictionary to ensure the workflows of data between country, regional and global offices is accurate, controlled and meet the quality requirements of the DTM programme to continue to feed internal and external information needs.

Responsibilities


Provide technical support to DTM global support team and field DTM implementations in the collection and tabulation of data and other information management activities including on the improvement and expansion of Central Data Warehouse, implementation of DTM Data Dictionary, development of data collection tools, use of Kobo platforms, as well as data processing activities.
Implement global DTM survey, tracking system for DTM operations worldwide, as well as addressing queries on data collected by DTM field operations, based on data consolidated in the Central Data Warehouse and any other databases available.
Responsible for ensuring standards in published DTM data in IOM websites, mainly displacement.iom.int, but may include any other portal as required by the operations and global developments.
Implement data quality control mechanism for DTM operations worldwide as set by the global standard.
Contribute to the implementation and roll out of responsible data mechanisms in DTM implementation worldwide. This includes the aspect of data governance, data protection, data security, and ethics in the collection and use of DTM data.
Support in development and adaptation of standard operating procedures for DTM field data collection and management.
Develop and implement Kobo forms to support DTM operations in the field worldwide.
Prepare tabulations for technical reports, information bulletins and other relevant information products related to displacement-affected populations and migration flows (such as statistical analysis and interpretation of results of multisectoral location assessment, return intention surveys, flow monitoring surveys).
Research, analyze and evaluate new applications related to information systems, to new technologies and processes to manage information in response to disasters and/or complex emergencies and make recommendations for their utilization.
Perform such other duties as may be assigned.


Qualifications

Education


Master’s degree in Computer Science, Demographics or an Information Management or a related field from an accredited academic institution; or,
University degree in the above fields with two years of relevant professional experience.


Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database.

Experience


Experience in global data consolidation from various databases and datasets;
Experience in managing or working with DTM data, datasets, and databases;
Experience in complex data quality control operations/mechanisms;
Experience in the development, refinement, and implementation of population database systems (such as registrations, census or surveys). Knowledge of other BI platforms (such as PowerBI or Tableau) is a plus.


Skills


Excellent knowledge of databases management, querying, quality assurance and control systems;
Required advanced proficiency of Microsoft Excel, Googledocs suite, and SQL, Kobo and PowerBI. Valued proficiency in using two or more of the following tools: XLSforms for ODK and Kobo, Access, Python, Stata, R;
Excellent analytical, writing and communication skills;
Have a solid awareness of existing and new developments in responsible, inclusive and ethical data science practices.


Languages

IOM’s official languages are English, French, and Spanish. All staff members are required to be fluent in one of the three languages.

For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written).

Working knowledge of French, and/or of an official UN Language (Arabic, Chinese, Russian, and Spanish) is an advantage.

Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments.

Required Competencies

IOM’s competency framework can be found at this link . Competencies will be assessed during the selection process.

Values - all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these five values:


Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion.
Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct.
Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day-to-day challenges.
Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance.
Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated.


Core Competencies – behavioural indicators


Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results.
Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes.
Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate.
Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work.
Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way.


Notes

Internationally recruited professional staff are required to be mobile. Please refer to this link for guidance on IOM Job Category. Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation.

Appointment will be subject to all clearances, including medical and security as well as accreditation, any residency or visa requirements. Vacancies close at 23:59 local time Geneva, Switzerland on the respective closing date. No late applications will be accepted.

IOM has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, harassment, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and any forms of discrimination. IOM is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children.

IOM conducts reference checks and background verification, including through system wide databases such as ClearCheck, to help ensure that candidates have no record of misconduct, including on sexual harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse, or fraud. Candidates are expected to uphold these standards and principles at all times.

IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process—whether for application, interview, processing, training, or otherwise—and does not request any information related to bank accounts.

IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the  IOM WAVE system  (for internal candidates link here ). The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website:  IOM Careers and Job Vacancies
