Under the direct supervision of the Information Management Officer, and the overall supervision of the Senior DTM Coordination Officer, the Data Management Officer – Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM) will be responsible in supporting the global data management activities and workflows, including the consolidation, cleaning, refinement, and quality assurance of datasets, databases, and the DTM’s Central Data Warehouse (CDW) as well as the roll out and implementation of Data Dictionary to ensure the workflows of data between country, regional and global offices is accurate, controlled and meet the quality requirements of the DTM programme to continue to feed internal and external information needs.

Responsibilities

  • Provide technical support to DTM global support team and field DTM implementations in the collection and tabulation of data and other information management activities including on the improvement and expansion of Central Data Warehouse, implementation of DTM Data Dictionary, development of data collection tools, use of Kobo platforms, as well as data processing activities.
  • Implement global DTM survey, tracking system for DTM operations worldwide, as well as addressing queries on data collected by DTM field operations, based on data consolidated in the Central Data Warehouse and any other databases available.
  • Responsible for ensuring standards in published DTM data in IOM websites, mainly displacement.iom.int, but may include any other portal as required by the operations and global developments.
  • Implement data quality control mechanism for DTM operations worldwide as set by the global standard.
  • Contribute to the implementation and roll out of responsible data mechanisms in DTM implementation worldwide. This includes the aspect of data governance, data protection, data security, and ethics in the collection and use of DTM data.
  • Support in development and adaptation of standard operating procedures for DTM field data collection and management.
  • Develop and implement Kobo forms to support DTM operations in the field worldwide.
  • Prepare tabulations for technical reports, information bulletins and other relevant information products related to displacement-affected populations and migration flows (such as statistical analysis and interpretation of results of multisectoral location assessment, return intention surveys, flow monitoring surveys).
  • Research, analyze and evaluate new applications related to information systems, to new technologies and processes to manage information in response to disasters and/or complex emergencies and make recommendations for their utilization.
  • Perform such other duties as may be assigned.

Qualifications

Education

  • Master’s degree in Computer Science, Demographics or an Information Management or a related field from an accredited academic institution; or,
  • University degree in the above fields with two years of relevant professional experience.

Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database.

Experience

  • Experience in global data consolidation from various databases and datasets;
  • Experience in managing or working with DTM data, datasets, and databases;
  • Experience in complex data quality control operations/mechanisms;
  • Experience in the development, refinement, and implementation of population database systems (such as registrations, census or surveys). Knowledge of other BI platforms (such as PowerBI or Tableau) is a plus.

Skills

  • Excellent knowledge of databases management, querying, quality assurance and control systems;
  • Required advanced proficiency of Microsoft Excel, Googledocs suite, and SQL, Kobo and PowerBI. Valued proficiency in using two or more of the following tools: XLSforms for ODK and Kobo, Access, Python, Stata, R;
  • Excellent analytical, writing and communication skills;
  • Have a solid awareness of existing and new developments in responsible, inclusive and ethical data science practices.

Languages

IOM’s official languages are English, French, and Spanish. All staff members are required to be fluent in one of the three languages.

For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written).

Working knowledge of French, and/or of an official UN Language (Arabic, Chinese, Russian, and Spanish) is an advantage.

Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments.

Required Competencies

IOM’s competency framework can be found at this link . Competencies will be assessed during the selection process.

Values - all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these five values:

  • Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion.
  • Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct.
  • Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day-to-day challenges.
  • Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance.
  • Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated.

Core Competencies – behavioural indicators

  • Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results.
  • Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes.
  • Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate.
  • Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work.
  • Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way.

Notes

Internationally recruited professional staff are required to be mobile. Please refer to this link for guidance on IOM Job Category. Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation.

Appointment will be subject to all clearances, including medical and security as well as accreditation, any residency or visa requirements. Vacancies close at 23:59 local time Geneva, Switzerland on the respective closing date. No late applications will be accepted.

IOM has a zero-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, harassment, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and any forms of discrimination. IOM is committed to promote the protection and safeguarding of all children.

IOM conducts reference checks and background verification, including through system wide databases such as ClearCheck, to help ensure that candidates have no record of misconduct, including on sexual harassment, sexual exploitation and abuse, or fraud. Candidates are expected to uphold these standards and principles at all times.

IOM does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process—whether for application, interview, processing, training, or otherwise—and does not request any information related to bank accounts.

IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the  IOM WAVE system  (for internal candidates link here ). The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website:  IOM Careers and Job Vacancies

Title: Analytics & Insights Manager
Description:
Join our dynamic corporate team and become part of Aman Group. With offices strategically located around the world (Zug, London, Dubai, Singapore, Bangkok, Miami and New York), our corporate colleagues are at the forefront of driving innovation and excellence for our ultra-luxury brand.

Role:

We are seeking an Analytics & Insights Manager reporting to the Director of Analytics & Insights. Based in our Baar, Switzerland corporate office you will lead data analysis, reporting, and dashboarding across the business. This role will transform complex data into actionable insights, oversee end-to-end data processes, and ensure accuracy across core systems. You will partner closely with cross-functional teams to align on KPIs, support strategic decision-making, and foster a strong data-driven culture.

Responsibilities:


Oversee daily data ingestion, synchronization, and validation processes across internal (CRM, PMS, Finance) and external data sources (vendors, partners, market feeds)
Ensure all data pipelines and manual uploads are executed accurately and on schedule
Coordinate with Data Engineering to troubleshoot data discrepancies, failed loads, or system mismatches
Collaborate with Data Engineering to ensure data readiness, consistency, and governance across CRM, PMS, and ancillary systems
Validate and maintain the integrity of datasets, ensuring alignment with enterprise data standards and with data privacy / compliance / governance requirements
Conduct deep-dive analyses and develop predictive models (e.g., seasonality, demand forecasting, pricing performance, customer engagement)
Partner with global and regional stakeholders to define and maintain standardized KPIs (e.g., occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, GOP, channel mix)
Own the end-to-end development of dashboards and reporting frameworks in PBI for hotel performance across regions
Ensure metrics for business performance are defined, measured, and maintained consistently across teams to support scalability and transparency
Partner with Commercial and Operations teams to identify performance drivers and opportunities across markets, lines of business, and properties Translate recurring requirements of commercial and operational teams into automated, self-service dashboards
Deliver actionable insights on revenue trends, booking behaviour and operational efficiency


Requirements:


Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
8–10 years of experience in customer analytics, data science, or business intelligence, preferably within luxury hospitality, travel, or consumer sectors
Demonstrated expertise in predictive modelling and business analysis
Proficiency in SQL and Power BI required; experience with Python, R, or statistical modeling tools preferred
Experience with DBT, GitHub and Snowflake
Experience integrating data from CRM, PMS, and external sources
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to communicate insights to senior stakeholders
Deep understanding of data governance, privacy compliance, and analytical best practices


Benefits

At Aman Group, we believe our colleagues are at the core of our success. We offer competitive benefits, including exciting global career opportunities within a rapidly expanding company. We prioritize and support your professional journey, ensuring you feel valued, included, and at home.

If you are passionate about attracting exceptional talent and creating a positive experience for every candidate, we invite you to apply to join us on our journey.

Title: Market Risk - Data Engineer
Description:
Main purpose

We are looking for a highly motivated Market Risk - Data Engineer to join Trafigura’s Market Risk team at the intersection of commodity trading and cutting-edge AI. This role goes beyond traditional risk analysis: you will harness agentic AI to build automated workflows, design analytical tools, and deliver rapid, high-quality insights that directly support trading decisions across our Oil & Petroleum Products, Metals, and Power & Gas divisions. If you are passionate about building things — from polished, production-ready processes to fast, creative ad hoc analyses — and you want to do it in one of the world’s most dynamic commodity trading environments, this is the role for you.

Key responsibilities

Build Data & Analytics Solutions

Develop and maintain data pipelines that support daily market risk processes and analytics.

Design and implement tools to automate data ingestion, validation, reporting, and analysis workflows.

Build scalable solutions that improve the quality, accessibility, and timeliness of risk data.

Apply AI & Automation

Help design and deploy AI-enabled workflows that support risk analysis, reporting, and operational efficiency.

Explore and evaluate emerging AI technologies, including LLMs, automation frameworks, and agent-based solutions.

Contribute to a shared library of reusable tools and frameworks that can be leveraged across the Market Risk function.

Support Quantitative Risk Analytics

Contribute to the development and enhancement of market risk models and metrics, including:

Value-at-Risk (VaR)

Stress testing and scenario analysis

Sensitivity analysis and Greeks

P&L explain and attribution

Partner with market risk managers and traders to deliver actionable insights and analytical support.

Ensure Operational Excellence

Monitor and maintain production data pipelines and analytics processes.

Investigate and resolve data quality issues, process failures, and exceptions.

Contribute to best practices in software development, testing, monitoring, and documentation.

Support continuous improvements in system reliability, scalability, and maintainability.

Required qualifications

Minimum 2–4 years’ experience in market risk, quantitative finance, or a related analytics role within commodity trading or financial services.

Strong Python programming skills and experience building production-quality solutions.

Experience working with data pipelines, APIs, data processing frameworks, or workflow automation tools.

Understanding of software engineering fundamentals, including testing, version control, and code quality practices.

Interest in applying AI and automation technologies to solve business problems.

Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.

Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal.

Preferred qualifications

Experience in financial markets, commodities, risk management, or trading environments.

Familiarity with market risk concepts such as VaR, stress testing, sensitivities, or P&L analysis.

Exposure to AI/LLM technologies, including APIs, prompt engineering, agent frameworks, or workflow automation tools.

Experience with cloud platforms, data platforms, or modern analytics environments such as Snowflake or Databricks.

Knowledge of Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, or related engineering practices.

Master's degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, Data Science, or a related quantitative discipline.

Attributes for success

Intellectually curious and self-driven, with a bias for action — you move quickly from idea to working solution without waiting to be asked.

AI-first mindset: genuinely excited by agentic AI and actively seeks opportunities to deploy it across the team’s workflows — not just as a personal productivity tool but as shared infrastructure that multiplies the team’s output.

Attention to detail: maintains rigorous accuracy when working with complex models, data, and code to minimise risk and errors in a production environment.

Communication: clearly conveys technical concepts and issues to both engineers and non-technical stakeholders, enabling fast and effective decision-making.

Resilience and adaptability: remains composed under pressure — whether navigating a production incident, a tight deadline, or a rapidly shifting AI landscape — and adapts quickly without losing rigour.

Department overview

The Market Risk team is a globally integrated function embedded at the heart of Trafigura's commodity trading operations, covering Oil & Petroleum Products, Metals, and Power & Gas. We partner directly with commercial and trading teams to quantify, monitor, and manage risk exposures across the full trade life cycle — from origination through settlement. The team maintains a strong quantitative focus and is actively investing in AI and automation to enhance both the depth and speed of our risk analytics. You would join a collegial, intellectually driven group that values initiative and innovation, with significant visibility into senior leadership and direct impact on the business.

Equal opportunity employer

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and take pride in a diverse workforce! We do not discriminate in recruitment, hiring, training, promotion or other employment practices for reasons of race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, age, marital or veteran status, medical condition or handicap, disability, or any other legally protected status.

Title: Applied AI Specialist - AI & Predictive Decision Science (m/f/d)
Description:
Your Tasks And Responsibilities

The Applied AI Specialist bridges Finance process expertise and artificial intelligence capabilities within the AI & PDS subhome. You ensure AI outputs are contextually correct, trustworthy, and plausible, combining domain knowledge with the ability to understand and work with AI. You validate AI-generated outputs, identify and prototype improvement opportunities using no-code/low-code tools, and serve as the critical interface between Finance domain knowledge and Data/AI Science delivery teams.

This role is designed with two equally valid entry paths: a Finance professional with solid process knowledge and a strong appetite to learn and apply AI and data science, or an AI/data professional seeking to ground their skills in Finance processes and contribute to measurable business outcomes.

KEY TASKS:


Validate AI-generated outputs for business plausibility and contextual correctness;
Perform root-cause analysis when AI outputs are unexpected, implausible, or incorrect;
Ensure local business context, market specificities, and domain knowledge are correctly captured in data inputs and model logic;
Document and escalate recurring output issues to inform model improvement cycles;
Provide structured, actionable feedback to Data/AI Scientists on output quality and contextual issues;
Identify opportunities where AI can improve Finance processes and workflow;
Prototype AI solutions using no-code/low-code tools;
Collaborate with Data/AI Science teams to implement AI-driven process improvements;
Communicate effectively with stakeholders to ensure alignment on AI initiatives;
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of AI technologies and their application to Finance processes.


WHO YOU ARE:


Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field
2–4 years of relevant professional experience
Finance path: solid grounding in Finance processes (forecasting, reporting, controlling, or FP&A) with demonstrated curiosity and motivation to apply AI, data science, or digital tools
AI/Data path: hands-on experience with analytics, data science, or AI/ML tools and genuine motivation to develop Finance domain expertise and drive business outcomes
Ability to interpret AI outputs critically and identify gaps between model results and business reality
Strong communication skills: able to translate between business language and technical concepts
Comfortable working in cross-functional, agile environments with iterative ways of working
Curiosity, growth mindset, and comfort with ambiguity in a dynamic transformation environment
Proficiency in English (working language); additional languages are a plus


This position does not require necessarily relocation, the role can be done remotely from the employees current location. The role may be filled at any Bayer site.

YOUR APPLICATION

Bayer welcomes applications from all individuals, regardless of race, national origin, gender, age, physical characteristics, social origin, disability, union membership, religion, family status, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression or any unlawful criterion under applicable law. We are committed to treating all applicants fairly and avoiding discrimination.

Bayer is committed to providing barrier-free access and support-facilities for individuals with disabilities - during the application process and beyond. Applicants with disabilities are encouraged to request any support / equipment they may need via the contact information below.

Location:

Switzerland : Basel-City : Basel

Division:

Consumer Health

Reference Code:

Title: Business Analyst Data - Industrie
Description:
Nous ouvrons un poste de Business Analyst Data (H/F) afin de renforcer l'équipe de notre client basée à Lausanne, pour le domaine Industriel (Supply, Manufacturing). Ce recrutement s'inscrit dans le cadre d'un programme de transformation du système décisionnel, au sein du Data Office.




Responsabilites du poste

Recueillir, analyser et prioriser les besoins métiers liés au reporting, à l’analyse de données et au pilotage de la performance.
Animer des ateliers avec les parties prenantes afin de définir les indicateurs, les processus métiers et les règles de gestion.
Étudier les processus existants pour identifier des opportunités d’amélioration, d’optimisation et de standardisation des usages de la donnée.
Rédiger les spécifications fonctionnelles et contribuer à la définition des KPI, des dimensions analytiques et des règles métier nécessaires aux solutions décisionnelles.
Collaborer avec les équipes Data (Data Engineers, Analytics Engineers et experts métiers) à la conception des modèles de données et des modèles sémantiques de la plateforme BI.
Accompagner les phases de développement, de tests et de validation en veillant à l’adéquation entre les solutions livrées et les besoins fonctionnels.
Participer aux activités de gouvernance des données, notamment en contribuant à la documentation, aux référentiels et aux standards de qualité.
Favoriser l’adoption des solutions analytiques auprès des utilisateurs et maintenir une documentation fonctionnelle claire et pérenne.




Prérequis du poste

Expérience confirmée de 8 à 10 ans en Business Intelligence, Data Analytics ou analyse fonctionnelle.
Excellente capacité à comprendre les besoins métiers et à les transformer en exigences fonctionnelles structurées.
Bonne maîtrise des principes de modélisation décisionnelle (schéma en étoile, dimensions, faits, KPI, modèles sémantiques).
Au moins 5 ans d’expérience sur des projets Supply Chain, Manufacturing ou équivalents.
Expérience avec des plateformes analytiques modernes telles que Power BI, Databricks, dbt ou des environnements Data Cloud.
Capacité à collaborer avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires dans un contexte de transformation Data.
Une expérience dans le secteur de l’horlogerie constitue un atout.




Rejoindre Sword, c’est…

💼 Formation continue : Un budget formation qui croit tous les mois à utiliser dans un large catalogue de formation

🏝 25 jours de congés payés : Tous nos employés ont droit à 25 jours de congés payés par an

🚑 Une couverture assurance-accident internationale

🎉 Evènements réguliers : Des événements mensuels pour se retrouver en petit ou grand comité

🌍 Environnements internationaux : Des missions dans des groupes d'envergure internationale

🛠 Innovation : Contribuez à des projets impactants et innovants dans de nombreux secteurs d'activité

🎁 Des petits plaisirs : Nos employés bénéficient d'un programme d'avantages et remises très intéressantes

  • Accompagner les phases de développement, de tests et de validation en veillant à l’adéquation entre les solutions livrées et les besoins fonctionnels.
  • Participer aux activités de gouvernance des données, notamment en contribuant à la documentation, aux référentiels et aux standards de qualité.
  • Favoriser l’adoption des solutions analytiques auprès des utilisateurs et maintenir une documentation fonctionnelle claire et pérenne.


Prérequis du poste

  • Expérience confirmée de 8 à 10 ans en Business Intelligence, Data Analytics ou analyse fonctionnelle.
  • Excellente capacité à comprendre les besoins métiers et à les transformer en exigences fonctionnelles structurées.
  • Bonne maîtrise des principes de modélisation décisionnelle (schéma en étoile, dimensions, faits, KPI, modèles sémantiques).
  • Au moins 5 ans d’expérience sur des projets Supply Chain, Manufacturing ou équivalents.
  • Expérience avec des plateformes analytiques modernes telles que Power BI, Databricks, dbt ou des environnements Data Cloud.
  • Capacité à collaborer avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires dans un contexte de transformation Data.
  • Une expérience dans le secteur de l’horlogerie constitue un atout.


Rejoindre Sword, c’est…

💼 Formation continue : Un budget formation qui croit tous les mois à utiliser dans un large catalogue de formation

🏝 25 jours de congés payés : Tous nos employés ont droit à 25 jours de congés payés par an

🚑 Une couverture assurance-accident internationale

🎉 Evènements réguliers : Des événements mensuels pour se retrouver en petit ou grand comité

🌍 Environnements internationaux : Des missions dans des groupes d'envergure internationale

🛠 Innovation : Contribuez à des projets impactants et innovants dans de nombreux secteurs d'activité

🎁 Des petits plaisirs : Nos employés bénéficient d'un programme d'avantages et remises très intéressantes

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
﻿

ABOUT THE ROLE:

At Ascent Health Services, we're continuing to expand our data capabilities and are looking for a Data Engineer to help us build and scale our data platform using Microsoft Fabric.

In this role, you'll work closely with stakeholders across the business to develop data solutions that support reporting, analytics, and decision-making. You'll be responsible for designing and maintaining data pipelines, building data products, and ensuring data is reliable, accessible, and secure.

This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys solving complex data challenges, working with modern data technologies, and turning business needs into scalable data solutions.

 

 

WHAT YOU WILL BE WORKING ON:

Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines using Microsoft Fabric
Integrate, transform, and manage data from multiple sources while ensuring data quality and integrity
Develop and maintain data products, datasets, and data models that support business needs
Build and enhance Power BI reports and dashboards that provide actionable insights
Partner with stakeholders across the organization to gather requirements and translate them into technical solutions
Support data governance, security, and compliance requirements when processing and storing data
Monitor, troubleshoot, and continuously improve existing data solutions and infrastructure
Stay up to date with developments in Microsoft Fabric and related technologies

 

 

YOUR PROFILE:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field or Swiss Federal Certificate EFZ in Information Technology with relevant work experience
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering, Business Intelligence, or a similar field
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric and Power BI
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Experience developing and maintaining ETL/ELT pipelines
Proficiency in Python and data engineering best practices
Experience with data modelling and data warehousing concepts
Ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent communication and collaboration skills
Curious mindset and willingness to continuously learn and improve

 

 

WHY JOIN ASCENT HEALTH SERVICES?

Join Ascent and thrive in a certified top-tier workplace, recognized by “Best Place to Work” and Kununu as one of the top 5% employers in our sector. We're committed to innovative thinking and championing diverse career paths in the pharmaceutical industry.

Embrace flexibility our hybrid work set up and benefit from comprehensive health insurance and 24/7 mental health resources. Enjoy a work-life balance with both Swiss and US public holidays, in addition to 25 days standard paid leave.

We nurture your professional growth with a range of training opportunities and a personal budget for external courses. Your well-being is further supported by an annual wellness stipend and a monthly transportation allowance.

At Ascent, every day is an opportunity to make a difference in a collaborative, respectful, and progress-driven environment. Your journey to excellence and real-world impact starts here.

 

HOW TO APPLY:

Take the first step towards joining our team by clicking the "Apply online" button below. We look forward to reviewing your application!

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Business Analyst (Commercial & Finance)



Our client is seeking a commercially minded Business Analyst to support strategic decision-making across a regional organisation. Working closely with Finance, Sales and Operations, you will turn data into actionable business insights, support financial planning activities and help drive business growth through informed decision-making.

This role is ideal for someone who combines strong analytical capabilities with commercial awareness and enjoys partnering with stakeholders across the business to solve complex challenges.



Responsibilities


Analyse commercial, financial and operational performance across the region.
Develop business cases to support strategic investments and growth initiatives.
Support budgeting, forecasting and financial planning activities.
Conduct market and competitor analysis to identify trends, opportunities and risks.
Partner with Sales teams to understand performance drivers and identify improvement opportunities.
Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and actionable recommendations.
Extract, structure and analyse data from ERP and business intelligence platforms.
Produce management reporting and dashboards using BI tools such as Power BI, Qlik Sense or Tableau.
Support ERP transformation projects, including system upgrades and migrations.
Participate in data integration and EDI-related projects with external partners and suppliers.
Challenge assumptions, improve processes and identify opportunities for efficiency gains.
Present findings and recommendations to senior stakeholders.


Requirements


Experience in Business Analysis, Commercial Analytics, FP&A, Business Intelligence or a similar analytical role.
Strong financial and commercial acumen with the ability to build business cases and support strategic decision-making.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving and communication skills.
Experience working with SAP or a similar ERP system.
Advanced Excel skills.
Experience with Power BI, Qlik Sense or Tableau is preferred.
Exposure to SAP S/4HANA migrations, ERP transformations or EDI integration projects would be advantageous.
Fluent English,German and French are essential; Italian would be a strong plus.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to work effectively across Finance, Sales and Operations.
Quick learner with a proactive, commercially minded and solution-oriented approach.
Comfortable challenging assumptions and providing data-driven recommendations.
Motivated to contribute to long-term business growth and performance.

Please note, only those shortlisted will be contacted and only those with the right to work in Switzerland will be considered.

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant
Description:
Unser Kunde aus dem Versicherungssektor sucht einen Business Intelligence Consultant für eine verantwortungsvolle Inhouse-Position mit vielseitigen Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten. Das Unternehmen bietet die Chance, datengetriebene Entscheidungsprozesse aktiv mitzugestalten und strategische BI-Lösungen zu entwickeln. In dieser Rolle erwartet den Kandidaten ein dynamisches Umfeld, in dem analytisches Know-how und unternehmerisches Denken geschätzt werden.

Aufgaben


Sie sind ein Beratertyp, haben Spass an BI Technologien, Prozessen und Menschen und unterstützen die Kunden aus unterschiedlichen Branchen bei der Anforderungsanalyse
Für Sie ist gut selten gut genug. Sie streben stets nach Verbesserungen, bei sich, beim Team und bei Kunden
Sie kennen den Wert von Geschäftsinformationen und haben eine Leidenschaft, aus Daten Erkenntnisse zu generieren
Sie sind ein motivierter Selbst-Starter, der die Zügel gerne selber in die Hand nimmt und auch gerne unter die Haube schaut
Analyse, beim Design und bei der Umsetzung von innovativen BI Projekten.



Profil


Kenntnisse mit Data-Warehouse Lösungen (Datenarchitekturen, -modellierung, ETL, Reporting) aus Studium oder Beruf
Erfahrungen mit Datenbanktechnologien
Kenntnisse mit ERP, CRM, BI Lösungen (von Vorteil)
Erfahrung mit System- und Server-Administration (optional)
Gute Programmierkenntnisse und Verständnis für KI
Empathie und gute Kommunikationsfähigkeiten
Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse



Wissenswertes


Inhouse-Position mit direkter Anbindung an strategische Entscheidungsträger
Gestaltung moderner BI-Lösungen für unternehmensinterne Fachabteilungen
Weiterentwicklung in einem zukunftsorientierten und stabilen Arbeitsumfeld
Einbindung in interdisziplinäre Projekte mit hoher Sichtbarkeit
Möglichkeit zur aktiven Mitgestaltung der digitalen Unternehmensstrategie

Title: Business Analyst - Master Data (H/F)
Description:
Pour notre division Systèmes d’Information, nous recherchons un(e) Business Analyst – Données de base. Votre mission sera de faire le pont entre les exigences métiers et les solutions techniques pour garantir l'excellence de nos données de référence. Vous serez un acteur clé dans la conception, la gouvernance et l'évolution de nos référentiels de données, dans notre environnement SAP. Le périmètre de données stratégiques sera lié aux Articles/produits & prix, les business partners (clients/fournisseurs) et la production (p.ex. nomenclatures, gammes).

Responsabilités

En tant que référent fonctionnel des données de base, vous interviendrez dans les domaines suivants:

Analyse & Conception


Recueillir, analyser et challenger les besoins des équipes métiers concernant et évaluer leurs impacts sur les processus de bout en bout
Concevoir et modéliser les processus de gouvernance (création, modification, validation) dans SAP MDG : workflows, règles de gestion, rôles et responsabilités
Garantir la qualité des masters data de l’entreprise
Rédiger des spécifications fonctionnelles
Suivre les développements et intégrations (SAP et non-SAP) pour garantir qu'ils répondent parfaitement aux besoins exprimés


Qualité & Déploiement


Organiser, exécuter et documenter les phases de tests fonctionnels et de recette
Participer activement aux projets de migration de données : analyse de l'existant, définition des règles de mapping et de reprise, coordination du "cutover" et support post-démarrage ("hypercare")
Mettre en place et analyser les indicateurs de qualité de données (complétude, unicité, fraîcheur) pour piloter l'amélioration continue


Accompagnement & Veille


Former et accompagner les utilisateurs finaux pour garantir l'adoption des nouveaux processus et outils (rédaction de guides, support fonctionnel)
Assurer une veille technologique et méthodologique sur les meilleures pratiques de Master Data Management et les évolutions des solutions SAP


Profil


Niveau d'études : Ingénieur ou Master (informatique, data ou équivalent)
Minimum 3-5 ans d'espérience sur des sujets SAP, dont une expérience significative sur la gouvernance et la qualité des données de base
Connaissance des objets Article/Produit, Business Partenaire Client, Fournisseur et, idéalement, des données de production (PP)
Connaissances appréciées : SAP MDG, S/4HANA
Compétences : animation d’ateliers, formalisation (processus, règles de gestion, spécifications), coordination multi-acteurs, approche orientée qualité et conformité
Qualités : rigueur, autonomie, sens du service, pragmatisme, excellent relationnel
Langues : français courant ; anglais professionnel


Nous vous offrons un environnement stimulant, d’excellentes conditions de travail, des solutions de flexibilisation du temps de travail et des prestations sociales de premier ordre.

Nous vous remercions d'adresser un dossier de candidature complet (CV, lettre de motivation, certificats et diplômes).

Title: Manager Supply Chain Business Intelligence (Power BI / SQL) (m/f/d)
Description:
Purpose & Overall Relevance For The Organization

Are you enthusiastic about the prospect of driving excellence in reporting & analytics capabilities to increase transparency and visibility across the entire supply chain organization? Do you enjoy using SQL to answer critical business questions and to deliver insights that drive decision-making? If you have strong front-end visualization experience and want to make a meaningful impact on the adidas supply chain, this role offers a great opportunity to do so. The role of Manager SC Business Intelligence is tailor-made for you!

As part of the Supply Chain Intelligence team, we are actively seeking an experienced Manager of Supply Chain Business Intelligence to build and enhance reporting capabilities, ensuring standardization, harmonization, and scalability. Your role will be instrumental in creating visibility to increase the proactive management of our business operations. Join us in shaping the future of supply chain excellence at adidas.

Key Responsibilities


Develop scalable reporting solutions and visualize data using BI tools such as Power BI.
Utilize SQL queries to extract, transform and load data – while securing one version of truth by actively standardizing, harmonizing data.
Translate business requirements into technical specifications for the Data Transformation team (e.g. data points, architecture, and models).
Ensure that relevant metrics are timely developed, validated across markets and documented to bring transparency. Generate detailed and up-to-date documentation in Confluence.
Manage the deployment of the reporting capabilities including optimizing dashboard performance, conducting thorough data validation, and onboarding the Supply Chain reporting user community.
Coordinate and actively contribute to user acceptance testing and change management for newly developed reporting capabilities, ensuring successful rollout and adoption across SCM Markets and key stakeholders.
Run root cause analysis to support business in answering key management questions.


Key Relationships

Stakeholders can relate to the following core functions:


SCM - Global / Market Functions (e.g. Digital Process Management)
SCM - Strategy & Digital Solutions (e.g., Strategy & Transformation, Product owners)
Global DNA, Global TECH, and external developers


Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


BI Expertise: Develop and maintain insightful dashboards and self-service reports using Power BI. Strong skills in DAX, data modeling, and report performance optimization. Use advanced tools to accelerate report performance (e.g. DAX Studio, Tabular Editor).
Data Proficiency: Manage the end-to-end process of gathering, extracting, and transforming data across sources using relevant tools (e.g. SQL, Python). Ensure data quality and harmonization to empower other teams.
Analytical Skills: Strong analytical, diagnostic and problem-solving skills. Able to identify root causes, spot patterns, and translate into actionable recommendations.
Operational Insight: Understands KPIs, operational processes, and the trade-offs needed to balance speed and accuracy.
Communication: Confident presenting to both technical and non-technical audiences to deliver insights and project updates.
Collaborative Style: Ability to work collaboratively across teams to support user acceptance testing, data validation, and user education for new reporting capabilities, engaging stakeholders at all levels.
Growth Mindset: Embraces feedback and setbacks as learning opportunities. Constantly seeks to improve tools, processes, and self.


Requisite Education And Qualifications


Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience in reporting and analytics, including front-end visualization and tools such as SQL and Python.
Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Logistics, Planning or Information Systems from an accredited institution.
Proven experience in business intelligence role with strong understanding of business intelligence concepts, tools (e.g. Power BI and SQL), and methodologies.
Good understanding of supply chain principles and operating practices, logistics terminology, basic finance, and management of cross functional interdependencies.


adidas celebrates diversity, supports inclusiveness and encourages individual expression in our workplace. We do not tolerate the harassment or discrimination toward any of our applicants or employees. We are an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Power Analyst
Description:
MET International AG (METI) was established in 2010 in order to consolidate and develop MET Group’s international natural gas sales and purchase portfolio, as well as to establish a trading arm for commodity lines.

METI is responsible for providing highly competitive supply structures to its gas and power sales affiliates, optimizing its power generation assets, managing the LNG portfolio, and trading around international positions.

We are looking to expand the Analytics team by hiring an experienced Power Analyst to support the growth of our power markets analytics capabilities.

Potential for a trading career path in 1-3 years based on candidate’s motivation and acumen.

Job Description


Maintain and improve the power market fundamental stack model across European markets.
Monitor model inputs and outputs, track model performance, and propose improvements.
Support the power desk with regular short to mid-term fundamental forecasting and analysis.
Follow market developments and analyze correlations between energy complex building blocks - CO2 emissions, coal, gas, and power prices.
Propose profitable Clear Spark Spread (CSS) and Clear Dark Spread (CDS) trading opportunities.
Based on research and statistical analysis, prepare fundamental scenarios.
Continuously improve understanding of fundamental power market dynamics (supply/demand).
Closely cooperate with traders, gas analysts, meteorologist, data engineers and developers.


Qualifications


A minimum of 5 years of experience as a Power Analyst, or strong power markets experience in the energy trading / commodities sector
Power market fundamental stack modelling experience
Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, or Mathematics;
Strong research, analytical, and quantitative skills (econometric and statistical modelling)
Excellent presentation and communication skills
Coding skills in Python and SQL are an advantage.
Ability to explain and communicate complex concepts, translate market analyses into propositions/strategies and to communicate them clearly.


Additional Information


Competitive remuneration
Working in a dynamic international team
High degree of responsibility and independence
Friendly and modern working environment

Title: Data Engineer (w/m/d)
Description:
Neue Wege finden
 

Data Engineer (w/m/d)
 

Das erwartet dich
Bei Pax hast du nicht einfach einen Job. Es erwarten dich Aufgaben, Herausforderungen und Leistungsgebiete. Diese geben dir die Chance zur Entfaltung. Für deinen Einsatz wirst du nicht nur bezahlt, sondern auch respektiert und geschätzt.

Das sind wir
Unsere Vision ist, für unsere Kundinnen und Kunden die beste Vorsorgeversicherung zu werden. Das erreichen wir, indem wir Vorsorge einfach gestalten. Deshalb arbeiten bei uns Persönlichkeiten, die viel bewegen und gemeinsame Ziele erreichen wollen.
 

80-100%

Dein Gestaltungsbereich

Gestaltung und Weiterentwicklung unserer datengetriebenen Organisation unter Einbringung eigener Ideen
Entwicklung skalierbarer Data-Warehouse- und Reporting-Lösungen (mit Data Vault, dbt und Spark)
Integration von Daten aus verschiedenen Quellen und Entwicklung zuverlässiger Datenpipelines
Sicherstellung qualitativ hochwertiger Daten für Reporting und Analytics (mit Power BI)
Optimierung bestehender Geschäftsprozesse in Bezug auf Performance, Stabilität und Datenqualität
Mitwirkung an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Systemlandschaft in Richtung moderner Cloud-Architektur
Einbringen in Themen wie Governance, CI/CD und Engineering Standards
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen sowie Unterstützung bei datenbasierten Entscheidungen

 

Dein Erfahrungsschatz

Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Data Engineering oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle, idealerweise im Cloud-Umfeld
Erfahrung im Aufbau und in der Weiterentwicklung von Data-Warehouse- oder Analytics-Lösungen
Kenntnisse in relevanten Technologien (Databricks, Spark, dbt, Azure Data Factory, SQL oder Python)
Erfahrung im Aufbau und in der Optimierung von Datenpipelines und ELT-Prozessen
Verständnis für Datenqualität, Governance und strukturierte Datenmodelle (z. B. Data Vault von Vorteil)
Erfahrung mit modernen Entwicklungspraktiken (z. B. CI/CD, Git) ist von Vorteil
Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Stakeholdern und am gemeinsamen Finden von Lösungen
Strukturierte, eigenverantwortliche Arbeitsweise sowie Interesse an kontinuierlicher Weiterentwicklung

 

Einfach fragen
Michael Hirt, HR Business Partner, hilft dir gerne weiter.
hr@pax.ch

Title: Marketing Analyst, Data Intelligence, EMEA- 2-year contract
Description:
Ralph Lauren Corporation (NYSE:RL) is a global leader in the design, marketing and distribution of premium lifestyle products in five categories: apparel, accessories, home, fragrances, and hospitality. For more than 50 years, Ralph Lauren's reputation and distinctive image have been consistently developed across an expanding number of products, brands and international markets. The Company's brand names, which include Ralph Lauren, Ralph Lauren Collection, Ralph Lauren Purple Label, Polo Ralph Lauren, Double RL, Lauren Ralph Lauren, Polo Ralph Lauren Children, Chaps, among others, constitute one of the world's most widely recognized families of consumer brands.

At Ralph Lauren, we unite and inspire the communities within our company as well as those in which we serve by amplifying voices and perspectives to create a culture of belonging, ensuring inclusion, and fairness for all. We foster a culture of inclusion through: Talent, Education & Communication, Employee Groups and Celebration.

,

2-years contract

We are looking for a data-driven, curious, and collaborative Analyst to join the Marketing Data Intelligence team. This role will support the development of actionable insights across market intelligence, brand health, consumer research, and customer insights, helping drive better decision-making across Brand Growth and Omnichannel marketing teams

,

Data Analysis & Insights Generation


Analyze performance data across brand, omnichannel, and market dimensions to generate clear, actionable insights
Support the team in identifying trends, risks, and opportunities through data-driven analysis
Contribute to market intelligence by tracking industry, competitor, and consumer trends


Consumer & Market Insights


Support the design, execution, and analysis of market research and consumer insights projects
Consolidate multiple data sources (quantitative and qualitative) into clear narratives
Develop a strong understanding of consumer behavior and regional market dynamics


Reporting & Operational Excellence


Own and maintain recurring reports and dashboards
Ensure accuracy, consistency, and timeliness of data delivery
Support / promote ad hoc analyses to respond to business needs


Omnichannel & Stakeholder Support


Contribute to the integration of data into omnichannel and leadership presentations
Act as a key support for cross-functional teams by providing relevant data and insights
Collaborate closely with Marketing, Business Development, and other stakeholders


Success Factors

To Excel In This Role, You Will Need


A strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
High attention to detail and a structured, organized way of working
Curiosity and a proactive approach to understanding business questions
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment


,

The Ideal Candidate Will Possess


Strong data appetite with advanced analytical capabilities
Experience in consumer insights, market research, or performance analysis, preferably in fashion/luxury retail or fast-moving consumer goods.
Ability to work with large datasets and synthesize complex information clearly
Strong written and oral communication skills, with the ability to present insights in a concise and impactful way, adapted to the audience
Collaborative mindset and ability to work / coordinate projects with multiple stakeholders
Proactive approach with the ability to anticipate business needs, identify emerging questions, and translate them into relevant analytical approaches and insights
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple tasks effectively
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, PowerPoint & Excel) and AI tools. Knowledge of data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) is a plus.
Fluency in English, any other language (especially French) is a bonus

Title: Senior Regulatory Data Analyst
Description:
Join us and celebrate the beauty of human experience. Create for happier, healthier lives, with love for nature. Together, with passion and creativity, we deliver food innovations, craft inspired fragrances and develop beauty and wellbeing solutions. There’s much to learn and many to learn from, with more than 17,000 employees around the world to explore ideas and ambitions with. Every day, your talent, your innovative spirit, and your determination will shape our future, making a positive difference on billions of people. Every essence of you enriches our world. We are Givaudan. Human by nature.

As Senior Regulatory Data Analyst (reporting to the Registration & Compliance Lead, Regulatory Ingredients Compliance Strategy CoE), you will work within our Regulatory Ingredients Compliance Strategy CoE – Registration & compliance, you will provide subject matter expertise with chemical regulatory compliance, chemical management and be responsible for by carrying out compliance monitoring reviews to ensure the business is meeting its regulatory obligations and risks are mitigated.

In this role, you will develop, implement and maintain processes, programs to ensure compliance with existing and emerging chemical control legislations.

Sounds interesting? It could well be your perfect opportunity if, like us, you always aim to exceed customer needs, deliver value and are skilled at fostering strong relationships.

In This Exciting Role You Will


Perform compliance monitoring on all aspects of the CCL (Chemical Control Legislation) to assess compliance with applicable regulations, standards and outcomes.
Collaborate with the RAPS team and business stakeholders to finalise reports for presentation and submission to relevant boards and sub-committees, in line with the requirements of the plan, including impact analysis for decision making.
Provide analyses for large datasets necessary for decisions using actionable insights
Attending sub-committee’s sessions as required to present monitoring findings and recommendations.
Support the review/update/development of the regulatory/compliance policies and procedures to ensure they remain appropriate, relevant and effective.
Participate to the development of the solutions to ensure of the compliance globally, the testing and the training of the teams as needed
Define and deliver metrics, reporting platforms, dashboards for tracking and managing the compliance
Tracking of monitoring actions through to successful resolution
Identify business challenges and initiate process improvement projects
Facilitate the computerization of analytics and data collection processes


You?

Are you someone who wants to grow and shape your own world? Who thrives being in a culture where you can express yourself in a creative team environment? Who wants to collaborate and learn together with teams who are as passionate as you are? Then come join us – and impact your world.

Your Professional Profile Includes


University / Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a scientific field
A minimum of 5 years relevant in a compliance role in chemical or related industry
Proficient knowledge of BI, SAP, or other business Intelligence tools. Must also demonstrate advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint
Familiarity with programming languages (e.g: Python, Javascript, SQL) and AI is a plus
Strong analytical skills
Good working knowledge of CCL regulatory requirements and legal guidelines is an advantage
Business fluency in English AND French is a must
Experience in supporting cross functional groups and teams
Ability to work well in a fast-paced, deadline driven environment


Our Benefits


Healthcare support
Paid Time Off and family leave
Retirement plan
Sport and leisure clubs
Company shop
Staff restaurant
Corporate Mobility Plan


At Givaudan, you contribute to delightful taste and scent experiences that touch people’s lives.

We value the different perspectives that come from diverse cultures, backgrounds, and experiences.

Everyone — regardless of race, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, culture, religion, or any personal circumstances — is warmly welcomed at Givaudan, a place where we all love to be and grow.

Every essence of you enriches our world. Join us in making a difference together.

Title: Analyst, Central Costing
Description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Analyst, Central Costing to join our team based in Stabio, Switzerland.




Let’s Talk About The Role

The Central Costing Analyst plays a key role in supporting brand margin visibility through accurate, timely, and insightful costing analysis. The role is responsible for maintaining the costing database, collecting and validating costing inputs, and ensuring that Standard costs are correctly uploaded and maintained in SAP for the assigned brand(s).




Beyond data maintenance, the analyst supports seasonal cost and margin analysis across different levels of detail — from SKU level to product families and total collection level — helping Brand Management understand key cost drivers, margin evolution, and deviations from brand targets and long-range planning objectives.




The role contributes to identifying margin opportunities by analyzing the impact of product purchase cost changes, duty rates, allocation changes, sourcing assumptions, product mix, and other relevant cost drivers. This includes working closely with Sourcing, Brand Pricing, Planning, Customs, and Finance to ensure costing information is accurate, end-to-end consistent and business-relevant.




The analyst is also expected to support the application of AI-enabled costing models and tools to improve analysis, forecasting, reporting, and scenario evaluation.




How You Will Make a Difference

You will achieve this by:

Support seasonal cost and margin analysis by providing accurate and timely costing data, identifying and explaining key cost drivers — including purchase cost changes, duty impact, allocation changes, and other relevant variances — to support business decisions during seasonal reviews, planning cycles, and ad hoc evaluations.
Build, maintain, and leverage costing tools, BI dashboards, and AI-supported analytical models by brand and season, consolidating relevant product information from line lists and other source files to support accurate and scalable costing analysis.
Collect and validate product purchase cost inputs, Country of Origin, and dual sourcing options where applicable, from the different Sourcing Hubs.
Collect trade agreement information and prepare the relevant database to support duty rate assessment in cooperation with the Customs team.
Support the preparation and maintenance of data required for Standard Cost upload into SAP.
Execute and monitor Standard Cost uploads in SAP for all relevant product categories, ensuring they remain updated across brands, warehouses, sites, and stores.
Work closely with Merchandising to collect the forecast and buy plan assumptions they provide, ensuring product mix inputs are properly reflected in seasonal costing and margin analysis.
Work proactively with cross-functional stakeholders to clarify missing information, improve data quality, and ensure costing assumptions are aligned across functions.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of costing processes, tools, databases, BI dashboards, and AI-supported analytical models, helping increase efficiency, transparency, and reliability across the end-to-end costing flow.
AI-ENABLED FINANCE EXPECTATIONS: Routinely use AI-enabled tools to support analysis and reporting activities, in line with company guidelines and business needs; Apply sound professional judgment to review and challenge AI-generated outputs and leverage technology and AI-enabled tools to support speed, efficiency, and insight generation, while remaining accountable for analysis quality and providing reliable support to business decision-making.

Skills For Success

The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required for the individual to be successful in this position.

Education: University degree in Economics, Engineering, Finance, or a related field.
Experience: Financial background; prior experience in costing, controlling, supply chain finance, or a similar analytical role.
Strong analytical mindset and AI fluency, with the ability to work effectively with large volumes of data and translate outputs into meaningful business insights.
Ability to work across functions and manage relationships with multiple stakeholders.
Good interpersonal and teamwork skills.
Technical skills: Advanced Excel skills and proficiency in Microsoft Office applications; SAP knowledge is a plus.
Strong analytical and organizational skills, with close attention to detail and high Standards of data accuracy.
Proactive approach and ability to follow through on open topics with the relevant stakeholders.
Clear communication skills, with the ability to summarize key findings and explain cost drivers effectively.
Comfortable working in a dynamic and cross-functional environment.
Language skills: Fluent in English




About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com




What’s In It For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do
An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together
Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization
On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives
50% discount on all VF brands
Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks




This post is not eligible for relocation support.




Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As an equal opportunity employer, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.




If you like what you have read and want to join our team then we would like to hear from you!

Title: Data Analyst - BI & Automation
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
Automate reporting processes where possible.
Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
Strong Excel skills.
Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
Experience with financial or business performance data.
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
Microsoft Power Automate experience.
Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.
Independent, structured, and reliable.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics)

📍 Lugano | Hybrid Working Model | Permanent Position

About the Company

Our client is a well-established financial services institution operating in Switzerland and committed to leveraging data-driven insights to enhance customer experience, digital engagement and business performance.

To support the continued development of its analytics capabilities, we are looking for a Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics) to join a dynamic and collaborative team based in Lugano.

This role offers a unique opportunity to combine data analytics, business intelligence, customer insights and predictive modelling within a modern and innovative environment.

Your Mission

As a Data Analyst, you will help transform customer and business data into actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.

Working closely with Marketing, Digital and Business teams, you will contribute to customer segmentation, behavioural analysis, campaign performance measurement and predictive modelling initiatives aimed at optimizing customer engagement and business growth.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse customer behaviour, product usage and customer journeys across multiple channels and touchpoints.
Develop customer segmentation and profiling models using demographic, transactional and behavioural data.
Build and maintain predictive models to support business and marketing initiatives.
Extract, manipulate and analyse data from multiple sources using SQL and Python.
Design and automate recurring reporting processes and analytical workflows.
Develop and maintain Business Intelligence dashboards and performance reports.
Monitor customer portfolio KPIs and identify actionable business insights.
Collaborate with Marketing and Digital teams to support customer acquisition, retention and engagement strategies.
Ensure data quality, consistency and integrity across reporting and analytical processes.
Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for business stakeholders.

Your Profile

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics or a related discipline.
Minimum 2 years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Customer Analytics, CRM Analytics or a similar analytical role.
Strong experience working with large datasets and relational databases.
Advanced SQL knowledge.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Practical experience with:
Pandas
NumPy
Scikit-Learn
Experience in statistical analysis, forecasting, customer segmentation or predictive modelling.
Advanced Excel skills.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capabilities.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Self-driven, proactive and business-oriented mindset.

Nice to Have

Experience within banking, financial services, fintech, payments or credit card environments.
Knowledge of Business Intelligence tools such as Power BI.
Experience with BigQuery, SPSS or similar analytical platforms.
Exposure to machine learning techniques and customer behavioural modelling.
Experience supporting marketing, CRM or customer engagement initiatives.

Languages

Mandatory

Italian – Fluent
English – Fluent
German – Professional proficiency

What’s on Offer

Permanent position within a stable and growing financial services organization.
Hybrid working model.
Exposure to customer analytics, predictive modelling and business intelligence projects.
Collaborative and international working environment.
Modern data and analytics ecosystem.
Long-term career growth opportunities.

If you are passionate about analytics, customer insights and transforming data into business value, we would be delighted to hear from you.

Title: Data Analyst – Cloud Migration & BigQuery
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)
Perform data profiling to identify anomalies, duplicates, and inconsistencies
Define logical data mappings from Oracle to BigQuery for each data domain
Collaborate with data engineers on the definition of transformation rules


Validation & Quality Assurance


Design and implement data reconciliation tests for pre- and post-migration phases
Develop SQL / BigQuery SQL queries to compare source and target datasets
Produce data quality reports covering completeness, accuracy, and consistency metrics
Log and track data anomalies using issue-tracking tools
Strong data modeling skills, including conceptual, logical, and physical design, with hands-on experience in normalization and dimensional modeling (star/snowflake schema).﻿


Migration Support ﻿


Actively participate in sprint planning and stand-ups with technical and business teams
Support the migration of legacy reports into GCP-native tools (Looker, Looker Studio)
Contribute to the definition of data governance standards and cloud best practices
Document data transformation processes for knowledge transfer purposes


Personal Profile & Soft Skills﻿


Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders
Operational autonomy and a proactive approach to problem-solving
Good time management in multi-task and project-driven environments
Team player: comfortable working in cross-functional IT/Business teams
Flexibility and adaptability in contexts of technological change
Language: Italian Native or Bilingual
Language: English (written and spoken, minimum B2 level)


Education


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Specialization courses in data engineering, cloud computing, or analytics are a plus

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

  • Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
  • Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
  • Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.

What We Look For

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
  • Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
  • Experience working with performance marketing data.
  • Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
  • Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
  • Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
  • Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
  • Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
  • Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
  • Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
  • Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
  • High level of ownership and initiative.

Nice to have

  • Hands-on experience with GA4.
  • Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
  • Experience working with PSP data.
  • A/B testing experience.
  • Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
  • BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
  • Experience with dbt.

We Offer

  • Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
  • Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
  • A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
  • Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
  • Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
  • Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
  • Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
  • Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
  • Medical insurance.
  • Learning and development budget.
