Title: Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F)
Description:
We are opening a position for a Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F) to strengthen a team committed to a high-level medical research program, based in Nyon. This role is part of a multidisciplinary environment combining data, science, and health, with a focus on structuring and advanced data exploitation in a demanding and evolving context.



Position Responsibilities

Analyze and exploit structured datasets, including longitudinal and potentially medical or sports data

Participate in structuring, validating, and documenting data (rules, definitions, quality)

Contribute to analytical projects related to research and innovation activities

Identify methodological limitations and propose improvements in data usage

Collaborate with non-technical stakeholders to popularize analytical issues and results

Intervene in the development and improvement of platforms or tools related to data management

Monitor multiple projects in parallel with an organized and rigorous approach

Gradually support activities as an autonomous reference on data issues

Job requirements

Degree in data science, statistics, computer science, or equivalent field

Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in data analysis, data management, or research support

Good understanding of complex data (health, sports, or longitudinal data is an asset)

Developed analytical mindset with the ability to assess data quality and relevance

Proficiency in at least one analytical language or tool (R, Python, SQL, SPSS, or equivalent)

Comfortable with Microsoft office tools, especially Excel

Ability to work in a team while gradually gaining autonomy

Proficiency in written and spoken English (French and/or German is an advantage)

Joining Sword means...
💼 Continuous training: A training budget that increases every month, with access to a wide range of courses

🏝 25 days of paid leave: All our employees are entitled to 25 days of paid vacation per year

🚑 International accident insurance coverage

🎉 Regular events: Monthly gatherings to connect in both small and larger groups

🌍 International environments: Opportunities to work on projects within large international organizations

🛠 Innovation: Contribute to impactful and innovative projects across various industries

🎁 Little perks: Our employees benefit from an attractive perks and discount program

Title: Developer Data Analytics (80 - 100%)
Description:
SIGA
Ruswil
Berufserfahrung
IT
Voll/Teilzeit


Das Data Analytics Team ist Enabler für datengetriebene Entscheidungen bei SIGA. Es sorgt für Transparenz und Datenintegrität und unterstützt das Business mit technischen Lösungen auf der Microsoft Azure Plattform. Durch die Entwicklung, Optimierung und Integration von Datenprodukten entlang der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette schafft das Team die Grundlage für fundierte Geschäftsentscheidungen. In enger Zusammenarbeit mit Bereichen wie Finance, Sales und Compliance entstehen skalierbare und revisionssichere Reports, die SIGA weiterbringen.

Qualifikationen und Eigenschaften


Abgeschlossener Bachelor oder Master in Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, Informatik oder einem vergleichbaren Bereich mit mindestens 3 Jahren Berufserfahrung als Data Analyst, Data Engineer oder Data Scientist. Alternativ technische Berufsausbildung mit mindestens fünf Jahren relevanter Berufserfahrung
Entwicklungserfahrung mit modernen Datenplattformen, (T-)SQL, Data Pipelines und Data‑Lake‑Strukturen sowie fundierte Kenntnisse in ELT‑Prozessen, Datenmodellierung und der Entwicklung von Reports
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (Niveau C1) sowie gute Englischkenntnisse (Niveau B2)
Ausgeprägte analytische und adaptive Fähigkeiten, um Prozesse im Detail zu verstehen und die Anforderungen der Stakeholder gezielt umzusetzen
Selbstständige und verantwortungsbewusste Arbeitsweise mit Fokus auf pragmatischen und kundennahen Lösungen


Über den Job

In dieser verantwortungsvollen Funktion entwickelst und implementierst du Data-Analytics-Lösungen innerhalb der Microsoft Azure Plattform. Weiter wartest und optimierst du bestehende ELT-Pipelines, Data Lakes und Power BI Reports, um eine effiziente und qualitativ hochwertige Datenaufbereitung und -visualisierung sicherzustellen. Die Datenqualität und der Schutz sensibler Informationen stehen dabei stets im Fokus.

Durch regelmässige Reviews mit den Stakeholdern stellst du sicher, dass die umgesetzten Lösungen zielgerichtet sind, den Bedarf der Fachbereiche treffen und echten Mehrwert schaffen. Du entwickelst und optimierst Datenmodelle und Reports, um fundierte, datenbasierte Entscheidungen zu ermöglichen. Zudem gehören der Support für Endnutzeranfragen sowie die Durchführung von Schulungen zu deinem Aufgabenbereich.

Die Position bietet dir die Möglichkeit, neue Technologien zu evaluieren und aktiv an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Systemarchitektur mitzuwirken. Ziel ist es, unsere Data-Analytics-Prozesse kontinuierlich zu verbessern und bereichsübergreifend weiterzuentwickeln.

Das bieten wir dir


Werde Teil unseres hochmotivierten SIGA-Teams und leiste einen wirkungsvollen Beitrag gegen den weltweiten Klimawandel und für den Schutz unserer Umwelt
Eine ausgeglichene Life-Work-Balance. Für uns sind flexible Arbeitszeiten selbstverständlich
Dich erwartet ein breites Aufgabenspektrum mit hoher Eigenverantwortung
Eine einzigartige innovative Firmenkultur mit Fokus auf kontinuierliche Verbesserung, flachen Hierarchien und schnellen Entscheidungswegen
Flexible Feriengestaltung, Ausbau von fünf auf sieben Wochen möglich
Vergünstigtes Mittagessen in der eigenen Sterneküche

Bitte bewirb dich über den "Bewerben-Button". E-Mail Bewerbungen werden nicht berücksichtigt. Bei Fragen melde dich gerne unter hr@siga.swiss .

Title: Energy Data Engineer
Description:
Drive the energy transition by providing sustainable technological solutions through our digital platform, facilitating from planning to contract tendering to implementation of renewable energy systems in real estate and industrial companies. We enable our customers to increase the economic efficiency and CO2 balance of their properties while minimizing technical, commercial, and energy risks.

To strengthen our Product Management team, we are looking for an Energy Data Engineer who will contribute to the analysis of energy data, support the development of AI-based price and energy forecasts, and generate actionable insights through the analysis of operational and financial performance. Join this energy revolution and help us transform energy supply in a sustainable and economically viable way!

Tasks


Analyze energy data, including production, consumption, and storage information to identify optimization opportunities
Conduct AI-based market analysis and contribute to the development of price and energy forecasts
Analyze asset data, project progress and operational, environmental as well as financial performance
Perform trend, variance and gap analysis, translating findings into actionable recommendations
Validate data quality and perform plausibility checks to continuously improve analytical outputs
Develop and maintain a central data and reporting infrastructure in close collaboration with IT teams
Create KPI reports, dashboards, and data visualizations for internal and external stakeholders
Collaborate closely with management, operations, sales, and country organizations to support data-driven decision-making


Requirements


Completed degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Business Informatics, Physics, Industrial Engineering or a related field.
2–4 years of experience as a Data Analyst, ideally within an energy sector
Proven experience in designing and building purpose-driven analytical environments
Experienced in building data pipelines, including data extraction, validation, transformation and loading
Practical experience working with modern cloud data infrastructures such as BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or Databricks
Ideally, experience applying and training AI models and machine learning algorithms
High level of proficiency in SQL and solid experience with Python, R, MATLAB or comparable programming languages


Benefits


An attractive salary package and exciting development opportunities
Further training in renewable energies and project management
A dynamic, innovative environment with short decision-making processes and plenty of creative freedom
The opportunity to play an active role in the energy transition and make a real impact
Show more

Title: Ticketing Data Coordinator
Description:
Job information

Division/Unit: UEFA Events SA / Ticketing

Contract type: Max-term

Location: Nyon (WFH up to one day per week)

Start date: 01.10.2026

End date: 31.07.2029

Main goal

The Ticketing Data Coordinator works with the Ticketing Data Analyst to deliver actionable ticketing data analytics and insights for UEFA club and national team competition finals, including UEFA EURO 2028 and UEFA Women’s EURO 2029.

The role primarily focuses on mobile ticketing data, while also supporting overall ticketing data quality and analysis to optimise sales operations, mobile ticket distribution and the fan experience.

Key Responsibilities


Developing, maintaining and optimising ticketing data sources within Snowflake and reporting dashboards in Tableau, or similar systems
Ensuring ticketing data quality and integrity across multiple platforms (SSO, ticketing sales system, UEFA Mobile Tickets app, etc.)
Analysing ticket sales and mobile distribution trends to identify patterns, risks and opportunities
Identifying and tracking KPIs to measure ticketing performance and operational success delivery
Working with multiple stakeholders, in particular internal ICT colleagues as well as external providers
Supporting operational tasks in relation to the distribution of mobile tickets, including monitoring, performance and incident tracking
Gaining sufficient knowledge on ticketing processes from end to end, including venue configuration, inventory management, ticket sales, mobile ticketing, access control and custom
Supporting and performing other ticketing functions as required

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in computer science, data analytics, engineering or a related field
From 1 to 3 years in data analysis using Tableau or Power BI and working with Snowflake or similar data warehouse platforms


Languages:


Fluent in English


Other requirements:


Data-driven and analytical mindset
Interest in mobile applications on Android and iOS
Experience in fraud prevention is an advantage
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-pressure event environment
Strong organisational skills and excellent attention to detail
Proactive problem solver with a collaborative team-player approach
Comfortable working with a high degree of flexibility, autonomy and responsibility.


Want to stay updated on our latest job opportunities, events, and behind-the-scenes insights? Join us on LinkedIn.

UEFA welcomes applications from all, actively working to ensure that we are an inclusive employer that finds the best candidate for our positions, regardless of aspects such as sex, gender identification, disability, age, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity or nationality.

Title: Business/Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Ekkiden ist eine in 2019 gegründete internationale Beratungsgruppe: sie vereint ein Ökosystem leidenschaftlicher und engagierter Beraterinnen und Berater, die Transformationsprojekte in den Bereichen Organisation, Operations und Technologie durchführen, insbesondere in IT/Digital, Industrie/F&E sowie Nachhaltigkeit, für Großunternehmen und KMU in Frankreich, der Schweiz, Spanien und Deutschland.

Über die Rolle

Als Business Analyst und/oder Data Analyst übernimmst du eine zentrale Rolle an der Schnittstelle zwischen Fachbereichen, Data/BI und IT. Du analysierst Prozesse und Daten, definierst Kennzahlen und Berichte und trägst so massgeblich zu datenbasierten Entscheidungen und zur Optimierung der Geschäftsabläufe bei.

Aufgaben


Aufnahme, Analyse und Strukturierung von fachlichen und datenbezogenen Anforderungen aus den Business Units
Analyse und Dokumentation von Geschäftsprozessen sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenflüsse und Systeme.
Durchführung von Datenanalysen und Auswertungen (z. B. in Excel oder BI-Tools) zur Unterstützung von Management-Entscheidungen.
Definition von KPIs, Reports und Dashboards in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und Data/BI-Teams.
Erstellung von Fachkonzepten, Spezifikationen und User Stories für datengetriebene Lösungen (Reporting, Analytics, Automatisierung).
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineers und BI-Spezialisten bei der Umsetzung der Anforderungen in Datenmodelle und Berichte.
Kontinuierliche Identifikation von Optimierungspotenzialen in Prozessen, Datenqualität und Transparenz.
Strukturierte Dokumentation von fachlichen Anforderungen, Prozessen und relevanten Datenstrukturen.


Must-have


Mehrjährige Erfahrung (5+) als Business Analyst, Data Analyst, Requirements Engineer oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle im datengetriebenen Umfeld
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten und ein ausgeprägtes Zahlenverständnis.
Erfahrung in der Arbeit mit Daten und Reporting (z. B. Excel, SQL-Grundlagen oder gängige BI-Tools z.B. Power BI)
Erfahrung in der Aufnahme und Dokumentation von Anforderungen (z. B. mittels Use Cases, User Stories, Fachkonzepten).
Sicheres Auftreten und starke Kommunikationsfähigkeit gegenüber fachlichen und technischen Stakeholdern.
Strukturierte, selbständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Qualitätsorientierung.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) in Wort und Schrift, gute Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil.


Nice to have


Kenntnisse in Datenmodellierung, DWH- oder ETL-Konzepten.
Erfahrung in agilen Methoden (Scrum, Kanban) und Arbeit mit Tools wie Jira und Confluence.
Kenntnisse in Prozessmodellierung (z. B. BPMN, UML).


Wie sieht der Rekrutierungsprozess bei Ekkiden aus?

Bei Ekkiden sind wir bestrebt, jedem Kandidaten eine positive Erfahrung zu bieten.


Erstgespräch: Unser Rekrutierungsteam wird sich mit Ihnen in Verbindung setzen, um Ihre Motivation und Erwartungen zu besprechen.
Interviews: Je nach der Rolle, für die Sie sich bewerben, führen Sie ein oder zwei Interviews mit unseren Geschäftsleitern.
Kundengespräch: Schließlich haben Sie die Möglichkeit, den Kunden kennenzulernen.


Obwohl dies unser Standardprozess ist, können wir Anpassungen vornehmen, die auf spezifischen Umständen basieren.

  • Erfahrung in der Arbeit mit Daten und Reporting (z. B. Excel, SQL-Grundlagen oder gängige BI-Tools z.B. Power BI)
  • Erfahrung in der Aufnahme und Dokumentation von Anforderungen (z. B. mittels Use Cases, User Stories, Fachkonzepten).
  • Sicheres Auftreten und starke Kommunikationsfähigkeit gegenüber fachlichen und technischen Stakeholdern.
  • Strukturierte, selbständige Arbeitsweise und hohe Qualitätsorientierung.
  • Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1) in Wort und Schrift, gute Englischkenntnisse von Vorteil.

Nice to have

  • Kenntnisse in Datenmodellierung, DWH- oder ETL-Konzepten.
  • Erfahrung in agilen Methoden (Scrum, Kanban) und Arbeit mit Tools wie Jira und Confluence.
  • Kenntnisse in Prozessmodellierung (z. B. BPMN, UML).

Wie sieht der Rekrutierungsprozess bei Ekkiden aus?

Bei Ekkiden sind wir bestrebt, jedem Kandidaten eine positive Erfahrung zu bieten.

  • Erstgespräch: Unser Rekrutierungsteam wird sich mit Ihnen in Verbindung setzen, um Ihre Motivation und Erwartungen zu besprechen.
  • Interviews: Je nach der Rolle, für die Sie sich bewerben, führen Sie ein oder zwei Interviews mit unseren Geschäftsleitern.
  • Kundengespräch: Schließlich haben Sie die Möglichkeit, den Kunden kennenzulernen.

Obwohl dies unser Standardprozess ist, können wir Anpassungen vornehmen, die auf spezifischen Umständen basieren.

Title: Data Analyst - BI & Automation
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
Automate reporting processes where possible.
Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
Strong Excel skills.
Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
Experience with financial or business performance data.
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
Microsoft Power Automate experience.
Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.
Independent, structured, and reliable.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics)

📍 Lugano | Hybrid Working Model | Permanent Position

About the Company

Our client is a well-established financial services institution operating in Switzerland and committed to leveraging data-driven insights to enhance customer experience, digital engagement and business performance.

To support the continued development of its analytics capabilities, we are looking for a Data Analyst (Customer & Marketing Analytics) to join a dynamic and collaborative team based in Lugano.

This role offers a unique opportunity to combine data analytics, business intelligence, customer insights and predictive modelling within a modern and innovative environment.

Your Mission

As a Data Analyst, you will help transform customer and business data into actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.

Working closely with Marketing, Digital and Business teams, you will contribute to customer segmentation, behavioural analysis, campaign performance measurement and predictive modelling initiatives aimed at optimizing customer engagement and business growth.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse customer behaviour, product usage and customer journeys across multiple channels and touchpoints.
Develop customer segmentation and profiling models using demographic, transactional and behavioural data.
Build and maintain predictive models to support business and marketing initiatives.
Extract, manipulate and analyse data from multiple sources using SQL and Python.
Design and automate recurring reporting processes and analytical workflows.
Develop and maintain Business Intelligence dashboards and performance reports.
Monitor customer portfolio KPIs and identify actionable business insights.
Collaborate with Marketing and Digital teams to support customer acquisition, retention and engagement strategies.
Ensure data quality, consistency and integrity across reporting and analytical processes.
Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for business stakeholders.

Your Profile

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Economics or a related discipline.
Minimum 2 years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, Customer Analytics, CRM Analytics or a similar analytical role.
Strong experience working with large datasets and relational databases.
Advanced SQL knowledge.
Strong programming skills in Python.
Practical experience with:
Pandas
NumPy
Scikit-Learn
Experience in statistical analysis, forecasting, customer segmentation or predictive modelling.
Advanced Excel skills.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving capabilities.
Ability to communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Self-driven, proactive and business-oriented mindset.

Nice to Have

Experience within banking, financial services, fintech, payments or credit card environments.
Knowledge of Business Intelligence tools such as Power BI.
Experience with BigQuery, SPSS or similar analytical platforms.
Exposure to machine learning techniques and customer behavioural modelling.
Experience supporting marketing, CRM or customer engagement initiatives.

Languages

Mandatory

Italian – Fluent
English – Fluent
German – Professional proficiency

What’s on Offer

Permanent position within a stable and growing financial services organization.
Hybrid working model.
Exposure to customer analytics, predictive modelling and business intelligence projects.
Collaborative and international working environment.
Modern data and analytics ecosystem.
Long-term career growth opportunities.

If you are passionate about analytics, customer insights and transforming data into business value, we would be delighted to hear from you.

Title: Data Analyst – Cloud Migration & BigQuery
Description:
Descrizione Del Lavoro

Our organization is launching a strategic initiative to migrate its data infrastructure from an on-premise Oracle Database environment to Google Cloud Platform (GCP). The project involves consolidating legacy data warehouses, re-architecting ETL/ELT pipelines, and adopting cloud-native services such as BigQuery, Cloud Storage, and Dataflow.

The selected Data Analyst will be a key contributor across every phase of the migration lifecycle: from as-is data analysis of source systems, through post-migration validation, to supporting business teams in their transition to new cloud-based analytics tools

Key Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Mapping


Inventory and document Oracle source data (schemas, tables, stored procedures, views)
Perform data profiling to identify anomalies, duplicates, and inconsistencies
Define logical data mappings from Oracle to BigQuery for each data domain
Collaborate with data engineers on the definition of transformation rules


Validation & Quality Assurance


Design and implement data reconciliation tests for pre- and post-migration phases
Develop SQL / BigQuery SQL queries to compare source and target datasets
Produce data quality reports covering completeness, accuracy, and consistency metrics
Log and track data anomalies using issue-tracking tools
Strong data modeling skills, including conceptual, logical, and physical design, with hands-on experience in normalization and dimensional modeling (star/snowflake schema).﻿


Migration Support ﻿


Actively participate in sprint planning and stand-ups with technical and business teams
Support the migration of legacy reports into GCP-native tools (Looker, Looker Studio)
Contribute to the definition of data governance standards and cloud best practices
Document data transformation processes for knowledge transfer purposes


Personal Profile & Soft Skills﻿


Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex data findings to non-technical stakeholders
Operational autonomy and a proactive approach to problem-solving
Good time management in multi-task and project-driven environments
Team player: comfortable working in cross-functional IT/Business teams
Flexibility and adaptability in contexts of technological change
Language: Italian Native or Bilingual
Language: English (written and spoken, minimum B2 level)


Education


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field
Specialization courses in data engineering, cloud computing, or analytics are a plus

Title: Associate, Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
VF Corporation is looking for an exceptional Associate, Data Analyst, Business Intelligence to join our BI implementation team based in Stabio, Switzerland.




Let’s talk about the role!

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Associate to join our EMEA Business Intelligence team.




This role is ideal for someone with a foundational understanding of data analytics and business intelligence who is eager to grow their technical and functional skills in a collaborative environment. The successful candidate will support the maintenance and enhancement of Data & Analytics solutions while working closely with senior team members and business stakeholders.




How You Will Make A Difference

Support the development and maintenance of ETL processes using tools such as SAP BODS, SQL Server, and Visual Studio
Hands on SQL, SAP HANA, Databricks, Power BI, Genie AI, IBM Cognos
Help monitor and maintain existing data solutions and pipelines
Participate in the analysis of current BI and analytics platforms to identify improvement opportunities
Contribute to technical and functional documentation for Data initiatives and migration projects
Work with business users and technical teams to understand requirements and support the delivery of BI solutions
Monitor scheduled jobs and escalate issues when necessary
Support implementation and adoption of Data AI solutions
Support cloud migration initiatives




Skills For Success

Good knowledge of SQL and relational databases
Familiarity with data warehousing and ETL concepts
Exposure to SAP HANA, SAP BODS, SQL Server, Databricks, Power BI or similar technologies is considered a plus
Familiarity with Cognitive AI concept and products
Ability to document processes and technical solutions clearly and accurately
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to understand business processes and translate business requirements into scalable technology and data solutions
Good attention to detail and willingness to learn new technologies
Effective communication, teamwork and proactiveness skills
English fluency, both written and spoken, is mandatory




Preferred Qualifications

Academic background in scientific or quantitative disciplines is mandatory
Up to 2 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field, preferably within multinational and international business environments.
Experience through internships, academic projects, or professional roles involving data analysis or reporting
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Agile methodology




About VF

VF is a global apparel company connecting people to the lifestyles they cherish through our family of iconic brands. At the heart of our journey lies our purpose: We power movements of sustainable and active lifestyles for the betterment of people and our planet. This is our purpose. It’s the reason we come to work every day. Our purpose unites us and leads us to pursue our goals, together. This is our calling. Learn more at vfc.com




What’s In It For You

We’re in the business of unleashing human potential, driven by the ideas, energy and commitment of our people. That’s why we offer comprehensive benefits that encourage mental, physical and financial well-being for all VF associates. When it comes to benefits, we’re the total package.

A supportive feedback-based culture where respect and integrity guide us in what we do
An inclusive international environment where people of diverse backgrounds, lifestyles and nationalities love working together
Be part of an iconic lifestyle brand in a multi brand, multi countries organization
On site gym offering health and well-being initiatives
A discount card with 50% on all VF brands
Break out areas offering complimentary hot drinks




Free to Be, Inclusion & Diversity

As a purpose-led, performance driven company, we strive to foster a culture of belonging based on respect, connection, openness and authenticity. We are committed to building and maintaining a workplace that celebrates the diversity of our associates, allowing them to bring their authentic selves to work every day.

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

  • Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
  • Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
  • Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
  • Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
  • Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.

What We Look For

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
  • Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
  • Experience working with performance marketing data.
  • Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
  • Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
  • Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
  • Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
  • Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
  • Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
  • Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
  • Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
  • High level of ownership and initiative.

Nice to have

  • Hands-on experience with GA4.
  • Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
  • Experience working with PSP data.
  • A/B testing experience.
  • Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
  • BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
  • Experience with dbt.

We Offer

  • Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
  • Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
  • A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
  • Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
  • Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
  • Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
  • Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
  • Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
  • Medical insurance.
  • Learning and development budget.

Title: Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F)
Description:
We are opening a position for a Data Analyst / Data Specialist (M/F) to strengthen a team committed to a high-level medical research program, based in Nyon. This role is part of a multidisciplinary environment combining data, science, and health, with a focus on structuring and advanced data exploitation in a demanding and evolving context.



Position Responsibilities

Analyze and exploit structured datasets, including longitudinal and potentially medical or sports data

Participate in structuring, validating, and documenting data (rules, definitions, quality)

Contribute to analytical projects related to research and innovation activities

Identify methodological limitations and propose improvements in data usage

Collaborate with non-technical stakeholders to popularize analytical issues and results

Intervene in the development and improvement of platforms or tools related to data management

Monitor multiple projects in parallel with an organized and rigorous approach

Gradually support activities as an autonomous reference on data issues

Job requirements

Degree in data science, statistics, computer science, or equivalent field

Minimum of 5 years of professional experience in data analysis, data management, or research support

Good understanding of complex data (health, sports, or longitudinal data is an asset)

Developed analytical mindset with the ability to assess data quality and relevance

Proficiency in at least one analytical language or tool (R, Python, SQL, SPSS, or equivalent)

Comfortable with Microsoft office tools, especially Excel

Ability to work in a team while gradually gaining autonomy

Proficiency in written and spoken English (French and/or German is an advantage)

Joining Sword means...
💼 Continuous training: A training budget that increases every month, with access to a wide range of courses

🏝 25 days of paid leave: All our employees are entitled to 25 days of paid vacation per year

🚑 International accident insurance coverage

🎉 Regular events: Monthly gatherings to connect in both small and larger groups

🌍 International environments: Opportunities to work on projects within large international organizations

🛠 Innovation: Contribute to impactful and innovative projects across various industries

🎁 Little perks: Our employees benefit from an attractive perks and discount program

Title: Data Analyst - Stage
Company: Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS
Description:
Descrizione azienda

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS, World’s Best Hospital per il quarto anno consecutivo secondo la classifica Newsweek e unico italiano nella top 40 a livello mondiale.

La sua Mission è quella di affermarsi nelle seguenti aree di maggior rilievo:

Innovazione, Cura, Ricerca e Solidarietà

CHI CERCHIAMO?

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS è alla ricerca di persone con spirito d'iniziativa e capacità innovative, approccio alla multidisciplinarietà e al lavoro di gruppo, con voglia di intraprendere un percorso insieme nell’eccellenza professionale sanitaria in un contesto sfidante ed internazionale.

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS si impegna ad attrarre ed assumere persone con diversi background e abilità, perseguendo la parità di genere in fase di selezione attraverso l’individuazione di una rosa di candidati/candidate tendenzialmente paritetica uomo-donna, valorizzando la meritocrazia, considerando esperienze, capacità e competenze criteri guida nella scelta dei/delle migliori candidati/candidate.

Nel mese di dicembre 2025 la Fondazione ha conseguito la Certificazione per la Parità di Genere, riconoscimento che attesta il concreto impegno della struttura nel promuovere un ambiente di lavoro equo, inclusivo e orientato alla valorizzazione di tutte le professionalità

Posizione

Chi Stiamo Cercando

Data Analyst

Unità Organizzativa Di Riferimento

Direzione Risorse Umane e Organizzazione

Attivita’ Del Ruolo

La risorsa si occuperà di:


Estrazione, pulizia ed elaborazione di complessi set di dati provenienti da fonti diverse
Supporto nella modellazione di KPI e metriche di performance clinico-assistenziali ed operative;
Sviluppo e manutenzione di dashboard interattive e reportistica periodica tramite strumenti di Business Intelligence;
Analisi esplorativa dei dati per l'identificazione di trend, anomalie e opportunità di ottimizzazione dei processi;
Collaborazione con i team multidisciplinari per la traduzione delle esigenze informative in requisiti analitici;
Supporto nella stesura di documentazione tecnica
analisi del costo e delle retribuzioni del personale
analisi di BDG e FCT
calcolo premialità contrattuali


Requisiti

Profilo Della Risorsa


Laurea (Triennale o Magistrale) in Ingegneria Gestionale, Statistica, Data Science, Matematica, Informatica, Economia o discipline affini;
Buona conoscenza del linguaggio SQL
Ottima padronanza del pacchetto Office, in particolare Excel avanzato (tabelle pivot, formule complesse)
Buona conoscenza della lingua inglese (scritta e parlata).


Completano Il Profilo


Spiccate capacità analitiche, di problem solving e attenzione ai dettagli;
Attitudine al lavoro in team e buone doti comunicative e relazionali;
Proattività e passione per il settore Healthcare e la Digital Health;
Sensibilità ed etica nella gestione e nel rispetto della riservatezza dei dati sensibili


Attività e Modalità Di Svolgimento Del Tirocinio

In presenza

Indennità Di Partecipazione Mensile Lorda

800 euro lordi

Durata Del Tirocinio

6 MESI

Tipologia Di Tirocinio

Tirocinio Extracurriculare

Altre informazioni

COSA TROVERAI

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS offre un’esperienza unica dal punto di vista umano e professionale, che consiste nel far parte di team che lavorano per obiettivi comuni sposando i valori dell’Etica e del Rispetto.


Un ambiente stimolante e sfidante che abbraccia il cambiamento in ogni sua forma
La possibilità di lavorare in modalità agile e flessibile all’interno di spazi di co-working nuovi e pensati per la collaborazione
Apprendimento continuo per lo sviluppo del sapere professionale, scientifico e tecnico attraverso percorsi formativi personalizzati
Una realtà che persegue obiettivi sostenibili allo scopo di salvaguardare il nostro pianeta e le nostre Persone
Una struttura che favorisce la Diversity ed Inclusion. Lavorerai in team con Persone di età diverse, culture diverse, storie diverse. Crediamo che la Diversità crei scambio di Idee e lo scambio di Idee crei Valore.
Una realtà che prevede, in fase di selezione ed assunzione del personale, un approccio equo nei confronti di qualsiasi profilo di candidato/a, prevenendo in modo attento qualsiasi comportamento discriminatorio e/o offensivo.
L’opportunità di accesso a percorsi di carriera nel rispetto dei principi di equità e valorizzazione del merito, assicurando la conformità con le leggi in materia di pari opportunità, la neutralità rispetto al genere ed evitando le possibili distorsioni cognitive legate agli stereotipi di genere.


GLI INGREDIENTI CHE ACCOMUNANO LE NOSTRE PERSONE

La Passione per il cosa si fa: è ciò che muove tutti noi a lavorare quotidianamente con competenza e professionalità al fine di Migliorare la Vita di ognuno di Noi.

L’Impegno per l’Innovazione: in quanto centro di Ricerca accogliamo il cambiamento ed il progresso continuo.

La Responsabilità etica e sociale: ci dedichiamo costantemente a realizzare progettualità legate alla sostenibilità sociale, perché riteniamo che ognuno di noi può dare il proprio contributo lavorando con integrità, trasparenza ed onestà in tutte le azioni

SU DI NOI

La Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS si conferma come il luogo in cui competenze scientifiche e tecniche, sensibilità umana, etica e valori cattolici diventano un impegno concreto per cure eccellenti e accessibili all’intera comunità. Il valore che viene quotidianamente posto al centro dell’operare è, fin dalle origini, la cura della persona malata, nel rispetto integrale della sua dignità, dei suoi bisogni, della sua sofferenza e della sua speranza.

LO SAPEVI CHE?

Nel 2018 è stato riconosciuto il carattere scientifico del Policlinico Gemelli per le discipline di “Medicina Personalizzata” e di “Biotecnologie innovative”, diventando un Istituto di Ricovero e Cura a Carattere Scientifico (IRCCS).

Nel maggio 2024, la Fondazione ha conseguito il rinnovo dell’accreditamento triennale come Academic Medical Center da parte della Joint Commission International (JCI), leader mondiale nell’accreditamento in ambito sanitario.

La Fondazione, anche per l’anno 2024, ha ottenuto la conferma (per l’ottavo anno consecutivo) della certificazione Top Employers Italia, prestigioso riconoscimento rilasciato alle Organizzazioni che stabiliscono e mantengono elevati standard nelle pratiche di gestione delle risorse umane, nonché la certificazione “Italy’s Best Employers 2024”.

La Fondazione ha ottenuto il Sigillo Ufficiale “Leader in Diversità e Inclusione 2025”, come struttura che si è distinta in Italia per il proprio operato in termini di diversità e inclusione (D&I) e la Certificazione per la Parità di Genere.

VUOI UNIRTI A NOI?

I PROSSIMI STEPS

Il recruiting della Fondazione Policlinico Universitario Agostino Gemelli IRCCS avviene tramite diversi canali in relazione alla tipologia di profilo ricercato con l’obiettivo di garantire la migliore candidate experience possibile.

Una volta scelta la posizione, basta seguire pochi e semplici passi per inoltrare la propria candidatura. Se in linea con il profilo, si avrà la possibilità di accedere ad un colloquio orale, motivazionale e tecnico.

Le candidature ricevute saranno considerate in base al match con la posizione ricercata, indipendentemente da nazionalità, origine etnica, genere, orientamento sessuale, età, religione etc.

Il processo di selezione è strutturato nel rispetto dei principi di equità, assicurando la conformità con le leggi in materia di pari opportunità e la neutralità rispetto al genere.

Title: Data & AI Analyst
Description:
Lavora al cuore del cambiamento

Perché assistere al cambiamento, quando puoi guidarlo? In Intellera, parte di Accenture, potrai crescere in un ambiente inclusivo e rispettoso della tua unicità e valori, sviluppando le tue abilità con programmi di training continuativi ed esperienziali. Unisciti al motore che alimenta Accenture, crea valore a 360° per i nostri clienti e le comunità in cui lavoriamo grazie al tuo contributo come Reinventor e accompagna le aziende più importanti nel loro percorso di trasformazione digitale.




Il tuo ruolo e le tue prospettive

Entra a far parte del Reinvention Partner Industry & Enterprise in ambito Data&AI nel settore Public Service. Lavorerai con tecnologie innovative su progetti entusiasmanti, collaborando con numerosi enti governativi centrali, locali e comunitari per garantire che le aspettative e le esigenze della comunità siano al centro dei servizi pubblici. Affiancherai anche le aziende sanitarie nel comprendere le attitudini ed esigenze dei pazienti, assistenti e operatori, nonché i sistemi più ampi in cui si inseriscono, al fine di generare impatto sociale e risultati positivi per tutti. Nello specifico, potrai:




Supportare le attività di analisi dati a supporto dei processi decisionali, occupandoti della raccolta, organizzazione e lettura dei dati funzionali a report e analisi operative
Collaborare alla creazione di report e dashboard tramite strumenti di Business Intelligence (es. Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Superset), traducendo i dati in insight chiari e utili per stakeholder di business
Affiancare figure senior in progetti di analisi e trasformazione data‑driven, contribuendo alla comprensione dei risultati e al supporto delle raccomandazioni verso il business
Supportare le attività di data governance, contribuendo alla definizione e applicazione di regole, standard e buone pratiche per un utilizzo corretto, coerente e responsabile dei dati, in linea con processi e policy aziendali
Collaborare nelle iniziative di data management e data quality, supportando il controllo dell’affidabilità del dato, la gestione delle basi informative e l’interrogazione dei dati (preferibile conoscenza di SQL)




La retribuzione prevista in Accenture viene definita sulla base di molteplici elementi di valutazione, tra cui – ad esempio - il ruolo ricoperto, il livello di seniority, il grado di responsabilità, le competenze professionali e l’esperienza maturata. Per la presente posizione, è previsto un livello di inquadramento III nel CCNL Commercio.

La fascia di retribuzione annua lorda prevista è compresa tra 27.904,80 € e 34.600 €.




Le tue caratteristiche

Se vuoi fare della tua passione una carriera e hai le seguenti caratteristiche, ti stiamo aspettando:




Laurea magistrale conclusa in discipline STEM (Informatica, Statistica, Matematica, Ingegneria) o Economiche con indirizzo quantitativo/analitico (es. Business Analytics)
Esperienza fino a 2 anni in ambito data e analytics, inclusi stage, tesi di laurea o progetti universitari
Familiarità con strumenti di Business Intelligence e analisi dati (es. Power BI, Tableau, Qlik), conoscenze di base di SQL e nella gestione del dato (es: pulizia, trasformazione e modellazione); rappresenta un plus la conoscenza introduttiva di Python
Interesse verso contesti di trasformazione digitale e utilizzo dei dati a supporto del business, maturato anche in ambito accademico
Spiccata attitudine analitica, orientamento al problem solving, buone capacità comunicative e relazionali, unite a un’ottima conoscenza della lingua italiana




Scopri di più su di noi

Intellera Consulting è un’azienda di consulenza organizzativa, gestionale e tecnologica d’eccellenza, forte di circa 1.400 professionisti con consolidate conoscenze di molteplici settori industriali nel mercato nazionale e internazionale. Combinando un’esperienza unica e competenze specialistiche in ambito public, fornisce servizi di digital transformation.

Da maggio 2024 Intellera è entrata a far parte del Gruppo Accenture, rafforzandone il posizionamento nel mercato della Pubblica Amministrazione e dell'Healthcare.

Accenture è un’azienda globale di servizi professionali con capacità avanzate in campo digitale, cloud e security. I nostri 791.000 talenti combinano ogni giorno tecnologia e ingegno umano, servendo clienti in oltre 120 paesi.




Policy

Qualora il tuo percorso di selezione andasse a buon fine, avrai la possibilità di aderire all’Accordo di smart working, ove compatibile con l’attività svolta, e lavorare da remoto secondo quanto previsto dalle policy aziendali.

HTML:
Lavora al cuore del cambiamento

Perché assistere al cambiamento, quando puoi guidarlo? In Intellera, parte di Accenture, potrai crescere in un ambiente inclusivo e rispettoso della tua unicità e valori, sviluppando le tue abilità con programmi di training continuativi ed esperienziali. Unisciti al motore che alimenta Accenture, crea valore a 360° per i nostri clienti e le comunità in cui lavoriamo grazie al tuo contributo come Reinventor e accompagna le aziende più importanti nel loro percorso di trasformazione digitale.


Il tuo ruolo e le tue prospettive

Entra a far parte del Reinvention Partner Industry & Enterprise in ambito Data&AI nel settore Public Service. Lavorerai con tecnologie innovative su progetti entusiasmanti, collaborando con numerosi enti governativi centrali, locali e comunitari per garantire che le aspettative e le esigenze della comunità siano al centro dei servizi pubblici. Affiancherai anche le aziende sanitarie nel comprendere le attitudini ed esigenze dei pazienti, assistenti e operatori, nonché i sistemi più ampi in cui si inseriscono, al fine di generare impatto sociale e risultati positivi per tutti. Nello specifico, potrai:


  • Supportare le attività di analisi dati a supporto dei processi decisionali, occupandoti della raccolta, organizzazione e lettura dei dati funzionali a report e analisi operative
  • Collaborare alla creazione di report e dashboard tramite strumenti di Business Intelligence (es. Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Superset), traducendo i dati in insight chiari e utili per stakeholder di business
  • Affiancare figure senior in progetti di analisi e trasformazione data‑driven, contribuendo alla comprensione dei risultati e al supporto delle raccomandazioni verso il business
  • Supportare le attività di data governance, contribuendo alla definizione e applicazione di regole, standard e buone pratiche per un utilizzo corretto, coerente e responsabile dei dati, in linea con processi e policy aziendali
  • Collaborare nelle iniziative di data management e data quality, supportando il controllo dell’affidabilità del dato, la gestione delle basi informative e l’interrogazione dei dati (preferibile conoscenza di SQL)


La retribuzione prevista in Accenture viene definita sulla base di molteplici elementi di valutazione, tra cui – ad esempio - il ruolo ricoperto, il livello di seniority, il grado di responsabilità, le competenze professionali e l’esperienza maturata. Per la presente posizione, è previsto un livello di inquadramento III nel CCNL Commercio.

La fascia di retribuzione annua lorda prevista è compresa tra 27.904,80 € e 34.600 €.


Le tue caratteristiche

Se vuoi fare della tua passione una carriera e hai le seguenti caratteristiche, ti stiamo aspettando:


  • Laurea magistrale conclusa in discipline STEM (Informatica, Statistica, Matematica, Ingegneria) o Economiche con indirizzo quantitativo/analitico (es. Business Analytics)
  • Esperienza fino a 2 anni in ambito data e analytics, inclusi stage, tesi di laurea o progetti universitari
  • Familiarità con strumenti di Business Intelligence e analisi dati (es. Power BI, Tableau, Qlik), conoscenze di base di SQL e nella gestione del dato (es: pulizia, trasformazione e modellazione); rappresenta un plus la conoscenza introduttiva di Python
  • Interesse verso contesti di trasformazione digitale e utilizzo dei dati a supporto del business, maturato anche in ambito accademico
  • Spiccata attitudine analitica, orientamento al problem solving, buone capacità comunicative e relazionali, unite a un’ottima conoscenza della lingua italiana


Scopri di più su di noi

Intellera Consulting è un’azienda di consulenza organizzativa, gestionale e tecnologica d’eccellenza, forte di circa 1.400 professionisti con consolidate conoscenze di molteplici settori industriali nel mercato nazionale e internazionale. Combinando un’esperienza unica e competenze specialistiche in ambito public, fornisce servizi di digital transformation.

Da maggio 2024 Intellera è entrata a far parte del Gruppo Accenture, rafforzandone il posizionamento nel mercato della Pubblica Amministrazione e dell'Healthcare.

Accenture è un’azienda globale di servizi professionali con capacità avanzate in campo digitale, cloud e security. I nostri 791.000 talenti combinano ogni giorno tecnologia e ingegno umano, servendo clienti in oltre 120 paesi.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Per CASSA NAZIONALE DI PREVIDENZA E ASSISTENZA A FAVORE DEI DOTTORI COMMERCIALISTI,  l'Ente di diritto privato che assicura, senza scopo di lucro e in autonomia gestionale, organizzativa e contabile, le funzioni di previdenza e assistenza a favore dei Dottori Commercialisti, iscritti agli albi professionali, e dei loro familiari, stiamo cercando un/una




Data Analyst




La risorsa, inserita all’interno della Direzione Innovazione e Organizzazione sarà coinvolta in attività di:




Estrazione, trasformazione e caricamento (ETL) dei dati da sorgenti multiple utilizzando strumenti e tecniche appropriate.
 Creazione di query SQL complesse per l'estrazione e l'analisi dei dati, procedure e funzioni PL/SQL.
Sviluppo e manutenzione di script Python per l'automazione dei processi e l'elaborazione dei dati.
 Analisi dei dati per identificare tendenze, modelli e insight significativi.
Collaborazione con altri team per sviluppare soluzioni e risolvere problemi relativi ai dati.
Documentazione accurata delle procedure e dei risultati delle analisi.
Partecipazione a riunioni e discussioni per presentare i risultati delle analisi e proporre soluzioni




Requisiti richiesti:




Laurea triennale in Informatica o percorso affine.
Esperienza pregressa in attività di Analisi Dati.
Esperienza comprovata nell'estrazione e nella manipolazione di dati da database.
Conoscenza del linguaggio PL/SQL per interrogare database relazionali.
Competenza nell'utilizzo di Python per l'automazione di processi e l'analisi dei dati.
Capacità di analisi critica e risoluzione dei problemi.
Buone capacità di comunicazione e propensione alla collaborazione e condivisione.
Buona conoscenza scritta e parlata della lingua inglese.




Requisiti Preferenziali:

 

Conoscenza della piattaforma di integrazione dei dati Oracle Data Integrator.
Conoscenza della piattaforma Tableau, per la visualizzazione dei dati e la business intelligence.
Esperienza nell'utilizzo di database NoSQL.
Familiarità con concetti di data mining e machine learning. 




Inquadramento: Contratto di lavoro a tempo determinato per un anno, con inquadramento in “Area B” del CCNL per il personale dipendente Enti Previdenziali Privatizzati del 15 gennaio 2020. Retribuzione annua lorda da €28.601,23 a €32.238,38 - buoni pasto giornalieri €7 Il CCNL e l’Accordo di secondo Livello sono pubblicati sul Sito CDC, sezione trasparenza.




Wyser S.r.l. è autorizzata ad operare dal Ministero del Lavoro e delle Politiche Sociali (Aut. Min. del 15/04/2014 Prot. N: 39/4903) e si occupa di ricerca e selezione di profili manageriali ed executive.

L’offerta si intende rivolta a candidati e candidate, nel rispetto del D.Lgs. n. 198/2006 e ss.mm.ii. e dei Decreti Legislativi n. 215 e n. 216 del 2003 sulle parità di trattamento.

Invitiamo chi si candida a leggere l’informativa privacy ai sensi degli artt. 13 e 14 del Reg. EU 679/2016 al seguente indirizzo https://it.wyser-search.com/privacy-policy/.

Title: Data Analyst Assistant
Description:
We are adding a Data Analyst Assistant to the team to support a large scale engagement with an established Italian infrastructure group. This position focuses on interrogating financial assumptions and identifying discrepancies in complex portfolios to ensure our partner makes decisions based on hard evidence rather than corporate narratives.

About Blue Oak Consulting
Blue Oak Consulting provides independent advisory on pricing, portfolio economics, capital allocation, and acquisition assumptions. We are not a traditional consulting firm focused on broad transformation programs or high level strategy decks. Instead, we interrogate specific commercial decisions where the financial stakes are high and the existing narrative is not sufficient. Our team looks past corporate language and P&L complexity to identify where value is leaking or capital is trapped. We work across sectors like software, manufacturing, and healthcare to test whether the assumptions behind a growth plan or an acquisition actually hold under pressure. We are a small, principal led firm where the people who do the work are the ones accountable for the results. We do not take transaction contingent fees, which allows us to be completely honest about whether the economics of a decision make sense. Our goal is to provide clarity through financial discipline and a healthy amount of skepticism.

The Role
This is a full time, permanent position that is conducted entirely in a remote environment. You will work closely with our principals to dig into the numbers provided by our partners, often starting with the Italian infrastructure project. The goal is to move past P&L complexity and find where value might be leaking. You will be expected to think critically and ask why certain numbers exist high up in a spreadsheet, rather than just accepting them as facts. This role offers an attractive and competitive compensation package for an entry level professional looking to gain deep experience in financial analysis.

What You Will Be Doing
Cleaning and organizing large datasets from various sources to build a clear financial picture.
Testing the logic behind pricing assumptions to see if they survive real world friction.
Helping build financial models that track capital allocation across different infrastructure assets.
Identifying outliers and anomalies in expenditure reports that do not align with the stated narrative.
Updating portfolio economics trackers to reflect new acquisition data.
Collaborating with the team through virtual channels to clarify findings and refine analysis.
Preparing clear summaries of data trends that highlight potential financial risks.
Verifying the accuracy of inputs used in growth forecasts for specific business units.
Assisting in the interrogation of acquisition assumptions for new investment opportunities.



What You Bring
A degree or background in finance, economics, mathematics, or a related field with a heavy emphasis on data.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including the ability to manage complex formulas and large tables.
A skeptical mindset that enjoys finding inconsistencies in spreadsheets and reports.
Comfort working in a fully remote setting where communication is largely written and specific.
Ability to organize messy data into structured formats without losing important context.
Interest in how large businesses actually spend money and allocate internal capital.
Reliability and a willingness to learn how to scrutinize financial statements at a deep level.
Strong attention to detail, especially when reviewing line items that seem out of place.



What We Offer
A fully remote work schedule that provides flexibility and removes the need for a daily commute.
Direct access to firm principals who provide mentorship and clear feedback on your work.
The opportunity to work on high stakes projects, such as our current work with the Italian infrastructure group.
A professional environment focused on logic and evidence rather than corporate politics.
Practical training in financial modeling and capital allocation analysis.
A permanent, stable role within a firm that values individual accountability and results.



At Blue Oak Consulting, we believe that clarity comes from discipline and a healthy amount of skepticism. We stay through the execution phase of our projects because we know that decisions looking sound in theory often face challenges once they meet reality. This role is a starting point for someone who wants to understand the mechanics of how value is created and protected in large organizations. We value the work over the performance, and we look for assistants who are eager to show how the numbers actually add up without relying on corporate jargon.

Title: Intern - Data Analyst
Description:
Mosaic Conseil is currently engaged in a detailed process with a leading Italian logistics firm to audit and rebuild their internal data structures. This partnership focuses on extracting clarity from their invoice ledgers and operational data to support a long term strategic reorganization. In this context, we are looking for an Intern - Data Analyst to join our remote workshop and contribute to this investigative work.

About Mosaic Conseil
Mosaic Conseil functions as a small, specialized workshop where we rebuild business artifacts one at a time. We do not operate on standing retainers, and we do not produce slide decks. Instead, we take existing files, such as financial models, pricing grids, or diligence documents, and we put them back together based on the hard evidence found in the company ledgers. Our process is personal and highly focused. We keep the workshop small to ensure that the individuals doing the analysis are the same ones who communicate directly with the clients. This remote structure allows us to maintain our independence and stay concentrated on the evidence at the floor of the business, without the distractions of office politics or redundant meetings.

What you would do
As an intern, you will work directly on the data artifacts for our client in the Italian logistics sector. Your primary task will be the meticulous cleaning and organization of large datasets from their invoice ledgers and transit logs. You will help rebuild their analytical models by testing assumptions against historical data. Following our internal protocol, every file you work on will carry two names: a builder and a challenger. You will often take the lead in building the data views, which another member of the team will then attempt to pull apart. In other instances, you will act as the challenger, looking for holes in the logic of a model. You will not be making presentations. Instead, you will be drafting clear, written arguments that explain the reasoning behind every data adjustment and rebuilt model.

Helpful background
We are looking for individuals who have a strong foundation in logical thinking and a comfort with numbers. You do not need years of professional experience, but you should have a solid grasp of spreadsheet software and basic data management tools. Experience with Python or SQL is useful, especially for handling the volume of data generated by a large logistics firm. More than specific technical mastery, we value intellectual honesty and the curiosity required to hunt for the truth in a messy ledger. You should be someone who enjoys the process of deconstructing a problem and putting it back together. Clear written communication is essential, as our final output is always a written document rather than a verbal presentation.

How we work
We operate as a fully remote team, which requires a high degree of autonomy and self discipline. Our work day is structured around deep work and quiet analysis. We avoid unnecessary meetings, scheduling only those that are essential for moving a project forward. The builder and challenger model ensures that every assumption is tested before a file is considered finished. This environment is ideal for someone who prefers to focus on the technical details of a task and thrives in a setting where the quality of the written evidence is the only thing that matters. Because we are remote, we rely heavily on clear, asynchronous communication through text and shared files.

What the role offers
This is a paid internship, and the compensation is competitive for the market and the level of responsibility. You will have the opportunity to work on high level analytical problems for a major international client while learning our specific methods for auditing and model building. The remote nature of the position provides flexibility and removes the burden of a daily commute, allowing you to focus entirely on the work at hand. You will receive direct feedback on your logic and your writing from experienced analysts. There is no corporate theater here, just a focus on doing deep, meaningful work alongside a dedicated team that values evidence over appearances.
Working at Mosaic Conseil means participating in a culture that rewards precision and skepticism. We believe that the best way to understand a business is to look at its ledgers with a critical eye. If you value the discipline of a small, focused workshop and want to develop your skills in an environment that prioritizes written logic and remote autonomy, you will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Title: Data Analyst Excel - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Data Analyst Excel

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth data analysis using Microsoft Excel to extract actionable insights and support decision-making across teams.
Collaborate with project stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into meaningful analyses.
Review, clean, and validate datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Develop clear, concise reports and visualizations to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in complex datasets, contributing to strategic initiatives.
Document methodologies, results, and recommendations with meticulous attention to detail.
Contribute expertise to enhance data-driven decision-making processes within the customer's team.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience performing data analysis in a business environment using Microsoft Excel as the primary tool.
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate analytical findings.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across teams to drive business outcomes.
Solid understanding of Excel functions, formulas, and data visualization tools.
Proficiency in identifying data inconsistencies and implementing corrective measures.
Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data insights to inform business strategy.
Background in presenting analytical outcomes to diverse audiences.
Familiarity with additional data analysis tools or business intelligence platforms.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
CHI SIAMO:

iQera Italia S.p.A. è una società italiana leader nel settore del Recupero Crediti, Real Estate, Due Diligence e Outsourcing di processi aziendali.

È attiva nel mercato italiano degli NPL (Non Performing Loans) e UTP (Unlikely to Pay) come branch locale di iQera Group e come Servicer specializzato nella gestione integrata del credito e dei beni immobiliari.

Cerchiamo, per la nostra sede di Roma, una figura da inserire con il ruolo di Junior Data Analyst all’interno della divisione PORTFOLIO & DATA MANAGEMENT.


COSA FARAI:

  • attività di supporto relativo all'organizzazione e valutazione di portafogli;
  • attività di supporto alla creazione di Business Plan dei portafogli NPL;
  • attività di supporto alla creazione, adattamento ed esecuzione di modelli statistici di interpretazione dei dati;
  • attività di supporto al monitoraggio e appraisal del valore dell’AUM ad ausilio delle Business Unit;
  • attività di supporto alla creazione di report e documenti relativo alle analisi da presentare ai clienti.


I TUOI REQUISITI:

  • Laurea specialistica in Ingegneria, Economia con focus quantitativo, Statistica, Matematica, Fisica;
  • Conoscenza di linguaggi di programmazione come R e Python, e delle librerie di data manipulation associate (tidyverse/dplyr, Pandas, NumPy);
  • Almeno 6 mesi di esperienza nel ruolo;
  • Esperienza con la manipolazione dei dati e delle logiche di SQL;
  • Buona conoscenza dei principi dei database relazionali e del data modelling;
  • Ottima propensione all' analisi;
  • Orientamento al team working e al problem solving;
  • Flessibilità e forte motivazione;
  • Ottima conoscenza della lingua inglese (B2 CEFR, da testare in sede di colloquio).


DA NOI TROVERAI:

  • Ambiente di lavoro internazionale;
  • Opportunità di crescita professionale;
  • Formazione continua;
  • Contratto subordinato a tempo pieno finalizzato all'assunzione a tempo indeterminato, CCNL Commercio, Fascia Retributiva (RAL) € 25.000-€ 30.000.

Il posizionamento nella fascia sarà determinato sulla base dell’esperienza coerente con il ruolo, competenze professionali e grado di autonomia.


La ricerca è rivolta a tutti i generi (L.903/77). Gli interessati possono inoltrare il proprio CV dando l'autorizzazione al trattamento dei dati personali, ai sensi D.Lgs.196/03

Title: Analista dati
Description:
La ricerca è finalizzata all'inserimento di una risorsa con funzioni di addetta/o Data Analyst nel Servizio Operation e Security – Area Technological Infrastructure.




Principali compiti e responsabilità (a titolo esemplificativo):

• Estrazione, trasformazione e caricamento (ETL) dei dati da sorgenti multiple utilizzando strumenti e tecniche appropriate.

• Creazione di query SQL complesse per l'estrazione e l'analisi dei dati, procedure e funzioni PL/SQL.

• Sviluppo e manutenzione di script Python per l'automazione dei processi e l'elaborazione dei dati.

• Analisi dei dati per identificare tendenze, modelli e insight significativi.

• Collaborazione con altri team per sviluppare soluzioni e risolvere problemi relativi ai dati.

• Documentazione accurata delle procedure e dei risultati delle analisi.

• Partecipazione a riunioni e discussioni per presentare i risultati delle analisi e proporre soluzioni




Requisiti Essenziali:

• Laurea triennale in Informatica o campo correlato.

• Specializzazione o competenze avanzate in Analisi Dati.

• Esperienza comprovata nell'estrazione e nella manipolazione di dati da database.

• Conoscenza approfondita del linguaggio PL/SQL per interrogare database relazionali.

• Competenza nell'utilizzo di Python per l'automazione di processi e l'analisi dei dati.

• Capacità di analisi critica e risoluzione dei problemi.

• Eccellenti capacità di comunicazione e collaborazione (Buone capacità di lavoro in team)

• Buona conoscenza scritta e parlata della lingua inglese.




Requisiti Preferenziali:

• Conoscenza della piattaforma di integrazione dei dati Oracle Data Integrator.

• Conoscenza della piattaforma Tableau, per la visualizzazione dei dati e la business intelligence.

• Esperienza nell'utilizzo di database NoSQL.

• Familiarità con concetti di data mining e machine learning.




- Capacità e competenze richieste -




Sociali:

• capacità comunicative e relazionali;

• capacità di gestione dello stress;

• capacità di lavorare in team;

• capacità di adeguamento e dinamicità;

• spiccata motivazione;

• empatia;

• problem solving, precisione e attenzione ai dettagli.




Organizzative:

• buone capacità di analisi e approfondimenti;

• buone capacità di pianificazione ed organizzazione delle attività

• buone capacità di lavoro per obiettivi

• predisposizione al metodo di verifica intermedia rispetto al piano di attività per il raggiungimento dell’obiettivo




Inquadramento

Contratto di lavoro a tempo determinato per un anno, con inquadramento in “Area B” del CCNL per il personale dipendente Enti Previdenziali Privatizzati del 15 gennaio 2020.

Retribuzione annua lorda da €28.601,23 a €32.238,38 su tredici mensilità - buoni pasto giornalieri €7.

Il CCNL e l’Accordo di secondo Livello sono pubblicati sul Sito CDC, sezione trasparenza.




Questa offerta è rivolta a candidati di entrambi i sessi (L.903/77 e D.lgs. n. 98/2006, art. 27).
